Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 127 BY SENATOR MILLER 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact Code of Civil Procedure Art. 4566(C) and to enact R.S. 9:1026 and 3 2374, relative to donations; to provide relative to management of affairs of the 4 interdict; to provide relative to donations made by a curator on behalf of an interdict; 5 to provide relative to the net worth of an interdict; to provide relative to donations 6 inter vivos and testamentary and charitable dispositions; to provide with respect to 7 limitations and exceptions on donations and testamentary and charitable dispositions 8 in certain circumstances; to provide with respect to tax exclusions, exemptions, and 9 maximum limits in certain circumstances; to provide relative to forced heirs with 10 disabilities; to provide relative to forced portion in certain circumstances; to provide 11 relative to the net worth of a forced heir with disabilities; to provide for certain 12 terms, conditions, and procedures; and to provide for related matters. 13 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 14 Section 1. Code of Civil Procedure Art. 4566(C) is hereby amended and reenacted 15 to read as follows: 16 Art. 4566. Management of affairs of the interdict 17 * * * 18 C. A curator may accept donations made to the interdict. A curator shall not ACT No. 11 SB NO. 127 ENROLLED 1 make donations of the property of the interdict except as provided by law. A 2 charitable donation inter vivos or testamentary charitable disposition made in 3 compliance with the provisions of R.S. 9:1026 and approved by the court shall 4 not constitute a breach of the fiduciary duty or conflict of interest by the 5 curator even if the curator, undercurator, parent, direct descendant, sibling, or 6 descendant of a sibling of the interdict is affiliated with the charity or a 7 recipient of the donation. A curator without court approval may refuse, 8 renounce, or disclaim a donation, inheritance, or bequest made to an interdict 9 with a direct or beneficial net worth as a trust beneficiary or otherwise of one 10 hundred million dollars or more. 11 * * * 12 Section 2. R.S. 9:1026 and 2374 are hereby enacted to read as follows: 13 §1026. Interdicts; donations; limitations; net worth 14 A. Notwithstanding the provisions of R.S. 9:1022 through 1024 and 15 pursuant to Subsection C of this Section, upon request of the curator and 16 undercurator, the court may authorize a curator, in the name of and on behalf 17 of an interdict with a direct or beneficial net worth of one hundred million 18 dollars or more as a trust beneficiary or otherwise, to make the following 19 donations: 20 (1) Subject to Paragraph (2) of this Subsection and Subsection C of this 21 Section, other than to charitable recipients, an interdict may make, directly or 22 indirectly, donations inter vivos of money and other assets from surplus funds 23 of the interdict utilizing all or a portion of the interdict's annual federal gift tax 24 exclusions pursuant to 26 U.S.C. 2503(b) and the interdict's lifetime credits and 25 exemptions from federal estate tax and the tax on generation skipping transfers, 26 pursuant to 26 U.S.C. 2010 and 2631 et seq., to one or more of the following: an 27 interdict's direct descendant, sibling, descendant of a sibling, stepsibling, 28 descendant of a stepsibling, or step-descendant of a sibling, or to one or more 29 trusts for the benefit of the recipient or one or more class trusts for the benefit 30 of such present and future recipients, including to grantor trusts. Additional SB NO. 127 ENROLLED 1 donations inter vivos pursuant to this Paragraph may be made, except to a 2 stepsibling, descendant of a stepsibling, step-descendant of a sibling, or a trust 3 for the benefit of a stepsibling or step-descendant of a sibling, subject to a 4 maximum lifetime limit of five times the prevailing federal estate tax lifetime 5 exemption equivalent. 6 (2) Inter vivos noncharitable donations authorized in Paragraph (1) of 7 this Subsection in the name or on behalf of an interdict shall be made in equal 8 amounts to each member of the same class and generation descending from a 9 common parent. Inter vivos noncharitable donations authorized in Paragraph 10 (1) of this Subsection and made to nieces and nephews, including step-nieces and 11 step-nephews, with the same parent shall be made equally to each descendant 12 of that parent of the same generation. 13 (3) If the federal gift tax has been repealed, cumulative inter vivos 14 transfers otherwise authorized in Paragraph (1) of this Subsection are 15 permitted subject to an aggregate lifetime amount equal to six hundred percent 16 of the highest gift tax lifetime exemption in effect prior to the gift tax being 17 repealed. Annual exclusion donations may be made subject to the provisions of 18 this Section after donations in the maximum allowable amounts pursuant to 19 Paragraph (1) of this Subsection. 20 (4) Noncharitable testamentary dispositions made upon the death of the 21 interdict, other than through intestacy, including a self-settled trust in which the 22 interdict is a beneficiary may only be made to a direct descendant, sibling, 23 descendant of a sibling of an interdict, one or more trusts for the benefit of such 24 recipients, or one or more class trusts for the benefit of such present and future 25 recipients, including to grantor trusts, but may not be made to a stepsibling, 26 descendant of a stepsibling, or trust for the benefit of such recipient. 27 Testamentary dispositions pursuant to this Paragraph shall be made in equal 28 amounts to recipients of the same class and generation, except that a catch-up 29 bequest may be made to an interdict's direct descendant, sibling, or descendant 30 of a sibling to equalize a donation to a permissible recipient born after inter SB NO. 127 ENROLLED 1 vivos donations were made to other recipients of the same degree. 2 (5) Nothing in this Section shall be construed to annul or supersede a 3 lawful will or trust in effect prior to a person being interdicted. 4 (6) Inter vivos charitable dispositions may be made to charitable 5 recipients, including split interest trusts, donor-advised funds, charitable trusts, 6 and private foundations, even if such donees are controlled by a parent, direct 7 descendant, sibling, or descendant of a sibling of an interdict, up to a maximum 8 annual aggregate amount of ten percent of the interdict's net worth in excess of 9 one hundred million dollars. An inter vivos charitable disposition pursuant to 10 this Paragraph shall not be allowed if the interdict's net worth is less than one 11 hundred million dollars. 12 B. Inter vivos donations, testamentary and charitable dispositions, 13 including a charitable remainder trust, to one or more charities may be made 14 that take effect upon the death of the interdict provided that such charitable 15 donations shall not exceed an aggregate of seventy-five percent of the interdict's 16 gross estate at the time of his death for federal estate tax reporting purposes. 17 C.(1) The court may authorize a curator to make a donation pursuant 18 to Subsection A of this Section when the procedure provided for in Code of Civil 19 Procedure Articles 4271 and 4566 is complied with and the donation does not 20 materially impair the financial condition of the interdict and is not likely to 21 deprive the interdict of sufficient funds to provide for his future needs and 22 support. The court may require the curator to provide documentation to 23 substantiate the request to make a donation and place the information obtained 24 under seal. 25 (2) For purposes of this Subsection, it is presumed that a donation does 26 not materially impair the financial condition of the interdict or deprive the 27 interdict of sufficient resources if the curator shows that the direct or beneficial 28 net worth of the interdict is not less than one hundred million dollars as a trust 29 beneficiary or otherwise, after subtracting the value of the donation. 30 * * * SB NO. 127 ENROLLED 1 §2374. Forced heirs; disability; forced portion; limitations; net worth 2 Notwithstanding the provisions of Civil Code Article 1495, a forced heir 3 who has attained the age of majority, is permanently incapable of taking care 4 of his person or administering his estate due to mental incapacity, physical 5 infirmity, an inherited incurable disease, or other documented medical 6 condition at the time of the death of a decedent who is an ancestor of such 7 forced heir, and who has a direct or beneficial net worth in excess of fifty 8 million dollars as a trust beneficiary or otherwise, shall have a forced portion 9 of one dollar. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: MOTOR VEHICLES:  Creates the "Master Combat Infantryman" specialty license plate. (8/1/26)
Summary: MOTOR VEHICLES:  Creates the "Master Combat Infantryman" specialty license plate. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 129 BY SENATOR FOIL AND REPRESENTATIVE CHASSION 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact the Section heading of R.S. 47:463.191, (A), and (C) and to enact 3 R.S. 47:463.243, relative to motor vehicle special prestige license plates; to modify 4 the member of congress special prestige license plate; to establish the "Master 5 Combat Infantryman" special prestige license plate; to provide for creation, issuance, 6 design, fees, distribution, and rule promulgation applicable to the license plate; to 7 provide for an effective date; and to provide for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. The Section heading of R.S. 47:463.191, (A), and (C) are hereby amended 10 and reenacted and R.S. 47:463.243 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 11 §463.191. Special prestige license plate; member of congress; designated state 12 officials 13 A. Notwithstanding the provisions of R.S. 47:463(A)(3)(b), the secretary of 14 the Department of Public Safety and Corrections shall establish a special prestige 15 license plate for motor vehicles, restricted to passenger cars, pickup trucks, 16 motorcycles, recreational vehicles, and vans, which may be issued upon application 17 of any Louisiana member of congress, to members of the Louisiana Supreme 18 Court, or upon written request of the clerk of court, for official vehicles used by 19 duly commissioned officers in the course and scope of their official duties. 20 * * * 21 C. The secretary shall promulgate rules and regulations necessary to 22 implement the provisions of this Section, including rules and regulations governing 23 the issuance, transfer, and disposition of the license plates upon the death of the 24 recipient and governing the design of the plate. 25 * * * 26 §463.243. Special prestige license plate; "Master Combat Infantryman" 27 A.(1) The secretary of the Department of Public Safety and Corrections ACT No. 463 SB NO. 129 ENROLLED 1 shall establish a special prestige motor vehicle license plate to be known as the 2 "Master Combat Infantryman" plate. The license plate shall be restricted to use 3 on passenger cars, pickup trucks, recreational vehicles, motorcycles, and vans. 4 (2) The Department of Public Safety and Corrections, office of motor 5 vehicles, shall create the special prestige license plate when the applicable 6 statutory provisions are met and the department's electronic vehicle and title 7 registration system is updated to accommodate the creation of new plates. 8 B. Provided the design is in compliance with R.S. 47:463(A)(3), the 9 design shall bear a likeness of the United States Army Master Combat 10 Infantryman Badge as set forth in Department of the Army MILPER 25-093, 11 centered on the left side of the license plate and the words "Master Combat 12 Infantryman" centered on the bottom of the plate under the license plate 13 number. The colors of the plate shall be light blue and gold. 14 C. The special prestige license plate shall be issued, upon application, to 15 any person having been awarded the Combat Infantryman Badge and one of the 16 following expert badges: the Expert Infantryman Badge, the Expert Field 17 Medical Badge, or the Expert Soldier Badge which shall be sufficient to show 18 the applicant is authorized to wear the Master Combat Infantryman Badge. 19 D. The fee for the license plate shall be pursuant to R.S. 47:463 and 20 490.1. 21 E.(1) The secretary shall promulgate and adopt rules and regulations as 22 are necessary to implement the provisions of this Section. 23 (2) Except as otherwise provided in this Paragraph, each military honor 24 license plate issued pursuant to this Section shall be returned to the secretary 25 upon the death of the person to whom the plate was issued. A surviving spouse 26 of a person to whom a license plate was issued pursuant to this Section may 27 retain a license plate issued pursuant to this Section, provided the surviving 28 spouse has not remarried and provided the surviving spouse applies to the 29 secretary for a transfer of the license plate to the surviving spouse. A military 30 honor license plate transferred pursuant to this Paragraph to a surviving spouse SB NO. 129 ENROLLED 1 shall be returned to the secretary upon the death or remarriage of the surviving 2 spouse. 3 Section 2. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 4 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 5 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 6 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 7 effective on the day following such approval. Title: CONGRESS:  Provides for the redistricting of Louisiana congressional districts. (See Act) (OR +$2,934,660 GF EX See Note)
Summary: CONGRESS:  Provides for the redistricting of Louisiana congressional districts. (See Act) (OR +$2,934,660 GF EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 350 130 SLS 26RS MORRIS, JAY Author: Subject: Secretary of State Redistricting Louisiana Congressional Districts March 5, 2026 3:55 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Kimberly Fruge Proposed law will result in a one-time increase in FY 27 of $2.9 M SGF expenditures to the Secretary of State to mail out notifications to registered voters about changes to the congressional districts. The department estimates the costs of the notification card, printing, and postage at $0.99 per notification. There are 2,964,303 voters in the state. The maximum cost exposure would thus equate to approximately $2.9 M for notification cards, assuming every voter required notification. The Secretary of State reports that it intents to distribute notifications to every voter. The legislation does not appear to require such notification. To the extent that the department only sends notifications to those voters impacted by redistricting, costs will decrease accordingly. The table below represents the potential costs based on the potential number of voters impacted by the redistricting of the congressional districts. Percentage of Voters Number of Voters Potential Cost 100% 2,964,303 $2,934,660 75% 2,223,227 $2,200,995 50% 1,482,152 $1,467,330 25% 741,076 $733,665 10% 296,430 $293,466 This fiscal note assumes the Secretary of State will send out all notifications when the law becomes effective. Proposed law may also result in a minimal increase in programming costs for the Secretary of State to update voting machines to reflect the new congressional districts. This cost can likely be absorbed within the department’s existing operating budget. Proposed law redraws district boundaries for the six congressional districts. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. Title: ATTORNEYS:  Provides for limitations on recovery of attorney fees and costs in occupational licensing board disciplinary hearings. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SG RV)
Summary: ATTORNEYS:  Provides for limitations on recovery of attorney fees and costs in occupational licensing board disciplinary hearings. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SG RV)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 131 BY SENATOR PRESSLY 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 37:21, relative to professions and occupations; to provide 3 relative to limiting recovery of attorney fees and costs in disciplinary proceedings 4 initiated by professional or occupational licensing boards; to provide relative to 5 licensees; to provide relative to negotiations; to provide relative to suspension and 6 probationary periods; to provide relative to monetary fees; to provide relative to 7 certain offers of judgments; to provide relative to consent order negotiations; to 8 provide relative to attorney fees and costs incurred by the board; and to provide for 9 related matters. 10 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 11 Section 1. R.S. 37:21 is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 12 §21. Limitations on disciplinary proceedings by professional or occupational boards 13 and commissions; recovery of attorney fees and costs 14 A.(1) Unless a special law is applicable, no proceeding of any kind may be 15 initiated by a professional or occupational board or commission as follows: 16 (a) If the nature of the complaint is based on negligence or gross negligence, 17 no proceeding may be initiated after two years from discovery by the complainant. 18 However, under no circumstances shall such a proceeding be initiated more than five 19 years from the date of the act or omission. 20 (b) If the nature of the complaint is based on an intentional act or omission, 21 no proceeding may be initiated after two years from discovery by the complainant. 22 However, under no circumstances shall such a proceeding be initiated more than five 23 years from the date of the act or omission. 24 (c) If the nature of the complaint is based on fraud, no proceeding may be 25 initiated after two years from discovery by the complainant. 26 (d) If the nature of the complaint is based on a license or rules violation, no 27 proceeding may be initiated after five years from the date of the act or omission. ACT No. 939 SB NO. 131 ENROLLED 1 (2) In all cases where a complaint is filed with a professional or occupational 2 board or commission, the board or commission shall notify the licensee who is 3 specifically named in the complaint as the subject of the complaint in writing of the 4 complaint within six months after the filing of the complaint or be barred from 5 further action thereon. The board or commission shall hold any required hearing 6 within six months after the notice of the hearing, but this period may be interrupted 7 by the filing of procedural motions or suspended as provided in Paragraph (3) of this 8 Subsection. 9 (3) The time periods provided in this Subsection are suspended during the 10 pendency of a legal action involving the licensee as a party or witness if the 11 complaint arises from the same facts giving rise to the legal action or arises from the 12 licensee's activities in the legal action. For the purposes of this Subsection, "legal 13 action" includes litigation, arbitration, mediation, administrative proceeding, or other 14 disciplinary proceeding. 15 B. The provisions of this Section Subsection A of this Section shall not 16 apply to the following: 17 (1) The Louisiana State Board of Medical Examiners. 18 (2) The Louisiana State Board of Dentistry. 19 (3) A certified public accountant. 20 (4) Repealed by Acts 2023, No. 381, §2, eff. August 1, 2023. 21 (5) The Louisiana State Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors. 22 (6) The Louisiana Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and 23 Audiology. 24 (7) The Louisiana Board of Pharmacy. 25 (8) The Louisiana State Board of Nursing. 26 (9) The Louisiana State Board of Social Work Examiners. 27 (10) The Louisiana Physical Therapy Board. 28 (11) The Louisiana State Board of Practical Nurse Examiners. 29 C. The provisions of Paragraph (A)(2) of this Section with respect to the time 30 of hearing only shall not apply to the following: SB NO. 131 ENROLLED 1 (1) The Louisiana Professional Engineering and Land Surveying Board. 2 (2) The State Board of Architectural Examiners. 3 D.(1) When a professional or occupational board initiates a disciplinary 4 proceeding against a licensee and the final disposition of the proceeding results 5 in a consent order, judgment, or finding in which the licensee is the prevailing 6 party, the board shall not collect from the licensee any attorney fees or costs 7 incurred by the board after a written offer of judgment. 8 (2) For purposes of this Subsection, a licensee is deemed the prevailing 9 party when either of the following occurs: 10 (a) The licensee made a written offer of judgment, and the matter is 11 subsequently terminated, dismissed, or resolved without an adverse finding 12 against the licensee. 13 (b)(i) The consent order, judgment, or finding results in a disposition 14 more favorable to the licensee than the terms previously offered by the licensee 15 pursuant to this Subsection. 16 (ii) A result is considered more favorable to the licensee under any of the 17 following circumstances: 18 (aa) The licensee offered a suspension period, and the final consent 19 order, judgment, or finding imposes a suspension period shorter than or equal 20 to that offered by the licensee. 21 (bb) The licensee offered a probationary period, and the final consent 22 order, judgment, or finding imposes a probationary period shorter than or 23 equal to that offered by the licensee. 24 (cc) The licensee offered payment of a monetary fine, and the final 25 consent order, judgment, or finding imposes only a monetary fine in an amount 26 less than or equal to that offered by the licensee. 27 (dd) The licensee offered a suspension of licensure, and the final consent 28 order, judgment, or finding imposes no suspension of licensure, regardless of 29 whether the final disposition includes a fine, probation, or both. For purposes 30 of this Subitem, one day of suspension shall be deemed equivalent to fifteen days SB NO. 131 ENROLLED 1 of probation when comparing disciplinary sanctions that include both 2 suspension and probationary periods. 3 (ee) The final consent order, judgment, or finding imposes a combined 4 suspension and probationary period that is less severe than or equal to that 5 offered by the licensee. For purposes of this Subitem, one day of suspension 6 shall be deemed equivalent to fifteen days of probation when comparing 7 disciplinary sanctions that include both suspension and probationary periods. 8 (3) The provisions of this Subsection supersede any agency rule, 9 regulation, custom, or practice governing the payment of attorney fees and costs 10 to the extent that such rule, regulation, custom, or practice is less favorable to 11 the licensee. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: SCHOOLS:  Provides that each public school board is to install exterior master key boxes to allow for rapid entry, response, and rescue by law enforcement and first responders during a school crisis. (8/1/26) (EN INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Summary: SCHOOLS:  Provides that each public school board is to install exterior master key boxes to allow for rapid entry, response, and rescue by law enforcement and first responders during a school crisis. (8/1/26) (EN INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 132 BY SENATOR KLEINPETER 1 2 3 AN ACT 4 To amend and reenact R.S. 17:416.16.1(A)(1), (2), and (4), and (E) and to enact R.S. 5 17:416.16.1(F), relative to public school safety; to require exterior master key boxes 6 to be installed in public schools under certain circumstances; to provide for 7 installation locations; to require that certain contents be placed in the exterior master 8 key boxes; to provide for definitions; to provide for standards; and to provide for 9 related matters. 10 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 11 Section 1. R.S. 17:416.16.1(A)(1), (2), and (4), and (E) are hereby amended and 12 reenacted and R.S. 17:416.16.1(F) is hereby enacted to read as follows: 13 §416.16.1. School mapping data; exterior master key box 14 A.(1) The State Board of Elementary and Secondary Education and the state 15 Department of Education shall coordinate with the Louisiana Center for Safe Schools 16 to provide the mapping data of all public schools in order to provide law enforcement 17 officials and other first responders with the appropriate tools needed for school 18 safety. 19 (2) Upon preparing a school crisis management and response plan in 20 accordance with R.S. 17:416.16 each city, parish, or other local public school system 21 governing authority or a vendor shall submit a copy of the most recent blueprints and 22 school mapping data of each school building and facility in the school system and 23 the method by which law enforcement officers and other first responders can 24 obtain access to locked facilities, including all external and internal locks, or the 25 location of the exterior master key box, to each local law enforcement agency with 26 jurisdiction over any portion of the school governing authority, each local and state 27 public safety and emergency preparedness office, the Louisiana Center for Safe ACT No. 738 SB NO. 132 ENROLLED 1 Schools, and to the school's office for use in response to emergencies. 2 * * * 3 (4) For the purposes of this Section, the following terms shall have the 4 following meanings: 5 (a) "Exterior master key box" means a secure, wall-mounted, anti-theft 6 resistant container that is installed outside a school's building to provide first 7 responders with immediate access to the school during a school crisis to allow 8 for rapid response and rescue. 9 (a)(b) "First responders" means local, state, and federal law enforcement 10 agencies, fire, public safety, and emergency preparedness officials. 11 (b)(c) "School mapping data" means information provided in an electronic 12 or digital format to assist first responders in responding to emergencies at public 13 schools. 14 * * * 15 E. Each public school governing authority that has not, prior to January 16 1, 2028, instituted a system for allowing access to locked facilities to law 17 enforcement officials and other first responders shall from funds appropriated 18 by the legislature to the Louisiana Center for Safe Schools, install at least one 19 exterior master key box on the campus of each public school under its 20 jurisdiction. 21 (1) Each exterior master key box is to be installed at a location on the 22 school's campus to permit first responders to gain expedited access to the school 23 building. 24 (2) The precise location of the exterior master key box shall be 25 determined by the public school governing authority in collaboration with local 26 law enforcement agencies for the jurisdiction in which the school building is 27 located. 28 (3) The exterior master key box shall contain all of the following: 29 (a) Copies of building keys or key cards to obtain entry to all doors on 30 the school campus. SB NO. 132 ENROLLED 1 (b) Printed maps with school mapping data that is required by this 2 Section. 3 (4) Each master key box installed at a school shall meet the basic 4 commercial applications standard for anti-theft alarms and devices as 5 prescribed under Underwriters Laboratories Standard 1037. 6 E.F. Blueprints and school mapping data of a public school building or 7 facility shall not be subject to the Public Records Law. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: SENATE:  Provides for redistricting of the Louisiana Senate. (See Act) (OR +$2,934,660 GF EX See Note)
Summary: SENATE:  Provides for redistricting of the Louisiana Senate. (See Act) (OR +$2,934,660 GF EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 143 136 SLS 26RS KLEINPETER Author: Subject: Secretary of State Redistricting Louisiana Senate Districts March 6, 2026 9:01 AM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Kimberly Fruge Proposed law will result in a one-time increase in FY 27 of $2.9 M SGF expenditures to the Secretary of State to mail out notifications to registered voters about changes to the Louisiana Senate districts. The department estimates the costs of the notification card, printing, and postage at $0.99 per notification. There are 2,964,303 voters in the state. The maximum cost exposure would thus equate to approximately $2.9 M for notification cards, assuming every voter required notification. The Secretary of State reports that it intends to distribute notifications to every voter. The legislation does not appear to require such notification. To the extent that the department only sends notifications to those voters impacted by redistricting, costs will decrease accordingly. The table below represents the potential costs based on the potential number of voters impacted by the redistricting of the Louisiana Senate districts. Percentage of Voters Number of Voters Potential Cost 100% 2,964,303 $2,934,660 75% 2,223,227 $2,200,995 50% 1,482,152 $1,467,330 25% 741,076 $733,665 10% 296,430 $293,466 This fiscal note assumes the Secretary of State will send out all notifications when the law becomes effective. Proposed law may also result in a minimal increase in programming costs for the Secretary of State to update voting machines to reflect the new Louisiana Senate districts. This cost can likely be absorbed within the department’s existing operating budget. Proposed law redraws district boundaries for the 39 Louisiana Senate districts. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. REVENUE EXPLANATION EXPENDITURE EXPLANATION Provides for redistricting of the Louisiana Senate. Proposed law removes provision that signs be bright yellow. Present law provides that an entrance to a school
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 138 Original 2026 Regular Session Cathey Present law provides signs are to be bright yellow and read "Photo enforcement devices at use". Proposed law removes provision that signs be bright yellow. Present law provides that an entrance to a school zone have a painted a two- foot-wide yellow stripe completely across the roadway with"Entering School Zone" painted in 12-inch white lettering. Proposed law removes provision for a two foot wide yellow stripe painted in 12 inch white lettering and adds the provision that pavement word markings be in accordance with the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices (MUTCD). Present law provides for a painted, two-foot-wide yellow stripe completely across the roadway with "School Zone Ahead" painted in 12 inch white lettering. Proposed law removes the provision for two foot wide yellow stripes and painted 12 inch white lettering and adds the provision that pavement word markings be in accordance with the MUTCD. Proposed law provides there are no requirements in present law or proposed law that will conflict with the MUTCD. Present law prohibits local municipal or parish authorities from using automated speed enforcement devices on interstate highways. Present law exempts a governing authority of a municipality with a population of less than sixteen thousand and more than fifteen thousand according to the latest federal decennial census within a parish with a population of less than 90,000 and more than 70,000 according to the latest federal decennial census from the prohibition in present law. Proposed law repeals the exemption. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends R.S. 32:47(A)(3), (4), and (5); adds R.S. 32:47(A)(8); repeals R.S. 32:43(A)(4))

Title: LAW ENFORCEMENT:  Provides relative to funding of bulletproof vests for peace officers. (gov sig) (EN SEE FISC NOTE EX)
Summary: LAW ENFORCEMENT:  Provides relative to funding of bulletproof vests for peace officers. (gov sig) (EN SEE FISC NOTE EX)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 143 BY SENATOR EDMONDS AND REPRESENTATIVES ADAMS, AMEDEE, BACALA, BOUDREAUX, BOYER, BRAUD, BROUSSARD, BRYANT, BUTLER, CARRIER, DICKERSON, FONTENOT, FREIBERG, HORTON, ILLG, JACKSON, MIKE JOHNSON, LACOMBE, MACK, MARTINEZ, NEWELL, OWEN, SAWYER, SCHAMERHORN, STAGNI AND VENTRELLA 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 40:2405.2, relative to the issuance of bulletproof vests to peace officers; to 3 provide relative to funding the acquisition and distribution of bulletproof vests; to 4 create the Louisiana Commission on Law Enforcement Bulletproof Vest Fund; to 5 provide for the purpose and use of the fund; to provide for eligibility; to provide for 6 administration by the Louisiana Commission on Law Enforcement and 7 Administration of Criminal Justice; to provide for the transfer, deposit, and use of 8 monies in the fund; to provide for definitions; to require the promulgation of rules 9 and regulations with regard to the standards of bulletproof vests; to provide for an 10 effective date; and to provide for related matters. 11 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 12 Section 1. R.S. 40:2405.2 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 13 §2405.2. Commission on Law Enforcement Bulletproof Vest Fund; creation of 14 fund; eligibility 15 A. The legislature hereby finds and declares all of the following: 16 (1) Smaller local law enforcement agencies within this state often lack ACT No. 739 SB NO. 143 ENROLLED 1 sufficient financial resources to provide adequate protective equipment, 2 including bulletproof vests, for their commissioned peace officers. 3 (2) The provision of bulletproof vests is essential to protect the lives and 4 safety of those officers who serve and protect the public. 5 (3) The establishment of a dedicated fund is necessary to assist local law 6 enforcement agencies with limited financial capacity in acquiring bulletproof 7 vests, thereby saving lives and enhancing officer safety. 8 B. For the purposes of this Section, the following terms shall mean: 9 (1) "Commission" means the Louisiana Commission on Law 10 Enforcement and Administration of Criminal Justice. 11 (2) "Eligible law enforcement agency" means any local, municipal, or 12 parish law enforcement agency within this state that employs fifteen or fewer 13 full-time commissioned peace officers. 14 (3) "Fund" means the Commission on Law Enforcement Bulletproof 15 Vest Fund established pursuant to this Section. 16 (4) "Peace officer" shall have the same definition as provided in R.S. 17 40:2402. 18 C.(1) There is hereby established in the state treasury, as a special fund, 19 the "Commission on Law Enforcement Bulletproof Vest Fund" hereinafter 20 referred to in this Section as the "fund". 21 (2) After allocation of money to the Bond Security and Redemption Fund 22 as provided in Article VII, Section 9(B) of the Constitution of Louisiana, the 23 treasurer shall deposit into the fund the following: 24 (a) Any monies transferred or appropriated to the fund by the 25 legislature. 26 (b) Donations, gifts, grants, or other revenue, including federal funds 27 received for the purchase of bulletproof vests, collected for the purposes of this 28 Section and received by the state treasurer. 29 (3) Monies in the fund shall be invested in the same manner as monies 30 in the state general fund. Any interest earned on the investment of monies in the SB NO. 143 ENROLLED 1 fund shall be credited to the fund. Unexpended and unencumbered monies in 2 the fund at the end of the fiscal year shall remain in the fund. 3 (4) The legislature shall appropriate monies from the fund to the 4 commission for any purpose authorized pursuant to this Section. 5 D.(1) Monies in the fund shall be used solely to provide financial 6 assistance to eligible law enforcement agencies for the purchase of bulletproof 7 vests for commissioned peace officers. 8 (2) In awarding funds pursuant to this Section, the commission shall give 9 priority to eligible law enforcement agencies that demonstrate a lack of 10 adequate bulletproof vests for their commissioned peace officers. 11 (3) The commission may consider the extent to which an agency lacks 12 bulletproof vests, the number of officers without vests, and the agency’s 13 financial needs when determining awards. 14 (4) The commission may establish matching fund requirements or 15 cost-sharing provisions as it deems appropriate. 16 E.(1) The Louisiana Commission on Law Enforcement and 17 Administration of Criminal Justice shall adopt rules and regulations in 18 accordance with the Administrative Procedure Act necessary for the 19 administration and enforcement of this Section. 20 (2) The commission shall adopt rules with respect to all of the following: 21 (a) Standards for the issuance of bulletproof vests; that the vests shall 22 meet United States Department of Justice, National Institute for Justice 23 standards; and that the vests shall be wholly manufactured within nations that 24 are a party to the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement entered into force 25 on July 1, 2020. 26 (b) Application procedures and requirements. 27 (c) Criteria for awarding funds. 28 (d) Reporting and accountability requirements for recipient agencies. 29 Section 2. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 30 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature SB NO. 143 ENROLLED 1 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 2 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 3 effective on the day following such approval. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

[R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 144 Original 2026 Regular Session Allain Present law authorizes the police juries of parishes to form and create one or more hospital service districts within the respective parishes. Present law provides for the objects and purposes of the hospital service districts and their governing bodies, including cooperating with other public and private institutions and agencies engaged in providing hospital and other health services to residents of the district. Proposed law adds cooperating with healthcare facilities that are engaged in providing hospital and other health services to the residents of the district as an object and purpose of the district. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends R.S. 46:1052(5))

Title: HEALTH SERVICES:  Provides relative to adult residential care providers. (gov sig)
Summary: HEALTH SERVICES:  Provides relative to adult residential care providers. (gov sig)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 145 BY SENATORS WOMACK, ABRAHAM, BARROW, BARTHELEMY, BASS, BOUDREAUX, CARTER, CLOUD, DUPLESSIS, FESI, FOIL, HARRIS, HENRY, JACKSON-ANDREWS, JENKINS, MCMATH, MIZELL, MORRIS, MYERS AND PRICE 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 40:2166.11, relative to adult residential care providers; to provide for 3 generators or alternative electrical power sources; to provide for requirements; to 4 provide for duties of the Louisiana Department of Health; provide for waivers; to 5 provide for rulemaking; to provide for duties of the office of the state fire marshal; 6 to provide for an effective date; and to provide for related matters. 7 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 8 Section 1. R.S. 40:2166.11 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 9 §2166.11. Generator; alternative electrical power source 10 A. All licensed adult residential care providers shall have a generator or 11 other alternative electrical power source in the event of the loss of primary 12 electrical power in accordance with this Section. "Alternative electrical power 13 source" shall include mobile generators, shared campus systems, contracted 14 generators, or other engineered emergency power solution. 15 B. The generator or alternative electrical power source shall have a 16 simultaneous capability of providing sufficient electrical power for all of the 17 following: 18 (1) Life safety systems. 19 (2) Lighting in patient care areas. 20 (3) Medical equipment in patient care areas. 21 (4) Electrical components of the approved potable water system. 22 (5) Electrical components of the approved sewer system. 23 (6) Operation of the adult residential care provider's medication 24 dispensing and medication refrigerator systems. 25 (7) Operation of the adult residential care provider's dietary services and ACT No. 740 SB NO. 145 ENROLLED 1 related refrigeration. 2 (8) Operation of the adult residential care provider's laundry services. 3 (9) Systems necessary to maintain a clinically safe indoor temperature 4 sufficient to protect residents from heat- or cold-related harm. 5 C.(1) The generator or alternative electrical power source shall either be 6 installed permanently onsite at the adult residential care provider facility or 7 provided through an alternative method, including mobile, shared, or 8 temporary systems. The adult residential care provider shall have an adequate 9 supply of fuel to operate the generator or alternative electrical power source 10 under full load for a minimum of seventy-two hours. The fuel shall be stored 11 onsite at the adult residential care provider facility, delivered to the provider 12 prior to the emergency event through contracted fuel delivery agreements or 13 shared fuel resources, or any combination thereof. 14 (2) Natural gas is an allowable fuel source and meets the onsite fuel 15 requirement if there is a direct municipal gas line or an onsite propane tank 16 sufficient in size to meet the fuel requirements provided for this in this Section. 17 D. Each adult residential care provider shall have fuel delivery 18 agreements in place that will extend the uninterrupted operation of the 19 generator or alternative electrical power source under full load for the duration 20 of a single emergency event. Adult residential care providers may interrupt 21 operation of the generator or alternative electrical power source to conduct 22 routine maintenance as recommended by the manufacturer's specifications. 23 E. Each adult residential care provider that does not have a permanently 24 installed onsite generator or alternative electrical power source shall do all of 25 the following: 26 (1) Have a rapid connect system for any contracted or mobile generators 27 and electrical power. 28 (2) Annually update its emergency operations plan to include provisions 29 for the health and safety of the provider's residents in the event of a power loss. 30 The plan shall include information on the mobile generators, shared campus SB NO. 145 ENROLLED 1 systems, contracted generators, other engineered power solutions, rapid connect 2 systems, and fuel arrangements and agreements. The provider's emergency 3 operations plan, or specific information contained in the plan, shall be 4 submitted to the Louisiana Department of Health upon request. 5 F. Adult residential care providers shall meet the requirements of this 6 Section no later than July 1, 2027. The department may grant a one-time 7 extension, not to exceed twelve months, upon written application by an adult 8 residential care provider that compliance with this Section has been delayed due 9 to extraordinary and unforeseen circumstances. No extension shall be granted 10 if the adult residential care provider fails to provide sufficient evidence of 11 substantial compliance or good faith efforts to comply with the requirement 12 deadline provided for in this Subsection. 13 G. The department, after consultation with the office of the state fire 14 marshal, may promulgate rules and regulations in accordance with the 15 Administrative Procedure Act, to implement the provisions of this Section. The 16 department is authorized to utilize the emergency rulemaking process provided 17 in R.S. 49:962 for adoption of the rules and regulations to implement the 18 provisions of this Section. 19 H. The office of the state fire marshal shall determine the adult 20 residential care provider's compliance with the provisions of Subsections B 21 through D of this Section. The office of the state fire marshal may require the 22 adult residential care provider to submit plan review applications with required 23 documentation, conduct on-site inspections, and determine that an alternative 24 electric power source is not compliant or not able to safely operate to meet the 25 requirements of this Section. Any noncompliance shall be referred to the 26 department. 27 Section 2. The Louisiana Department of Health shall convene a stakeholder advisory 28 workgroup, including representatives of the Louisiana Assisted Living Association and 29 LeadingAge Southeast, to discuss implementation, compliance progress, potential barriers, 30 and rulemaking of the provisions provided for in this Act. The stakeholder advisory SB NO. 145 ENROLLED 1 workgroup shall be convened for its first meeting no later than October 1, 2026, by the 2 department and shall terminate no later than July 1, 2028. 3 Section 3. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 4 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 5 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 6 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 7 effective on the day following such approval. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: OFFICIAL JOURNALS:  Provides relative to the official journal of parishes, municipalities, school boards, and other political subdivisions. (gov sig)
Summary: , which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 146 Original 2026 Regular Session Fesi Proposed law provides that "Website" shall mean a Uniform Resource Locator ("URL"), or unique online web address, used to locate and access resources on the internet, which complies with the requirement of p
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 146 Original 2026 Regular Session Fesi Proposed law provides that "Website" shall mean a Uniform Resource Locator ("URL"), or unique online web address, used to locate and access resources on the internet, which complies with the requirement of present law (R.S. 43:141.1(A)) and meets all of the following requirements: (1) Is the primary internet website owned or operated by or on behalf of the governing body choosing to designate a website as its official journal. (2) Is publicly accessible at no cost to users and without requiring registration or login. (3) Includes a dedicated section for legal notices, advertisements, proclamations, minutes, ordinances, resolutions, budgets and other official proceedings, clearly labeled and easily navigable from the homepage. (4) Maintains an archive of all published notices for at least one year from the date of posting, with searchable functionality by date, keyword, and type of notice. (5) Ensures that notices are posted in a timely manner as required by law and remain unaltered once posted, except for corrections clearly noted. (6) Complies with accessibility standards under the Americans with Disabilities Act and provide a mechanism for users to request alternative formats. (7) Identifies a person, and that person's contact information, responsible for receiving and publishing information required by law to be published in the official journal. Present law provides that the police juries, city and parish councils, municipal corporations, and school boards in all the parishes, the parish of Orleans excepted, at the first meeting in June of each year, shall select a newspaper as the official journal for their respective parishes, towns, or cities for a term of one year. Proposed law retains present law and adds that a website may be designated as an official journal. Present law provides that every official journal selected pursuant to present law shall have a website and shall post all official proceedings and public notices that it publishes on that website and on a collective website in which a majority of the state's official journals participate. Proposed law retains present law but specifies that this requirement only applies to newspapers. Present law provides that the newspaper selected shall be known as the official journal of the parish, town, city or school board, and it shall publish all minutes, ordinances, resolutions, budgets and other official proceedings of the police jury, town or city councils, or the school board. Proposed law retains present law but adds websites as qualifying official journals. Present law provides that an official journal that publishes official proceedings and public notices shall, within 30 days after such a publication, submit a bill for the publication with a copy of the original published proceeding or notice and a verified statement that does both of the following: (1) States the rate charged to the political subdivision for the publication. (2) Certifies the number and dates of publication. Proposed law retains present law but adds newspapers. Present law provides that the official of any municipal corporation, police jury, or school board by law responsible for the preparing and recording of the official proceedings who, within 20 days from the date of any meeting at which the official proceedings were had, wilfully neglects or fails to furnish the official journal with a copy of the minutes, ordinances, resolutions, budgets, and proceedings for publication, shall be fined not less than $25 nor more than $500, or be imprisoned for not less than 10 days nor more than six months, or both. Proposed law retains present law. Proposed law provides that the person identified, in accordance with present law (R.S. 43:140(6)(g)), as responsible for publishing of the official proceedings of any municipal corporation, police jury, or school board, who, within 20 days from receipt of any request to publish official proceedings, willfully neglects or fails to publish a copy of the minutes, ordinances, resolutions, budgets, and proceedings on the governing body's website, shall be fined not less than $25 nor more than $500, or be imprisoned for not less than 10 days nor more than six months, or both. Proposed law provides that notwithstanding any provision of proposed law, a levee, drainage, subdrainage, road, subroad, navigation, and sewerage districts, or other political subdivisions of state and parishes, may designate a website as defined in present law (R.S. 43:140(6)) as its official journal, in the same manner and under the same provisions as required by present law (R.S. 43:141, et seq). Effective upon signature of the governor or lapse of time for gubernatorial action. (Amends R.S. 43:141(A), 141.1, 143, 143.1(B)(1), and 144; adds R.S. 43:140(6) and 171(D))

Title: FOOD SAFETY:  Prohibits certain actions with respect to cell cultured food products for human consumption. (8/1/26)
Summary: FOOD SAFETY:  Prohibits certain actions with respect to cell cultured food products for human consumption. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 152 BY SENATOR FESI AND REPRESENTATIVES AMEDEE, BAYHAM, COATES, EGAN, OWEN, SCHAMERHORN AND WYBLE 1 2 AN ACT 3 To enact Part V of Chapter 30 of Title 3 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, to be 4 comprised of R.S. 3:4750.1 through 4750.5, relative to cell cultured food products; 5 to prohibit the sale of cell cultured food products for human consumption; to provide 6 for penalties; to provide for rules and regulations; to provide for certain exceptions; 7 to provide for effectiveness; and to provide for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. Part V of Chapter 30 of Title 3 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, 10 comprised of R.S. 3:4750.1 through 4750.5, is hereby enacted to read as follows: 11 PART V. CELL CULTURED FOOD PRODUCTS 12 §4750.1. Short title 13 This Part shall be known and may be cited as the "Cell Cultured Food 14 Products Act". 15 §4750.2. Cell cultured food products; prohibition 16 A. Notwithstanding any other provision of law to the contrary, no person 17 shall manufacture, distribute, sell, or offer for sale, any cell cultured food 18 product for human consumption in this state. 19 B. For purposes of this Part, "cell cultured food product" means any 20 cultured animal tissue produced from in vitro animal cell cultures outside of the 21 organism from which it is derived. 22 §4750.3. Violations; penalties 23 A. A violator of any provision of this Part or of any rule or regulation 24 adopted pursuant to the provisions of this Part shall be subject to a civil penalty 25 of not more than five hundred dollars for each violation. Each day on which a 26 violation occurs shall be a separate offense. ACT No. 742 SB NO. 152 ENROLLED 1 B. Penalties may be assessed only by a ruling of the commissioner based 2 upon an adjudicatory hearing held in accordance with the provisions of the 3 Administrative Procedure Act. 4 C. In addition to civil penalties, the commissioner may assess the 5 proportionate costs of the adjudicatory hearing against the offender. The 6 commissioner shall determine the amount of costs to be assessed. 7 D. The commissioner may institute civil proceedings to enforce his 8 rulings in the district court for the parish in which the violation occurred. 9 E. The commissioner may institute civil proceedings seeking injunctive 10 relief to restrain and prevent the violation of the provisions of this Part, or of 11 the rules and regulations adopted pursuant to the provisions of this Part, in the 12 district court for the parish in which the violation occurred. 13 §4750.4. Rules and regulations 14 The commissioner may adopt rules and regulations necessary to 15 implement this Part. All rules and regulations shall be adopted in accordance 16 with the provisions of the Administrative Procedure Act. 17 §4750.5. Effectiveness 18 The provisions of this Part shall become effective only upon a final ruling 19 by the United States Supreme Court holding that prohibitions on cell cultured 20 food products do not violate the United States Constitution; however, nothing 21 in this Part shall prohibit accredited institutions of higher education in this state 22 from conducting research on cell cultured food products. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: SUPPLEMENTAL PAY:  Provides for employees who are eligible for extra compensation paid by certain local entities. (8/1/26) (OR DECREASE GF EX See Note)
Summary: SUPPLEMENTAL PAY:  Provides for employees who are eligible for extra compensation paid by certain local entities. (8/1/26) (OR DECREASE GF EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 491 153 SLS 26RS KLEINPETER Author: Subject: Department of Public Safety Supplemental pay for certain local entities March 19, 2026 5:48 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Katherine Granier Proposed law provides for employees of certain local government entities to be eligible for supplemental or extra compensation. Proposed law defines eligible employee classifications and the conditions under which local governing authorities may provide such pay. Proposed law retains present law but further excludes from the term “employee” persons who do not hold an active emergency medical responder license or higher issued in accordance with the provisions of R. S.40:1133.1 et. seq. Proposed law provides for an effective date of August 1, 2026. Proposed law may result in an indeterminable decrease in state expenditures due to the exclusion of certain employees from eligibility for state-funded supplemental pay. The magnitude of the decrease will depend on the number of employees affected and the amount of supplemental pay otherwise payable. Additionally, the number of current recipients who do not hold an active emergency medical responder license is unknown, introducing further uncertainty in estimating fiscal effects. Under current law (R.S. 40:1666), eligible full-time paid firefighters who meet statutory training and service requirements are entitled to state supplemental pay in addition to their regular local compensation. Eligible firefighters currently receive $600 per month in supplemental pay from the state. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. The clerk of court or his 17 designated deputy shall have the same powers, duties, and responsibilities, and 18 shall be governed by all applicable provisions of law pertaining to jury 19 commissioners. The clerk of court of West Baton Rouge Parish shall perform ACT No. 249 SB NO. 154 ENROLLED 1 the duties and responsibilities otherwise imposed upon him by law with respect 2 to jury venires, shall coordinate the jury venire process, and shall receive the 3 compensation generally authorized for a jury commissioner. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: HEALTH/ACC INSURANCE:  Provides for coverage of certain dental care when connected with cancer treatment. (gov sig) (EN INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Summary: HEALTH/ACC INSURANCE:  Provides for coverage of certain dental care when connected with cancer treatment. (gov sig) (EN INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 155 BY SENATOR TALBOT AND REPRESENTATIVES BERAULT, CARRIER, CHASSION, DEWITT, FISHER, FREEMAN, FREIBERG, HORTON, ILLG, KERNER, LAFLEUR, LYONS, MACK AND ZERINGUE 1 2 AN ACT 3 To enact R.S. 22:1077.6, relative to dental care and cancer treatment; to provide for 4 healthcare coverage for certain medically necessary dental procedures as a result of 5 cancer treatment; to clarify healthcare insurers are not required to provide coverage 6 for routine preventative dental care; to provide for applicability; to provide for an 7 effective date; and to provide for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. R.S. 22:1077.6 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 10 §1077.6. Coverage for dental services for cancer patients 11 A.(1) Except as provided in Subsections B and C of this Section, an 12 insurer offering a health coverage plan in this state shall provide coverage for 13 evaluation, imaging, clinical examination, and dental extractions that are 14 medically necessary to reduce the risk of infection, eliminate infection, or treat 15 tooth loss or decay as part of a pre-cancer treatment screening for an insured 16 who has been diagnosed with cancer. 17 (2) The dental services shall be covered when performed as part of a pre- 18 treatment medical clearance protocol for insureds scheduled to undergo cancer 19 treatment. 20 B. A healthcare insurer is not required to provide coverage for routine 21 preventive dental care, including cleaning and sealants. 22 C. The provisions of this Section shall not apply to a plan providing 23 coverage for excepted benefits as defined in R.S. 22:1061, limited benefit health 24 insurance plans, and short-term policies that have a term of less than twelve 25 months. 26 Section 2. The provisions of this Act shall apply to any new policy, contract, ACT No. 743 SB NO. 155 ENROLLED 1 program, or health coverage plan issued on or after January 1, 2027. Any policy, contract, 2 or plan in effect prior to January 1, 2027, shall convert to conform to the provisions of this 3 Act on or before the renewal date thereof, but in no event later than January 1, 2028. 4 Section 3. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 5 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 6 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 7 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 8 effective on the day following such approval. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: CRIME/PUNISHMENT:  Increases the maximum period of imprisonment for negligent homicide. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE GF EX)
Summary: CRIME/PUNISHMENT:  Increases the maximum period of imprisonment for negligent homicide. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE GF EX)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 156 BY SENATOR KLEINPETER 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 14:32(C)(1) and (2), relative to negligent homicide; to provide 3 relative to penalties; to increase the maximum term of imprisonment for negligent 4 homicide under certain circumstances; and to provide for related matters. 5 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 6 Section 1. R.S. 14:32(C)(1) and (2) are hereby amended and reenacted to read as 7 follows: 8 §32. Negligent homicide 9 * * * 10 C.(1)(a) Except as provided for in Paragraph (2) of this Subsection, whoever 11 commits the crime of negligent homicide shall be imprisoned with or without hard 12 labor for not more than ten years, fined not more than five thousand dollars, or 13 imprisoned, with or without hard labor, for not more than fifteen years, or both. 14 (b) If the court does not order the offender to a term of imprisonment 15 when the following two factors are established, the court shall state, both orally 16 and in writing at the time of sentencing, the reasons for not sentencing the 17 offender to a term of imprisonment: 18 (i) The fatality was caused by a person engaged in the operation of, or in 19 actual physical control of, any motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or other 20 means of conveyance; and 21 (ii) The offender's blood alcohol concentration contributed to the fatality. 22 (2)(a) If the victim killed was under the age of ten thirteen years, the 23 offender shall be imprisoned at hard labor, without benefit of probation, parole, or 24 suspension of sentence, for not less than two years nor more than ten twenty years. 25 (b) If the court does not order the offender to a term of imprisonment when 26 the following two factors are established, the court shall state, both orally and in 27 writing at the time of sentencing, the reasons for not sentencing the offender to a ACT No. 466 SB NO. 156 ENROLLED 1 term of imprisonment: 2 (i) The fatality was caused by a person engaged in the operation of, or in 3 actual physical control of, any motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or other means of 4 conveyance; and 5 (ii) The offender's blood alcohol concentration contributed to the fatality. 6 * * * PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: SCHOOLS:  Provides for paid parental leave for eligible employees. (gov sig) (EN INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Summary: SCHOOLS:  Provides for paid parental leave for eligible employees. (gov sig) (EN INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 157 BY SENATOR JENKINS AND REPRESENTATIVES ADAMS, BRASS, BROUSSARD, CARPENTER, CARRIER, CHASSION, COATES, FISHER, FONTENOT, FREEMAN, FREIBERG, GREEN, DANA HENRY, JACKSON, KNOX, LAFLEUR, TERRY LANDRY, LYONS, MACK, MARCELLE, MARTINEZ, MENA, MURRAY, NEWELL, SCHLEGEL, STAGNI, TAYLOR, WALTERS AND YOUNG 1 2 AN ACT 3 To enact Subpart C-1 of Part X of Chapter 2 of Title 17 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes 4 of 1950, to be comprised of R.S. 17:1214 and 1214.1, and R.S. 17:3996(B)(92), and 5 to repeal R.S. 17:48, 500.2(A)(1)(b), 1202(A)(1)(b), 1206.2(A)(1)(b), and 1211, 6 relative to parental leave for educators; to provide for paid leave for eligible 7 employees; to provide for definitions; to provide for requirements to qualify for 8 parental leave; to provide for compensation and length of time for use of parental 9 leave; to provide for policies and procedures to implement the parental leave; to 10 provide for certain notification; to provide for a special fund; to provide for 11 implementation; to provide for an effective date; and to provide for related matters. 12 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 13 Section 1. Subpart C-1 of Part X of Chapter 2 of Title 17 of the Louisiana Revised 14 Statutes of 1950, comprised of R.S. 17:1214 and 1214.1, and R.S. 17:3996(B)(92) are hereby 15 enacted to read as follows: 16 SUBPART C-1. PARENTAL LEAVE 17 §1214. Parental leave for educators; tenure status unaffected; definitions 18 A. As used in this Subpart, the following terms have the following 19 meanings: 20 (1) "Child" means under the age of eighteen years, the biological, 21 adopted, or foster child, stepchild, legal ward, or other minor to whom a ACT No. 744 SB NO. 157 ENROLLED 1 covered individual stands in loco parentis. 2 (2) "Department" means the Louisiana Department of Education. 3 (3) "Eligible employee" means any individual identified in R.S. 17:43, 4 17:411, 17:441, 17:491, 17:1170, 17:1200, or 17:1205 who is classified as full or 5 part time and has been employed for at least twelve months with a local 6 education agency. 7 (4) "LEA" means local education agency, which has the same definition 8 as in R.S. 17:1942. 9 (5) "Paid parental leave" means six weeks, or two hundred forty hours, 10 of paid leave at one hundred percent of the eligible employee's base pay without 11 deduction to an eligible employee's current or accrued annual, sick, or 12 compensatory leave balances. 13 (6) "Qualifying event" means: 14 (a) Before and after the birth of a child, including but not limited to 15 prenatal and postnatal appointments. 16 (b) Pregnancy loss, including stillbirth. 17 (c) Placement of a child with the employee for adoption, including but 18 not limited to mandatory meetings related to adoption or preplacement and 19 post-placement court proceedings. 20 (d) Placement of a child with the employee for foster care, including but 21 not limited to mandatory meetings related to foster placement or preplacement 22 and post-placement court proceedings. 23 (7) "Superintendent" means the superintendent of the Louisiana 24 Department of Education. 25 B.(1) Pursuant to this Section, every city, parish, and other local public 26 school board shall: 27 (a) Grant paid parental leave to an eligible employee who experiences a 28 qualifying event, and as of the date of the qualifying event has been employed 29 by the LEA for at least twelve months. 30 (b) Allow the position to be filled by the appointment of substitute SB NO. 157 ENROLLED 1 teachers when it has been temporarily vacated by a teacher who has been 2 granted paid parental leave, or by a teacher transferred to the position when it 3 has been temporarily vacated by a teacher taking a maternity, foster, or 4 adoptive leave pursuant to this Section. A substitute teacher appointed pursuant 5 to this Subparagraph shall not acquire any tenure rights or privileges, unless 6 the substitute teacher has subsequently been appointed to fill a regular vacancy 7 and has the necessary qualifications. 8 (c) Restore an eligible employee to the same position and same 9 assignment at the same school held before the taking of paid parental leave 10 upon return from such leave. 11 (d) Allow an eligible employee to use paid parental leave continuously or 12 intermittently, provided that the paid parental leave is used within twelve weeks 13 of the qualifying event. 14 (e) Prohibit an eligible employee who takes fewer than six weeks, or two 15 hundred forty hours, of paid parental leave reserving his remaining weeks of 16 paid parental leave for a subsequently occurring qualifying event. 17 (f) Compensate a full-time employee at the rate of one hundred percent 18 of the employee's base pay pursuant to the salary schedule provided in R.S. 19 17:418 for a period not to exceed six weeks during the available leave period. 20 (g) Compensate a part-time employee at the rate of one hundred percent 21 of the employee's base pay for a period not to exceed six weeks, based upon a 22 prorated basis corresponding to the percentage of hours the employee normally 23 works. 24 (h) Prohibit the donation of paid parental leave to another employee or 25 leave pool. 26 (i) Allow both parents, if they are eligible employees, paid parental leave 27 to be taken concurrently, consecutively, or at a different time as the other 28 eligible employee. 29 (j) Prohibit requiring the use of an eligible employee's sick, annual, or 30 other accrued leave before taking paid parental leave. Paid parental leave shall SB NO. 157 ENROLLED 1 run concurrently with leave requested under the Family and Medical Leave Act 2 "FMLA". 3 (k) At least twelve months must elapse from the beginning of one 4 parental leave period before an employee is eligible for another parental leave 5 period. For a new parental leave period, there must be a new qualifying event 6 on or after the twelve month waiting period. 7 (2)(a) It shall be unlawful for an LEA or any other person to commit 8 interference or restrain or deny the exercise of, or the attempt to exercise, any 9 right protected under this Section. 10 (b) It shall be unlawful for an LEA's absence control policy to count paid 11 parental leave taken under this Section, as an absence that may lead to or result 12 in discipline, discharge, demotion, suspension, or any other adverse action. 13 (3) The granting of paid parental leave shall not affect any of the tenure 14 rights or privileges which the eligible employee may have acquired. 15 (4)(a) The provisions of this Section shall not diminish an employer's 16 obligation to comply with any of the following that provide more generous leave 17 or more generous benefits: 18 (i) A collective bargaining agreement. 19 (ii) An employer policy. 20 (iii) An employment contract. 21 (iv) Any applicable local, state, or federal law. 22 (b) An individual's rights, privileges, or remedies to paid parental leave 23 and benefits under this Section shall not be diminished by a collective 24 bargaining agreement entered into, retained, amended, or renewed, or an 25 employer policy adopted, amended, or retained, after the effective date of this 26 Section. 27 (c) The provisions of this Section shall not diminish an individual's 28 rights, privileges, or remedies under a collective bargaining agreement, 29 employer policy, or employment contract, as applicable. 30 (d) Any agreement by an individual to waive the individual's rights SB NO. 157 ENROLLED 1 under the provisions of this Section is null and void as against public policy. 2 C. Every city, parish, and other local public school board shall adopt 3 policies and procedures to give effect to the intent and purposes of this Section. 4 However, unless as otherwise indicated, the following applies: 5 (1) Prior to taking paid parental leave, an eligible employee shall submit 6 a request for paid parental leave form thirty days before the qualifying event 7 to the LEA. However, if an eligible employee learns of the qualifying event less 8 than thirty days in advance of the qualifying event then the eligible employee 9 must give written notice of the qualifying event to the LEA as soon as 10 reasonably possible to receive paid parental leave. 11 (2)(a) Each LEA shall have the requesting employee complete a request 12 for leave form developed by the superintendent prior to the granting of leave. 13 (b) In addition to the request form, an LEA shall require an employee 14 requesting paid parental leave to produce supporting documents of his request, 15 such as a birth certificate or insurance certificate confirming his relationship 16 to the child for whom parental leave is requested or proof of attendance at court 17 proceedings or other mandated meetings related to adoption or foster 18 placement. It shall not be required by an LEA that an employee produce 19 medical records or scientific evidence to prove paternity. 20 (3) Each LEA shall provide written notice to each employee upon hiring 21 and annually thereafter of all of the following: 22 (a) Its policies and procedures, pursuant to this Subsection. 23 (b) The employee's right to parental leave benefits under this Section and 24 the terms under which it may be used. 25 (c) The amount of parental leave benefits available to the employee. 26 (d) The procedure for applying for parental leave. 27 (e) That discrimination and retaliatory actions against an employee, for 28 requesting, applying for, or using parental leave benefits, are prohibited under 29 Subsection B of this Section. 30 (f) That the employee has a right to file a grievance against an LEA for SB NO. 157 ENROLLED 1 violating the provisions of this Section. 2 D. Any eligible employee who believes that his rights under this Section 3 have been violated, restrained, or denied or that he has been discharged or 4 otherwise discriminated against, may within twenty-four months after the 5 violation occurs, or the employee should reasonably have known that the 6 violation occurred, file a grievance pursuant to R.S. 17:100.4. 7 E. Each LEA shall display and maintain a poster in a place accessible to 8 employees at the employer's place of business that contains the information 9 required by this Section in a form approved by the department. However, in the 10 event the LEA does not maintain a physical workplace, or an employee tele- 11 works or performs work through a web-based or app-based platform, 12 notification shall be sent by electronic communication or a posting in the 13 web-based or app-based platform. 14 F.(1) Subject to the availability of funds in a fiscal year, the department 15 shall reimburse an LEA, from the special fund created pursuant to R.S. 16 17:1214.1, that provides paid parental leave in an amount equal to the cost of 17 substitute employees required to fill the position of an eligible employee on 18 parental leave not to exceed six weeks. 19 (2) The department shall use existing communication methods to inform 20 workers and employers regarding the availability of paid parental leave 21 benefits. 22 G. If any provision of this Section or the application thereof is held 23 invalid as to any person or circumstance, such invalidity shall not affect other 24 provisions or applications of this Section which can be given effect without the 25 invalid provisions or applications. 26 §1214.1. Paid Parental Leave For Educators Fund 27 A. There is hereby established in the state treasury, as a special fund, the 28 Paid Parental Leave for Educators Fund, hereinafter referred to in this Section 29 as the "fund". 30 B. After allocation of money to the Bond Security and Redemption Fund SB NO. 157 ENROLLED 1 as provided in Article VII, Section (9)(B) of the Constitution of Louisiana, the 2 treasurer shall deposit into the fund any monies designated for the fund and 3 received by the state treasurer from donations, gifts, grants, appropriations, or 4 any other source of revenue. 5 C. Monies in the fund shall be invested in the same manner as monies in 6 the state general fund. Interest earned on investment of monies in the fund shall 7 be deposited into the fund. All unexpended and unencumbered monies in the 8 fund at the end of the fiscal year shall remain in the fund. 9 D. Monies in the fund shall be appropriated to the Louisiana Department 10 of Education to fund the costs associated with reimbursing a local education 11 agency for substitute employees to fill the position of an eligible employee on 12 paid parental leave pursuant to R.S. 17:1214. 13 * * * 14 §3996. Charter schools; exemptions; requirements 15 * * * 16 B. Notwithstanding any state law, rule, or regulation to the contrary and 17 except as may be otherwise specifically provided for in an approved charter, a 18 charter school established and operated in accordance with the provisions of this 19 Chapter and its approved charter and the school's officers and employees shall be 20 exempt from all statutory mandates or other statutory requirements that are 21 applicable to public schools and to public school officers and employees except for 22 the following laws otherwise applicable to public schools with the same grades: 23 * * * 24 (92) Paid parental leave, R.S. 17:1214. 25 * * * 26 Section 2. R.S. 17:48, 500.2(A)(1)(b), 1202(A)(1)(b), 1206.2(A)(1)(b), and 1211 are 27 hereby repealed. 28 Section 3. The rules and regulations necessary for the implementation of the 29 provisions of this Act shall be adopted and promulgated by November 1, 2026. 30 Section 4. The implementation of the provisions of this Act shall be subject to the SB NO. 157 ENROLLED 1 appropriation of funds by the legislature for this purpose. 2 Section 5. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 3 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 4 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 5 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 6 effective on the day following such approval. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:
