Title: WORKERS' COMPENSATION:  Provides relative to the workers' compensation medical treatment schedule. (8/1/26)
Summary: WORKERS' COMPENSATION:  Provides relative to the workers' compensation medical treatment schedule. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 162 BY SENATOR SEABAUGH 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 23:1203.1(K), relative to the workers' compensation medical 3 treatment schedule; to provide for appeals; to provide for the admissibility of 4 evidence; and to provide for related matters. 5 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 6 Section 1. R.S. 23:1203.1(K) is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 7 §1203.1. Definitions; medical treatment schedule; medical advisory council 8 * * * 9 K. After the issuance of the decision by the medical director or associate 10 medical director of the office, any party who disagrees with the decision may then 11 appeal by filing a "Disputed Claim for Compensation", which is LWC Form 1008, 12 within forty-five days of the date of the issuance of the decision. The decision may 13 be overturned when it is shown, by clear and convincing evidence, the decision of 14 the medical director or associate medical director was not in accordance with the 15 provisions of this Section. The party who filed the appeal shall notify the medical 16 director that he has done so within ten days. The medical director shall compile 17 and forward the entire record used by the medical director in issuing the 18 decision to the district hearing office within fifteen days of receiving the notice. 19 The record shall be made available to the parties upon request. At the hearing 20 on the appeal, if no evidence in addition to the record is introduced, the 21 workers' compensation judge shall either affirm or reverse the decision of the 22 medical director based upon the record that was before the medical director. 23 If additional evidence that was not submitted to the medical director is 24 introduced at the hearing, then the judge shall remand the case to the medical 25 director for consideration of the additional evidence. Following remand, the 26 medical director shall render a new decision after considering the additional ACT No. 95 SB NO. 162 ENROLLED 1 evidence within thirty days of the remand. If the medical director takes no 2 action within thirty days of the remand order, the judge shall decide the appeal 3 based upon the entire record. 4 * * * PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Summary: EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS:  Provides relative to first responders. (gov sig)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 164 BY SENATOR MCMATH AND REPRESENTATIVE CARVER 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 23:1017.1(6) and R.S. 29:739(C) and to enact R.S. 29:723(21), 3 relative to first responders; to expand the definition of "first responder" to include 4 public works employees; to provide for a definition of "public works employee"; to 5 provide for an effective date; and to provide for related matters. 6 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 7 Section 1. R.S. 23:1017.1(6) is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 8 §1017.1. Definitions 9 Unless the context clearly indicates otherwise, the following words and 10 terms, when used in this Part, shall have the following meanings: 11 * * * 12 (6) "First responder" means a volunteer engaged in activities involving the 13 Governor's Office of Homeland Security and Emergency Preparedness pursuant to 14 R.S. 29:721 et seq., and first responders as defined in R.S. 29:723 including but not 15 limited to medical personnel, emergency and medical technicians, volunteer firemen, 16 auxiliary law enforcement officers, state agency essential workers, emergency 17 service dispatchers, emergency response operators, electrical linemen as defined by 18 R.S. 29:723, public works employees as defined in R.S. 29:723, and members of 19 the Civil Air Patrol. 20 * * * 21 Section 2. R.S. 29:739(C) is hereby amended and reenacted and R.S. 29:723(21) is 22 hereby enacted to read as follows: ACT No. 467 SB NO. 164 ENROLLED 1 §723. Definitions 2 As used in this Chapter: 3 * * * 4 (21)(a) "Public works employee" means an employee of a municipal or 5 parish public works department who is responsible for the maintenance and 6 improvement of municipal or parish infrastructure. Public works employees 7 shall be considered first responders only in instances involving emergencies as 8 declared by the governor pursuant to R.S. 29:721 et seq. 9 (b) The term "first responder" may be displayed on the uniform of a 10 public works employee. 11 * * * 12 §739. Intrastate Mutual Aid Compact 13 * * * 14 C. Definitions 15 As used in this Section, "first responder" refers to those individuals who in 16 the early stages of an incident are responsible for the protection and preservation of 17 life, property, evidence, and the environment, including emergency response 18 providers as defined in Section 2 of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, 6 U.S.C. 19 101, as well as emergency management, public health, clinical care, public works, 20 and other skilled support personnel, such as equipment operators, and electrical 21 linemen, and public works employees that provide immediate support services 22 during prevention, response, and recovery operations consistent with Homeland 23 Security Presidential Directive 8. 24 * * * 25 Section 3. This Act shall be known as and may be cited as the "Shelby LaSalle Act". 26 Section 4. The Louisiana State Law Institute is hereby authorized and directed to 27 renumber the definitions in R.S. 29:723 to ensure that the definitions are in alphabetical 28 order. 29 Section 5. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 30 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature SB NO. 164 ENROLLED 1 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 2 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 3 effective on the day following such approval. Title: COMMERCIAL REGULATIONS:  Provides relative to the Self-Service Storage Facility Act. (8/1/26)
Summary: COMMERCIAL REGULATIONS:  Provides relative to the Self-Service Storage Facility Act. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 165 BY SENATOR ABRAHAM 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 9:4757, 4758.1(A)(2) and (B), and 4759(3), (5)(a), and (9) and 3 to enact R.S. 9:4759.1 and 4759.2, relative to self-service storage facilities; to 4 provide relative to the rental agreement; to provide for default of rental agreement 5 by lessee; to provide relative to delivery of notice of privilege; to provide for the sale 6 or other disposition of certain movable property; to provide for definitions and 7 procedures; and to provide for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. R.S. 9:4757, 4758.1(A)(2) and (B), and 4759(3), (5)(a), and (9) are hereby 10 amended and reenacted and R.S. 9:4759.1 and 4759.2 are hereby enacted to read as follows: 11 §4757. Definitions 12 As used in this Part, unless the context clearly requires otherwise: 13 (1) "Default" means the failure by the lessee to perform, on time, any 14 obligation or duty set forth in the rental agreement or pursuant to this Part. 15 (2) "Electronic mail" means an electronic message that is transmitted between 16 two or more telecommunications devices, computers, or electronic devices capable 17 of receiving electronic messages, whether or not the message is converted to printed 18 format after receipt or is viewed upon transmission or stored for later retrieval. 19 "Electronic mail" includes electronic messages that are transmitted through a local, 20 regional, or global computer network. 21 (2)(3) "Electronic mail address" means a destination, commonly expressed 22 as a string of characters, to which electronic mail can be sent or delivered. An 23 "electronic mail address" may include a user name or mailbox and a reference to an 24 Internet domain. ACT No. 468 SB NO. 165 ENROLLED 1 (3)(4) "Last known address" means the mailing address or the electronic mail 2 address provided by the lessee in the latest rental agreement or, subject to any 3 requirements in the rental agreement, the mailing address or the electronic mail 4 address provided by the lessee in a subsequent written notice of a change of address. 5 (4)(5) "Lessee" means a person, his sublessee, successor, or assign, entitled 6 to the use of storage space at a self-service storage facility under a rental agreement, 7 to the exclusion of others. 8 (5)(6) "Owner" means the owner, operator, lessor, or sublessor of a 9 self-service storage facility, his agent, or any other person authorized by him to 10 manage the facility or to receive rent from a lessee under a rental agreement. 11 (6)(7) "Rental agreement" means any agreement or lease, written or oral, 12 entered into between the owner and a lessee, that establishes or modifies the terms, 13 conditions, rules, or any other provisions concerning the use of self-service storage 14 facility. The rental agreement may be delivered and accepted electronically. 15 (7)(8) "Self-service storage facility" means any real property designed and 16 used for the purpose of renting or leasing individual storage space to lessees who are 17 to have access to such for the purpose of storing and removing movable property. No 18 lessee shall use a self-service storage facility for residential purposes. A self-service 19 storage facility shall not be considered as a warehouse subject to the provisions of 20 Title 10 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950; however, if an owner issues any 21 warehouse receipt, bill of lading, or other document of title for the movable property 22 stored, the owner and the lessees shall be subject to the provisions of Title 10 of the 23 Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950 and the provisions of this Part shall not apply. 24 (8)(9) "Verified mail" means any method of mailing that is offered by the 25 United States Postal Service or a private delivery service that provides evidence of 26 mailing. 27 (9)(10) "Wireless telecommunications device" means a cellular telephone, 28 a text messaging device, a personal digital assistant, a stand-alone computer, or any 29 other substantially similar wireless device. 30 * * * SB NO. 165 ENROLLED 1 §4758.1. Notice of privilege 2 A. A lessee shall be notified of the privilege created by this Part before 3 enforcement of the privilege by an owner. Notification of the privilege created by 4 this Part shall be satisfied by either of the following: 5 * * * 6 (2) A written notification of the privilege sent to the lessee by verified mail 7 to the last known address of the lessee, by electronic mail to a primary and secondary 8 email address of the lessee, and by text message to the wireless telecommunications 9 device of the lessee provided the email addresses and number of the wireless 10 telecommunications device are listed in the rental agreement and by electronic mail 11 to the email address provided by the lessee and listed in the rental agreement. 12 B. An owner who does not have a written rental agreement that includes a 13 notice of the privilege created by this Part shall not initiate an enforcement action as 14 provided in this Part until thirty days after the written notice of a privilege is mailed 15 sent to the lessee as required by Paragraph (A)(2) of this Section. 16 * * * 17 §4759. Options of owner upon lessee's default 18 In the event of default by the lessee, the owner of a self-service storage 19 facility has the option to enforce judicially all of his rights under the rental 20 agreement, including, if the agreement so provides, his right to accelerate all rentals 21 that will become due in the future for the full term of the lease or to cancel the lease 22 and enforce his privilege for the debt due him, as follows: 23 * * * 24 (3) The notice shall be delivered in person to the lessee or sent by verified 25 mail to the last known address of the lessee, by electronic mail to a primary and 26 secondary email address of the lessee, and by text message to a wireless 27 telecommunications device provided the email addresses and number of the wireless 28 telecommunications device are listed by the lessee in the rental agreement and by 29 electronic mail to the email address provided by the lessee and listed in the 30 rental agreement. SB NO. 165 ENROLLED 1 * * * 2 (5)(a) Actual receipt of the notice made pursuant to this Section shall not be 3 required. At least ten days after its mailing, or at least ten days after the date that 4 payment is demanded, whichever is later, an advertisement of the sale or other 5 disposition of movable property subject to the privilege shall on at least one occasion 6 be published in a newspaper of general circulation where the self-service storage 7 facility is located or on a publicly accessible website that regularly advertises or 8 conducts personal property auctions or sales. 9 * * * 10 (9) If the property upon which the lien is claimed is a motor vehicle, 11 watercraft, or trailer, and rent and other charges remain unpaid for sixty days and the 12 lessee is in default for sixty days, the owner may have the property towed in lieu 13 of foreclosing on the lien. If a motor vehicle, watercraft, or trailer is towed pursuant 14 to the provisions of this Paragraph, the owner shall not be liable for the motor 15 vehicle, watercraft, or trailer or for any damages to the motor vehicle, watercraft, or 16 trailer once the tower takes possession of the property. Any tower shall be licensed 17 pursuant to the Louisiana Towing and Storage Act, R.S. 32:1711 et seq. 18 * * * 19 §4759.1. Termination of rental agreement 20 A. An owner may terminate a rental agreement in accordance with the 21 provisions of the rental agreement or as authorized by law by delivering to the 22 lessee a written notice of termination. The notice shall include all of the 23 following information: 24 (1) The lessee shall have fifteen days after the date of delivery of the 25 notice as provided by this Subsection to remove all personal property from the 26 self-service storage facility. 27 (2) The owner may limit access to the self-service storage facility to the 28 owner's posted business hours. 29 (3) Failure of the lessee to remove all personal property as provided by 30 this Subsection shall constitute a default of the rental agreement by the lessee. SB NO. 165 ENROLLED 1 B. Upon default of the lessee as provided in Subsection A of this Section, 2 the owner may assert a lien privilege on any personal property remaining at the 3 self-service storage facility and may enforce the lien by sale or public auction 4 in accordance with this Part, except the requirement to advertise in accordance 5 with this Part shall not apply. 6 §4759.2. Nonrenewal of rental agreement; abandonment 7 A. If the owner provides written notice to the lessee that the rental 8 agreement shall not be renewed upon expiration of its term, the lessee shall 9 remove all personal property from the self-service storage facility on or before 10 the date of expiration of the rental agreement. 11 B. Any personal property remaining at the self-service storage facility 12 fifteen days after expiration of the rental agreement shall be deemed 13 abandoned. Upon determination that the storage space is abandoned, the owner 14 shall have the right to dispose of the personal property without further notice 15 to the lessee. Guilty of second degree murder. Guilty of manslaughter. Not
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 166 Original 2026 Regular Session Seabaugh Present law provides for the following responsive verdicts when the indictment charges the following offenses: (1) First Degree Murder: Guilty. Guilty of second degree murder. Guilty of manslaughter. Not guilty. (2) Second Degree Murder: Guilty. Guilty of manslaughter. Guilty of negligent homicide. Not guilty. (3) Manslaughter: Guilty. Guilty of negligent homicide. Not guilty. (4) Negligent homicide: Guilty. Not guilty. Proposed law, in addition to the current responsive verdicts, adds the following responsive verdicts to the following offenses. (1) First Degree Murder: Guilty of attempted first degree murder. Guilty of attempted second degree murder. Guilty of attempted manslaughter. (2) Second Degree Murder: Guilty of attempted second degree murder. Guilty of attempted manslaughter. (3) Manslaughter: Guilty of attempted manslaughter. Proposed law further removes the responsive verdict of "guilty of negligent homicide" from the offenses of second degree murder and manslaughter. Proposed law repeals the responsive verdict provisions for the offense of negligent homicide. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends C.Cr.P. Art. 814(A)(1), (3), and (5); repeals C.Cr.P. Art. 814(A)(7))

Title: CIVIL PROCEDURE:  Provides relative to fees for service of process. (8/1/26)
Summary: , which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 167 Original 2026 Regular Session Seabaugh Present law (C.C.P. Art. 1201) provides that citation and service thereof are essential in all civil actions except summary and executory proceedings, divorce actions under Civil Code Article 102, and pr
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 167 Original 2026 Regular Session Seabaugh Present law (C.C.P. Art. 1201) provides that citation and service thereof are essential in all civil actions except summary and executory proceedings, divorce actions under Civil Code Article 102, and proceedings under the Children's Code, and provides that without them all proceedings are absolutely null. Present law (C.C.P. Art. 1201(C)) provides that service of the citation shall be requested on all named defendants within 90 days of commencement of the action. Present law provides that when a supplemental or amended petition is filed naming any additional defendant, service of citation shall be requested within 90 days of its filing, and the additional defendant shall be served with the original petition and the supplemental or amended petition. Present law provides that the requirement provided by present law (C.C.P. Art. 1201(C)) shall be expressly waived by a defendant unless the defendant files, in accordance with the provisions of C.C.P. Art. 928, a declinatory exception of insufficiency of service of process specifically alleging the failure to timely request service of citation or a contradictory motion in accordance with C.C.P. Art. 1672(C). Present law (C.C.P. Art. 1201(D)) provides that, if not waived, a request for service of citation upon the defendant shall be considered timely if requested on the defendant within the time period provided by present law (C.C.P. Art. 1201), notwithstanding insufficient or erroneous service. Proposed law retains present law. Proposed law provides that service of citation shall not issue until the party who requests service first deposits with the clerk of court a sum of money sufficient to pay all fees for service of citation within the 90 day period set forth for the request of service of citation pursuant to present law. Present law (C.C.P. Art. 1672(C)) provides that a judgment dismissing an action without prejudice shall be rendered as to a person named as a defendant for whom service has not been requested within the time prescribed by C.C.P. Art. 1201(C) or C.C.P. Art. 3955 upon the sustaining of a declinatory exception filed by such defendant, or upon contradictory motion of any other party, unless good cause is shown why service could not be requested, in which case the court may order that service be effected within a specified time. Proposed law provides that a judgment dismissing an action without prejudice shall be rendered as to a person named as a defendant for whom service has not been requested or for whom all fees for service of citation have not been paid within the time prescribed by proposed law C.C.P. Art. 1201 or 3955 upon the sustaining of a declinatory exception filed by such defendant, or upon contradictory motion of any other party, unless good cause is shown why service could not be requested or all fees for service of citation have not been paid, in which case the court may order that service be effected and all fees for service of citation paid within a specified time. Proposed law provides that the provisions of proposed law shall have prospective application only. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends C.C.P. Art. 1672(C); adds C.C.P. Art. 1201(E))

Title: BEHAVIORAL HEALTH:  Provides relative to the Addictive Disorder Regulatory Authority. (2/3CA7s2.1(A)) (8/1/26) (OR INCREASE SG EX See Note)
Summary: BEHAVIORAL HEALTH:  Provides relative to the Addictive Disorder Regulatory Authority. (2/3CA7s2.1(A)) (8/1/26) (OR INCREASE SG EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 192 168 SLS 26RS STINE Author: Subject: Department of Health/Addictive Disorder Regulatory Auth. Addictive Disorder Regulatory Authority March 17, 2026 3:57 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Anthony Shamis Proposed law modifies present law to add definitions to the Addictive Disorders Practice Act for “behavioral Health condition”, “licensed mental health professional”, “peer support”, “peer support specialist”, “peer support specialist associate”, and “Peer support supervisor.” Proposed law modifies present law to designate the Addictive Disorder Regulatory Authority (ADRA) to be the single authority issuing credentials for substance use disorder counseling, prevention, and peer support professionals in Louisiana. Proposed law increases the number of voting members on the ADRA board of directors from eight members to nine, adding a representative from the LDH Office of Behavioral Health (OBH). Proposed law establishes qualifications, scope of practice, initial application, and renewal requirements to be recognized by ADRA as a peer support specialist, peer support specialist associate, and peer support supervisor. Proposed law establishes an application fee of not less than $50 and not more than $300, an examination fee of not less than $100 and not more than $300, and a renewal fee of not less than $100 and not more than $300 for a peer support specialist, associate, or supervisor. Effective August 1, 2026. Proposed law is anticipated to increase SGR expenditures in the Addictive Disorder Regulatory Authority (ADRA) by an indeterminable amount in FY 27 and subsequent fiscal years. The increase is associated with designating the ADRA as the single authority for issuing credentials for substance use disorder counseling, prevention, and peer support professionals in Louisiana. The ADRA estimates that implementation will require the addition of one (1) part time position, along with associated operating expenses and equipment costs, to regulate and issue credentials and renewals to peer support professionals. In addition, implementation is expected to require one-time expenditures in FY 27 for attorney fees, promulgation costs, and equipment necessary for licensing and regulation. Information provided by the Office of Behavioral health (OBH) indicates that there will be no impact to expenditures as a result of adding a representative from OBH to the board. ADRA estimates that SGR collections will be sufficient to offset projected expenditure increases. Note: The LFO is unable to corroborate the estimated staffing level, operating expenses, and equipment costs projected by the ADRA. The LFO has requested the ADRA to provide detailed information on the assumptions and rationale used to estimate the additional position needed to implement the requirements of this legislation. To the extent the required staffing levels may be lower or higher, corresponding operating costs would shift accordingly. To the extent that a portion or all of the prescribed duties can be absorbed by existing staff and resources, a portion of the projected costs may be mitigated. Should additional information regarding estimated expenditures become available, this fiscal note will be updated. Proposed law is anticipated to increase SGR revenues collected by the ADRA by an indeterminable amount in FY 27 and subsequent fiscal years associated with additional fees authorized by this measure. According to the ADRA, the LDH’s Office of Behavioral Health (OBH) reports that between 510 and 529 peer support specialists are currently registered with the department. The ADRA estimates that revenues from these additional fees could generate up to $65,000 in FY 27 from the transition of peer support professionals currently registered through OBH, as well as any new applications received during the first year. The fee amounts are subject to approval by the ADRA board and are not quantifiable until implementation. The ADRA notes that, without direct knowledge of application and renewal trends, it is difficult to project revenue growth in the out-years. However, peer support services are expected to grow minimally beginning in FY 28. ADRA estimates that fee collections will be sufficient to offset projected expenditure increases. Note: Actual fees collected by the ADRA are unknown at this time. The LFO is unable to corroborate any revenue collections associated with additional fees. Actual revenues will depend on the fees set by ADRA, the transition of existing peer support professionals from OBH, and the number of new individuals who choose to register with ADRA. Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 171 BY SENATOR PRESSLY 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 38:5.1(A), 20(A) and (B), 84(A) and (B), 2351(A) and (B), 2352, 3 2353(B), 2354(A)(1), (B)(1) and (4), (D)(1), and (E)(1), 2355(A)(1) through (4), 4 (B)(6) and (10), and (C), the introductory paragraph of 2356(A), 2356(A)(2), and 5 (B)(2) and (5), and (C), 2358, 2359(B), (D)(2), and (E)(1)(a), and 2360(C) and to 6 repeal R.S. 38:2353(D), relative to public works; to provide for realigning of 7 agencies and responsibilities; to provide for the technical changes relative to the 8 Department of Transportation and Development's members of the authority; to 9 provide for Coastal Protection and Restoration Authority; and to provide for related 10 matters. 11 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 12 Section 1. R.S. 38:5.1(A), 20(A) and (B), 84(A) and (B), 2351(A) and (B), 2352, 13 2353(B), 2354(A)(1), (B)(1) and (4), (D)(1), and (E)(1), 2355(A)(1) through (4), (B)(6) and 14 (10), and (C), the introductory paragraph of 2356(A), 2356(A)(2), and (B)(2) and (5), and 15 (C), 2358, 2359(B), (D)(2), and (E)(1)(a), and 2360(C) are hereby amended and reenacted 16 to read as follows: 17 §5.1. Planning assistance to municipal and other planning agencies; federal grants 18 A. For the purpose of providing planning assistance to municipal, parish, 19 regional and other planning agencies of the state of Louisiana in the solution of their 20 planning problems, the Department of Public Works, State of Louisiana, be and 21 Transportation and Development, is authorized to apply for and accept grants of 22 money from the government of the United States or any federal agencies in 23 connection with such assistance and, to this end, the said department may contract 24 with the United States or such federal agencies for the acceptance of such grants 25 including any requirement for matching said grants in whole or in part. 26 * * * 27 §20. Red River Compact Commission membership ACT No. 358 SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 A. There shall be two members appointed to represent the state of Louisiana 2 on the Red River Compact Commission as required by Act No. 71 of the 1978 3 Regular Session of the Legislature. One member shall be the chief engineer of the 4 office of engineering of the Department of Transportation and Development 5 secretary of the Department of Transportation and Development, or his designee 6 and one member shall be appointed by the governor, subject to Senate confirmation. 7 B. The member of the Red River Compact Commission appointed by the 8 governor to represent the state as provided by Article IX of the Red River Compact 9 contained in Act No. 71 of the 1978 Regular Session of the Legislature shall be 10 entitled to receive a per diem allowance of fifty dollars for each day of a meeting of 11 the administration or any of its committees actually attended by such member, to be 12 paid out of funds available for the purpose by the office of engineering of the 13 Department of Transportation and Development secretary of the Department of 14 Transportation and Development, on his own warrant. In addition, such the 15 appointed member of the administration shall be entitled to be reimbursed for 16 expenses actually incurred in attending meetings of the administration or its 17 committees, or in the transaction of any business of the administration, to be paid out 18 of funds available for that purpose by the office of engineering of the Department of 19 Transportation and Development. 20 * * * 21 §84. Parishes and municipalities authorized to comply with federal flood insurance 22 act 23 A. In order to secure for the citizens of the state of Louisiana the flood 24 insurance coverage provided for by the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968, 42 25 U.S.C. 4001 et seq., all of the parishes and municipalities of the state may adopt, in 26 coordination with the chief resilience officer such Department of Transportation 27 and Development ordinances, rules, and regulations, including zoning and land use 28 regulations, as are necessary to comply with the requirements of said the Act and the 29 regulations adopted pursuant thereto by the Federal Emergency Management 30 Agency. For upland noncoastal areas of the state as defined in R.S. 38:2352, said SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 ordinances, rules, and regulations shall also comply with any statewide floodplain 2 management standards which may be established by the Coordinated Use of 3 Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and Transportation Authority. 4 B. The chief resilience officer Department of Transportation and 5 Development shall cooperate with the Federal Insurance Administrator of the 6 Federal Emergency Management Agency in the planning and carrying out of state 7 participation in the National Flood Insurance Program and shall aid, advise, and 8 cooperate with parishes and municipalities endeavoring to qualify for participation 9 in said program. 10 * * * 11 §2351. Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and 12 Transportation Authority; purpose and intent 13 A. The Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, 14 and Transportation Authority, referred to in this Chapter as the "authority", is hereby 15 established as the single coordinating entity responsible for the prioritization, 16 planning, and funding of projects related to flood control, flood risk reduction, 17 navigation, and water resource floodplain and watershed management in all areas 18 of the state not included within the coastal area as defined in R.S. 49:214.2. 19 B. The legislature declares that it is the public policy of the state to develop 20 and implement, on a comprehensive and coordinated basis, an integrated plan that 21 shall support and integrate the development and implementation of policies and 22 initiatives for flood protection, infrastructure planning, and economic development 23 in the upland noncoastal area. Consistent with this goal, it is the policy of this state 24 to achieve a proper balance between development and conservation and encourage 25 the use of state resources in responsible economic development of the area. The 26 authority shall implement the policy within these areas ensuring the efficient and 27 coordinated management of water resources within its jurisdiction, addressing issues 28 related to water quantity watershed management, flood risk mitigation, floodplain 29 management, navigation, economic development, and recreation. It shall be the 30 policy of the state within these areas that through the comprehensive and coordinated SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 efforts and acting in accordance with the provisions of this Chapter, the authority 2 shall exercise all powers necessary to plan, develop, and implement projects and 3 programs consistent with the legislative intent expressed in this Chapter. 4 * * * 5 §2352. Definitions 6 As used in this Chapter, the following terms shall have the meanings ascribed 7 to them: 8 (1) "Integrated" means the coordinated planning, funding, construction, and 9 operation of flood control, floodplain management, navigation, recreation, water 10 watershed management, and economic development projects to ensure efficient use 11 of resources, minimize conflicts, and promote sustainable development. 12 (2) "Upland Noncoastal area" means all land in the state of Louisiana that 13 is not included within the coastal area as defined in R.S. 49:214.2. 14 (3) "Upland Noncoastal parishes" means all parishes of the state, in whole 15 or in part, that are not included within the coastal area as defined in R.S. 49:214.2. 16 These areas are primarily affected by riverine flooding, watershed management, 17 inland navigation, and flood control infrastructure rather than coastal erosion or sea 18 level rise. 19 §2353. Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and 20 Transportation Authority Board 21 * * * 22 B.(1) The Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, 23 Navigation, and Transportation Authority Board shall consist of no more than sixteen 24 members, selected by the governor, representing the following: 25 (a) The Red River Waterway District, nominated by the commission. 26 (b) The office of multimodal commerce. The commissioner shall serve until, 27 or without, express gubernatorial designation of an alternative. 28 (c) Statewide flood protection. The chief resiliency officer shall serve until, 29 or without, express gubernatorial designation of an alternative. 30 (d) Louisiana Economic Development. The secretary of the department shall SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 serve until, or without, express gubernatorial designation of an alternative. 2 (e) The North Louisiana Economic Partnership. The executive director shall 3 serve until, or without, express gubernatorial designation of an alternative. 4 (f) The Department of Culture, Recreation and Tourism. The lieutenant 5 governor shall serve until, or without, express gubernatorial designation of an 6 alternative. 7 (g) Ports, located within the upland parishes, whole or in part. 8 (h) A levee board located within the upland parishes, whole or in part. 9 (i) Duly elected members of the executive branch of local government 10 located within the upland parishes, whole or in part. 11 (j) The Department of Agriculture and Forestry. The commissioner shall 12 serve until, or without, express gubernatorial designation of an alternative. 13 (k) Industry operating and located within the upland parishes, whole or in 14 part. The executive directors of the Red River Valley Association and Ouachita 15 River Valley Association, shall serve until, or without, express gubernatorial 16 designation of an alternative. 17 (l) The Tensas Basin Levee District, nominated by the Tensas Basin Levee 18 District. 19 (2) The board shall not consist of more than two port directors and no more 20 than two levee boards. 21 B.(1) The Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, 22 Navigation, and Transportation Authority Board also known as 23 "C.U.R.R.E.N.T." shall consist of sixteen members as follows: 24 (a) The lieutenant governor, or his designee, shall serve as representative 25 of the Department of Culture, Recreation and Tourism. 26 (b) The commissioner of the Department of Agriculture and Forestry, or 27 his designee. 28 (c) A member appointed by the governor who shall serve as chairman of 29 the board. 30 (d) The executive director of the Coastal Protection and Restoration SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 Authority Board pursuant to R.S. 49:214.5.1, or the gubernatorial designee for 2 this appointee shall be a nonvoting member. 3 (e) The commissioner of the office of multimodal commerce, Department 4 of Transportation and Development. 5 (f) Statewide flood protection chief resiliency officer. 6 (g) A duly elected member of the executive branch of local government 7 located within the noncoastal parishes, whole or in part. 8 (h) Two members from levee districts located within the noncoastal 9 parishes, whole or in part. 10 (i) A member nominated from Tensas Basin Levee District. 11 (j) A member of the Red River Waterway District, nominated by the 12 commission. 13 (k) The executive director of the Red River Valley Association. 14 (l) The executive director of Ouachita River Valley Association. 15 (m)The executive director of the North Louisiana Economic Partnership. 16 (n) Two members from ports located within the noncoastal parishes, 17 whole or in part. 18 (2) The members appointed pursuant to Paragraph (1)(c) through (n) of 19 this Subsection shall be appointed by the governor and serve as members of the 20 board unless express gubernatorial designation of an alternative and shall be 21 subject to Senate confirmation. 22 (3) The board shall consist of no more than two representatives from 23 ports and no more than three representatives from levee districts. 24 * * * 25 §2354. Master and annual plans; development; priorities 26 A.(1) The board shall, in accordance with the procedures set forth in this 27 Section, develop a master plan and an annual plan for protecting, conserving, 28 enhancing, and developing the area outside the coastal zone area through the 29 construction and management of integrated projects and programs, including 30 privately funded projects or plans, and addressing those activities that significantly SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 affect such projects, all consistent with the legislative intent as expressed in this 2 Chapter, and which plan shall be subject to the approval of the legislature as 3 provided in Subsections B and E of this Section. In addition, the board, in 4 accordance with the procedures set forth in this Section including legislative 5 approval, shall review, revise, and amend the master plan when necessary or, at a 6 minimum, every six years. 7 * * * 8 B. The board shall develop the master and annual plans in accordance with 9 the following procedure: 10 (1) The board shall conduct not less than three public hearings in separate 11 locations in the upland noncoastal parishes for the purpose of receiving comments 12 and recommendations from the public and elected officials. All public hearings must 13 be held at least sixty days prior to the submission of the plans to the legislature. 14 * * * 15 (4) At least seven days prior to each hearing the board shall publish a notice 16 of the hearing in the official journal of each parish within the area of the hearing. The 17 notice of a hearing shall have been published in the official journal of each parish in 18 the upland noncoastal area prior to the final scheduled public hearing. The board 19 may provide for additional public hearings when necessary upon at least three days 20 notice published in the official journal of the parishes in the area of the hearing and 21 written notice to the parish governing authorities. 22 * * * 23 D.(1) Where feasible, the master plan shall include scientific data and other 24 reasons, including but not limited to the social, geographic, economic, engineering, 25 and biological considerations as to why each project or program was selected for 26 inclusion. Specifically, an explanation shall be included as to how each project or 27 program advances the plan objectives with respect to the protection, conservation, 28 enhancement, and development of the upland noncoastal area. 29 * * * 30 E.(1) After adoption by the board, the master plan shall be submitted to the SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 House Committee on Natural Resources and Environment and the Senate Committee 2 on Natural Resources and the House Committee on Transportation, Highways and 3 Public Works and the Senate Committee on Transportation, Highways and Public 4 Works for approval. In addition, the annual plan shall be submitted to the House 5 Committee on Natural Resources and Environment and the Senate Committee on 6 Natural Resources and the House Committee on Transportation, Highways and 7 Public Works and the Senate Committee on Transportation, Highways and Public 8 Works on or before the fifteenth day of the regular legislative session of each year. 9 The committees shall take action on the annual plan on or before June first of each 10 calendar year. 11 * * * 12 §2355. Functions and responsibilities; Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, 13 Economy, Navigation, and Transportation Authority Board 14 A. The board shall: 15 (1) Represent the state's position on policy relative to the protection, 16 conservation, enhancement, and management of the upland noncoastal area of the 17 state through oversight of integrated projects and programs and by addressing 18 activities which could significantly affect integrated projects and programs, all 19 consistent with the legislative intent as expressed in this Title. 20 (2) Develop, coordinate, make reports on, and provide oversight for a 21 comprehensive upland noncoastal area master plan and annual plans, working in 22 conjunction with state agencies, political subdivisions, including flood protection 23 authorities, levee districts, and federal agencies. The master plan shall include a 24 comprehensive strategy addressing the protection, conservation, enhancement, and 25 management of the upland area through the construction and management of 26 integrated projects and programs, all consistent with the legislative intent as 27 expressed in this Chapter. The annual plan shall be developed as the annual 28 implementation of the comprehensive master plan and shall be submitted to the 29 legislature for approval as set forth in R.S. 38:2354. The annual plan shall include 30 a description and status of all projects and programs pertaining to the authority's SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 mission, including privately funded projects or plans, and addressing those activities 2 which significantly affect projects set forth in the plan, all consistent with the 3 legislative intent as expressed in this Title. 4 (3) Submit to the House Committee on Natural Resources and Environment 5 and the Senate Committee on Natural Resources and the House Committee on 6 Transportation, Highways and Public Works and the Senate Committee on 7 Transportation, Highways and Public Works the integrated plans developed pursuant 8 to R.S. 38:2354. Upon approval of the plans by the legislative committees and prior 9 to implementation of the plans, in whole or in part, the plans shall be approved by 10 the legislature as provided in R.S. 38:2354(E). 11 (4) Develop procedures in accordance with the Administrative Procedure Act 12 and take actions against any entity, including political subdivisions, to enforce 13 compliance with the comprehensive master upland noncoastal area plan. Such 14 procedures and actions may include but are not limited to determinations of 15 noncompliance; appeal from such determinations; the taking of administrative 16 action, including the withholding of funds; and civil action, including the seeking of 17 injunctive relief, or any other remedy necessary to ensure compliance with the plan. 18 * * * 19 B. The board may: 20 * * * 21 (6) Approve all requests for programs and projects in the upland noncoastal 22 area, insofar as such requests are for funds to be appropriated from the funds 23 available to the authority. 24 * * * 25 (10) Enter into any agreement with a parish governing authority located 26 wholly or partially within the coastal area but which is not part of a levee district for 27 the construction, operation, maintenance, repair, rehabilitation, or replacement of any 28 upland noncoastal protection, conservation and restoration, hurricane protection, 29 infrastructure, storm damage reduction, integrated coastal protection, or flood control 30 project. The board shall have the power to provide in the agreement for the use and SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 exercise by the parish governing authority of all powers of levee districts or levee 2 and drainage districts. 3 C. Approval by the board shall be required for any request by a state agency 4 or department for any funds to finance research, programs, mitigation, or projects 5 involving integrated protection, including hurricane protection or the conservation 6 and restoration, of upland noncoastal areas. However, this Subsection shall not 7 affect self-generated or dedicated funds. 8 * * * 9 §2356. Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and 10 Transportation Authority 11 A. The Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, 12 and Transportation Authority is hereby created within the office of public works 13 Department of Transportation and Development. The authority shall: 14 * * * 15 (2) Serve as the implementation arm of the Statewide Flood Control Program 16 in R.S. 38:90.1 et seq., and for other functions of the department outside of the 17 coastal area. The authority shall be Be directed by the policy set by the Coordinated 18 Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and Transportation 19 Authority Board. 20 * * * 21 B. * * * 22 * * * 23 (2) The governor may appoint an executive director of the Coordinated Use 24 of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and Transportation Authority. 25 In the event an executive director is not appointed, the director of public works 26 secretary of the Department of Transportation and Development, or his 27 designee, may serve as the executive director of the Coordinated Use of Resources 28 for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and Transportation Authority. 29 * * * 30 (5) The executive director, or in his discretion a subordinate or subordinates, SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 shall administer the programs, projects, and activities approved for funding by the 2 Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and 3 Transportation Authority Board relating to and affecting an integrated upland 4 noncoastal system, including conservation, restoration, creation, and enhancement 5 of upland noncoastal areas, storm damage reduction, flood control, infrastructure, 6 economic development, and navigation in Louisiana's uplands area noncoastal areas 7 as provided by law and as provided in the master and annual plans, and other special 8 programs as may be directed by the board. 9 * * * 10 C. Nothing in this Chapter shall be construed to diminish or impair the 11 constitutional, statutory, or contractual authority of any levee district, drainage 12 district, navigation district, or port commission operating within the upland 13 noncoastal area. The authority shall coordinate with such entities to the greatest 14 extent practicable. 15 * * * 16 §2358. Interaction with Coastal Protection and Restoration Authority 17 The Coastal Protection and Restoration Authority and the Coordinated Use 18 of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and Transportation Authority 19 shall jointly develop integrated flood protection strategies. The Coastal Protection 20 and Restoration Authority shall remain responsible for its functions and 21 responsibilities as defined in R.S. 49:214.5.1 et seq. The Coordinated Use of 22 Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and Transportation Authority shall 23 oversee riverine flood management, inland navigation, and water resource flood 24 control infrastructure within the upland noncoastal area as defined in R.S. 38:2352 25 and as consistent with this Chapter. 26 §2359. Functions and responsibilities; Coordinated Use of Resources for Recreation, 27 Economy, Navigation, and Transportation Authority 28 * * * 29 B. The authority shall implement projects in the upland noncoastal area as 30 approved by the board and consistent with the legislative intent of this Chapter. SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 However, no project shall be undertaken except those included in an annual plan 2 finally approved by the legislature in accordance with the provisions of R.S. 3 38:2354, regardless of the source of funds for the project, except in cases of projects 4 undertaken and financed out of the emergency fund, established in the annual plan. 5 An emergency for which such fund shall be used shall be defined by the Coordinated 6 Use of Resources for Recreation, Economy, Navigation, and Transportation 7 Authority Board and all funds shall be spent only in accord with procedures 8 established by the authority board for such fund. All projects undertaken pursuant 9 to the provisions of this Chapter shall be either funded through the Statewide Flood 10 Program, the state general fund, or other sources of funding available to the 11 authority, including but not limited to direct federal aid, grants, gifts, and other 12 donations received by the state for the purposes of this Chapter. 13 * * * 14 D. The authority may: 15 * * * 16 (2) Acquire by purchase, donation, or otherwise any land rights needed for 17 integrated upland noncoastal area projects and other property required for the 18 operation of the projects that are to be owned and operated by the office or political 19 subdivision of the state; provided, that any property acquired for any project may 20 reserve the minerals to the landowners, whether private or public, in accordance with 21 the provisions of R.S. 31:149. 22 * * * 23 E.(1) The board or the authority shall be authorized to solely utilize the 24 science and technology capacity of Louisiana universities, the water institute, and 25 other institutes within the state to enhance programs, projects, and activities for the 26 following purposes: 27 (a) To identify any uncertainty related to the physical, chemical, geological, 28 biological, or cultural baseline conditions in the upland noncoastal area. 29 * * * 30 §2360. Infrastructure priority program; applications; evaluations SB NO. 171 ENROLLED 1 * * * 2 C. The applications submitted by agencies shall be evaluated by the board 3 which shall hold no less than three public hearings in separate locations within the 4 upland noncoastal area for the purpose of receiving public testimony and comment 5 from requesting authorities and citizens regarding the proposed infrastructure 6 projects. Such hearings may be held at the same time and location as hearings set for 7 public comment on the annual plan. The authority shall prioritize and rank such 8 applications. The authority shall provide its application evaluations to the board. 9 * * * 10 Section 2. R.S. 38:2353(D) is hereby repealed. Title: PLANNING/ZONING:  Provides relative to planning commissions. (8/1/26)
Summary: PLANNING/ZONING:  Provides relative to planning commissions. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 172 BY SENATOR LUNEAU 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 33:113(A) and (B), relative to the development of parishes and municipalities; 3 to provide relative to planning commissions; to provide relative to the administrative 4 procedures for approving plats by planning commissions; to provide relative to 5 exceptions for certain municipalities; to provide relative to certain requirements or 6 restrictions for approval of plats; to provide relative to the waiver of a public hearing 7 in certain circumstances; and to provide for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. R.S. 33:113(A) and (B) are hereby enacted to read as follows: 10 §113. Procedure; legal effect of approval of plat 11 A. A planning commission shall approve or disapprove a plat within sixty 12 days after the submission thereof to it; otherwise such the plat shall be deemed to 13 have been approved, and a certificate to that effect shall be issued by such the 14 commission on demand. The applicant for a commission's approval may, however, 15 waive this requirement and consent to an extension of such the period. The ground 16 of disapproval of any plat shall be stated upon the records of such the commission. 17 Any plat submitted to such the commission shall contain the name and address of a 18 person to whom notice of a hearing shall be sent; and no plat shall be acted on by 19 such the commission without affording a hearing thereon. Notice shall be sent to the 20 said address by certified mail of the time and place of such the hearing not less than 21 five days before the date fixed therefor. A planning commission shall give notice of 22 such the hearings, including the purpose, time, and place, by at least one publication 23 in a newspaper of general circulation in the area surrounding the proposed 24 subdivision, not less than five days prior to the hearing date; provided, however, that 25 in parishes or municipalities with a population in excess of one hundred fifty 26 thousand, the public hearing may be waived by the planning commission or planning 27 authority for subdivisions creating five or less lots not involving the creation of any ACT No. 359 SB NO. 172 ENROLLED 1 new streets, and provided further that the provisions in such the waivers shall be 2 clearly set forth in the official subdivision regulations. Every plat approved by a 3 planning commission shall, by virtue of such the approval, be deemed to be an 4 amendment of or an addition to or a detail of the official plan and a part thereof. 5 Approval of a plat shall not be deemed to constitute or effect an acceptance by the 6 public of any street or other open space shown upon the plat. A planning commission 7 may, from time to time, recommend to the local legislative body amendments to the 8 zoning ordinance or map or additions thereto to conform to such the commission's 9 recommendations for the zoning regulation of the territory comprised within 10 approved subdivisions. 11 B. In the case of a parish planning commission, such the requirements or 12 restrictions shall be stated upon the plat prior to the approval and recording thereof 13 and shall have the same force of law and be enforceable in the same manner and with 14 the same sanctions and penalties and subject to the same power of amendment or 15 repeal as though set out as a part of a zoning ordinance or map. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: CIVIL PROCEDURE:  Provides for the garnishment process for banks. (8/1/26)
Summary: CIVIL PROCEDURE:  Provides for the garnishment process for banks. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 173 BY SENATOR MORRIS 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact Code of Civil Procedure Art. 2415(B) and to enact Code of Civil 3 Procedure Art. 2415(C) and (D), relative to garnishments; to provide for delivery of 4 property or payment of indebtedness to sheriff; to provide relative to financial 5 institutions; and to provide for related matters. 6 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 7 Section 1. Code of Civil Procedure Art. 2415(B) is hereby amended and reenacted 8 and Code of Civil Procedure Art. 2415(C) and (D) are hereby enacted to read as follows: 9 Art. 2415. Delivery of property or payment of indebtedness to sheriff 10 * * * 11 B. Notwithstanding the requirements provided in Paragraph A of this 12 Article, if the garnishee is a federally insured financial institution and admits 13 in its answer that it has in its possession funds on deposit belonging to the 14 judgment debtor, and the garnishee has received no notice of opposition to the 15 garnishment, then, if the funds on deposit are one thousand dollars or less, the 16 garnishee may, without further court order, deliver the funds immediately to 17 the sheriff. Delivery or payment to the sheriff discharges the garnishee's 18 obligation to the judgment debtor to the extent of the delivery or payment to the 19 sheriff. 20 C. Notwithstanding the requirements provided in Paragraph A of this 21 Article, if the garnishee is a federally insured financial institution and admits 22 in its answer that it has in its possession funds on deposit belonging to the 23 judgment debtor, and the garnishee has received no notice of opposition to the 24 garnishment, and if the funds on deposit are greater than one thousand dollars, 25 then the garnishee may, upon waiting a period of thirty days after the filing of 26 its garnishment answer, and without further court order, deliver the funds on 27 deposit to the sheriff. Delivery or payment to the sheriff discharges the ACT No. 250 SB NO. 173 ENROLLED 1 garnishee's obligation to the judgment debtor to the extent of the delivery or 2 payment to the sheriff. 3 D. This Article does not apply to garnishment of wages, salaries, tips reported 4 to the employer, or commissions. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: PUBLIC LANDS:  Authorizes the transfer of certain state property in Caddo Parish. (gov sig) (EN +$775,000 SG RV See Note)
Summary: PUBLIC LANDS:  Authorizes the transfer of certain state property in Caddo Parish. (gov sig) (EN +$775,000 SG RV See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 174 BY SENATOR PRESSLY 1 AN ACT 2 To authorize and provide for the transfer of certain state property; to authorize the transfer 3 of state property in Rapides Parish and Caddo Parish; to provide for the property 4 description; to provide for reservation of mineral rights; to provide terms and 5 conditions; to provide for an effective date; and to provide for related matters. 6 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 7 Section 1. The commissioner of administration is hereby authorized to enter into such 9 agreements, covenants, conditions, and stipulations and to execute such documents as 10 necessary to properly effectuate any conveyance, transfer, assignment, lease, or delivery of 11 title, excluding mineral rights, to the property described in Section 3 of this Act, and as more 12 specifically described in any such agreements entered into and documents executed by and 13 between the parties, in exchange of consideration proportionate to the appraised value of the 14 property. 15 Section 5. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 16 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 17 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 18 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 19 effective on the day following such approval. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 176 Original 2026 Regular Session Allain Present law provides that complaints about prescribed burns must be made to the commissioner, who will investigate such complaints and who possesses the authority to suspend or revoke authorization for the prescribed burn issued to persons found to be in violation of the prescribed burn requirements or rules and regulations. Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 177 Original 2026 Regular Session Allain Present law provides for renewal of automobile policies. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law provides for a standard automobile insurance form. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law provides for proof of a motorcycle endorsement. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law prohibits an insurer from raising premium rates due to an insured's nonfault accident. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law prohibits an insurer from refusing to issue or renew an automobile policy to any person over the age of 65 who is mentally and physically capable of driving an automobile. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law prohibits an insurer from failing to issue or renew an automobile policy solely because the insured is deaf or hard of hearing. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law prohibits an insurer from charging a higher premium rate based solely on an insured's activities as a fireman. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law prohibits an insurer from refusing to issue an automobile policy containing collision or comprehensive coverage on a new vehicle based solely upon a named storm in the Gulf of America. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Present law provides that a motor vehicle insurance policy shall not be construed to allow an insurer to assume or accede to the legal title of a motor vehicle without assuming certain credit obligations of the insured. Proposed law retains present law and makes technical changes. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends R.S. 22:1261, 1281, 1282, 1283(A), 1284(A), 1285, 1288(A), 1289, 1289.1, and 1292(A) and (B))

Title: COURTS:  Provides for the territorial jurisdiction of the Jeanerette City Court. (8/1/26) (OR SEE FISC NOTE LF)
Summary: COURTS:  Provides for the territorial jurisdiction of the Jeanerette City Court. (8/1/26) (OR SEE FISC NOTE LF)
Content: Fiscal Note OFFICE OF LEGISLATIVE AUDITOR Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 251 179 SLS 26RS ALLAIN Author: Subject: City Court of Jeanerette Territorial Jurisdiction March 9, 2026 4:38 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Elle Craft There may be an indeterminable effect on court system expenditures (City Court, JPs, JDC, Parish Clerk) as a result of this measure, but any impacts depend on possible caseload shifts and resulting work demands. This bill extends the City Court’s territorial jurisdiction outside of the city limits. Individual courts could see their expenditures increase (City Court) or decrease (JDC, Parish Clerk, JP) due to a shift in caseloads, but the exact impact, if any, is dependent on the actual volume of cases transferring jurisdiction. The City Court was unable to estimate the specific fiscal impact of these potential shifts. There may be an indeterminable effect on expenditures for entities providing support services to the courts (Marshal and Sheriff), but any impacts depend on possible caseload shifts and resulting work demands. Similar to the courts, entities providing support services to the court may see their expenditures increase (City Marshal) or decrease (Sheriff and Constable) as case responsibilities shift, but the exact impact, if any, is dependent on the actual volume of cases transferring jurisdiction. However, the City Marshal anticipates that he will need to hire two additional deputies to serve approximately 3,200 additional residents (approximately 66% increase in number of residents served). This may increase local fund expenditures by an estimated $190,000 in FY 26-27 ($76,000 for salaries and $114,000 for two equipped vehicles) and $76,000 annually thereafter. Additionally, these two deputies could receive state supplemental pay, increasing state general fund expenditures by $14,400 annually ($7,200 per deputy). There is no anticipated direct material effect on the expenditures of the Secretary of State as a result of this measure. An official from the Secretary of State’s office informed us that the expenditure increase required to update jurisdictional boundaries for election purposes would be minimal and can be absorbed into the department’s existing operating budget. Purpose of Bill: This bill expands the territorial jurisdiction of the City Court of Jeanerette to include specific portions of Iberia Parish. Within these newly added areas outside the city limits, the city court will have concurrent jurisdiction with the local justice of the peace. The provisions of this bill could potentially have an impact on the following entities: (1) City Court of Jeanerette (City Court); (2) Iberia Parish Ward 2 Justice of the Peace (JP) and Constable; (3) 16th Judicial District Court (JDC); (4) Jeanerette City Marshal (City Marshal); (5) Iberia Parish Sheriff (Sheriff); (6) Iberia Parish Clerk of Court (Parish Clerk) and (7) Secretary of State. There may be an indeterminable effect on governmental revenues, as this bill may cause transfers of court activities and revenues from parish entities to city entities, but any impacts depend on possible caseload shifts. Activities and associated revenues (e.g. civil fees) may increase for the City Court and City Marshal. Conversely, revenues for the JDC, Parish Clerk, Sheriff, JP, and Constable may decrease. Specific dollar impacts could not be estimated as the exact shift in future cases is unknown. Title: TAX EXEMPTIONS:  Authorizes the surviving spouse of a deceased veteran with a service connected disability who receives an expanded property tax exemption to transfer the exemption under certain circumstances. (2/3-CA13s1(A)) (1/1/27) (EN SEE FISC NOTE LF RV See Note)
Summary: TAX EXEMPTIONS:  Authorizes the surviving spouse of a deceased veteran with a service connected disability who receives an expanded property tax exemption to transfer the exemption under certain circumstances. (2/3-CA13s1(A)) (1/1/27) (EN SEE FISC NOTE LF RV See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 180 BY SENATOR FOIL 1 A JOINT RESOLUTION 2 Proposing to amend Article VII, Section 21(K)(1) of the Constitution of Louisiana, relative 3 to ad valorem tax exemptions; to provide relative to the ad valorem tax exemption 4 for certain disabled veterans and their surviving spouses; to allow for the transfer of 5 certain exemptions by a surviving spouse; to provide for limitations; to provide for 6 effectiveness; and to specify an election for submission of the proposition to electors 7 and provide a ballot proposition. 8 Section 1. Be it resolved by the Legislature of Louisiana, two-thirds of the members 9 elected to each house concurring, that there shall be submitted to the electors of the state, for 10 their approval or rejection in the manner provided by law, a proposal to amend Article VII, 11 Section 21(K)(1) of the Constitution of Louisiana, to read as follows: 12 §21. Other Property Exemptions 13 Section 21. In addition to the homestead exemption provided for in Section 14 20 of this Article, the following property and no other shall be exempt from ad 15 valorem taxation: 16 * * * 17 (K)(1)(a) In addition to the homestead exemption authorized pursuant to the 18 provisions of Section 20 of this Article, which applies to the first seven thousand five 19 hundred dollars of the assessed valuation of property, the next two thousand five 20 hundred dollars of the assessed valuation of property receiving the homestead 21 exemption that is owned and occupied by a veteran with a service-connected ACT No. 39 SB NO. 180 ENROLLED 1 disability rating of fifty percent or more but less than seventy percent by the United 2 States Department of Veterans Affairs shall be exempt from ad valorem taxation. 3 The surviving spouse of a deceased veteran with a service-connected disability rating 4 of fifty percent or more but less than seventy percent by the United States 5 Department of Veterans Affairs shall be eligible for this exemption if the surviving 6 spouse occupies and remains the owner of the property, whether or not the 7 exemption was in effect on the property prior to the death of the veteran. If property 8 eligible for the exemption provided for in this Subsubparagraph has an assessed 9 value in excess of ten thousand dollars, ad valorem property taxes shall apply to the 10 assessment in excess of ten thousand dollars. 11 (b) In addition to the homestead exemption authorized pursuant to the 12 provisions of Section 20 of this Article, which applies to the first seven thousand five 13 hundred dollars of the assessed valuation of property, the next four thousand five 14 hundred dollars of the assessed valuation of property owned and occupied by a 15 veteran with a service-connected disability rating of seventy percent or more but less 16 than one hundred percent by the United States Department of Veterans Affairs shall 17 be exempt from ad valorem taxation. The surviving spouse of a deceased veteran 18 with a service-connected disability rating of seventy percent or more but less than 19 one hundred percent by the United States Department of Veterans Affairs shall be 20 eligible for this exemption if the surviving spouse occupies and remains the owner 21 of the property, whether or not the exemption was in effect on the property prior to 22 the death of the veteran. If property eligible for the exemption provided for in this 23 Subsubparagraph has an assessed value in excess of twelve thousand dollars, ad 24 valorem property taxes shall apply to the assessment in excess of twelve thousand 25 dollars. 26 (c) In addition to the homestead exemption authorized pursuant to the 27 provisions of Section 20 of this Article, which applies to the first seven thousand five 28 hundred dollars of the assessed valuation of property, the remaining assessed 29 valuation of property receiving the homestead exemption that is owned and occupied 30 by a veteran with a service-connected disability rating of one hundred percent SB NO. 180 ENROLLED 1 unemployability or totally disabled by the United States Department of Veterans 2 Affairs shall be exempt from ad valorem taxation. The surviving spouse of a 3 deceased veteran with a service-connected disability rating of one hundred percent 4 unemployability or totally disabled by the United States Department of Veterans 5 Affairs shall be eligible for this exemption if the surviving spouse occupies and 6 remains the owner of the property, whether or not the exemption was in effect on the 7 property prior to the death of the veteran. 8 (d) Once a surviving spouse of a deceased veteran with a 9 service-connected disability is eligible for and has taken the exemption, if the 10 surviving spouse then acquires a different property which qualifies for the 11 homestead exemption, the surviving spouse shall be entitled to a one-time 12 transfer of the exemption to that subsequent homestead, the exemption being 13 limited in value to the amount of the exemption claimed on the prior homestead 14 in the last year for which the exemption was claimed. The assessor may require 15 the submission of certain information concerning the amount of the exemption 16 on the prior homestead for purposes of determining the extent of the exemption 17 available for the subsequent homestead. 18 * * * 19 Section 2. Be it further resolved that the provisions of this amendment shall become 20 effective January 1, 2027, and shall apply to all taxable years beginning on January 1, 2027. 21 Section 3. Be it further resolved that this proposed amendment shall be submitted to 22 the electors of the state of Louisiana at the statewide election to be held on November 3, 23 2026. 24 Section 4. Be it further resolved that on the official ballot to be used at said election 25 there shall be printed a proposition, upon which the electors of the state shall be permitted 26 to vote YES or NO, to amend the Constitution of Louisiana, which proposition shall read as 27 follows: 28 Do you support an amendment to allow the surviving spouse of a deceased 29 veteran with a service-connected disability, who receives the additional 30 property tax exemption, to make a one-time transfer of the additional SB NO. 180 ENROLLED 1 property tax exemption to a subsequent qualifying property? 2 (Effective January 1, 2027) 3 (Amends Article VII, Section 21(K)(1)) PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES

Title: FUNDS/FUNDING:  Provides for the creation, transfer, dedication, deposit, and use of certain funds. (gov sig) (OR SG EX See Note)
Summary: FUNDS/FUNDING:  Provides for the creation, transfer, dedication, deposit, and use of certain funds. (gov sig) (OR SG EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 302 183 SLS 26RS WOMACK Author: Subject: Statewide/Treasury Reserve Fund March 6, 2026 8:50 AM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Kimberly Fruge Proposed law creates the Reserve Fund as a special fund in the state treasury and provides that any money transferred, donated, or appropriated to the fund by the legislature shall be deposited into the fund. Proposed law provides that the monies in the fund shall remain in the fund at the end of the fiscal year and interest earned on the investment of monies in the fund shall be credited to the fund. Proposed law provides that monies in the fund can only be withdrawn subject to an appropriation by the legislature. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental expenditures as a result of this measure. Proposed law creates the Reserve Fund, but does not provide a recurring source of revenue nor stipulate allowable uses of the monies deposited into the fund. Note: Treasury requires certain resources to create and administer a statutory dedication, as in this measure. Should aggregate session action result in the creation of funds beyond that which can be absorbed within existing resources, additional funding may be required, which is assumed to be SGR in this fiscal note. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. Title: SEX OFFENSES:  Provides that nondisclosure agreements involving victims of child sexual abuse are against public policy and unenforceable. (gov sig) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: SEX OFFENSES:  Provides that nondisclosure agreements involving victims of child sexual abuse are against public policy and unenforceable. (gov sig) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 185 BY SENATOR PRESSLY AND REPRESENTATIVES WILFORD CARTER, CHASSION, CHENEVERT, GLORIOSO, LARVADAIN, MELERINE, SCHLEGEL AND TAYLOR 1 AN ACT 2 To enact Chapter 1-D of Code Title IV of Title 9 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, 3 to be comprised of R.S. 9:2720.21 through 2720.23, relative to requisites for a valid 4 agreement; to prohibit nondisclosure agreements involving victims of child sexual 5 abuse; to provide for terms and definitions; to provide relative to enforcement of 6 nondisclosure agreements in certain circumstances; to provide for retroactive 7 application; to provide for an effective date; and to provide for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. Chapter 1-D of Code Title IV of Title 9 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes 10 of 1950, comprised of R.S. 9:2720.21 through 2720.23 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 11 CHAPTER 1-D. TERMINATING RESTRICTIVE ENFORCEMENT 12 OF YOUTH SETTLEMENTS LAW 13 §2720.21. Short title 14 This Chapter shall be known and may be cited as the "Terminating 15 Restrictive Enforcement of Youth Settlements Law'' or "TREY'S Law''. 16 §2720.22. Definitions 17 As used in this Chapter, the following terms have the following 18 meanings: 19 (1) "Minor" means a person who has not attained eighteen years of age. 20 (2) "Nondisclosure clause" means a provision in a contract or agreement 21 that prohibits one or more parties to the contract or agreement from disclosing 22 conduct or information covered by the terms and conditions of the contract or 23 agreement. 24 (3) "Sexual abuse" means: 25 (a) Any sexual act or conduct constituting a crime under the laws of this 26 state, including a sex offense or sexual offense as defined in R.S. 15:541, ACT No. 251 SB NO. 185 ENROLLED 1 regardless of whether the conduct results in a criminal charge, conviction, 2 adjudication, or sentence. 3 (b) Any sexual act or conduct constituting abuse as defined in Children's 4 Code Article 603, including the aiding or toleration of a minor's involvement in 5 any sexual act or sexual activity. 6 §2720.23. Nondisclosure agreements; victims of child sexual abuse; void and 7 unenforceable 8 A. A nondisclosure clause shall be void and unenforceable as against 9 public policy only to the extent that the nondisclosure clause prohibits the 10 following: 11 (1) A victim or alleged victim of sexual abuse against a minor from 12 disclosing that act of sexual abuse, or facts related to that act of sexual abuse. 13 (2) Any other person from disclosing facts related to sexual abuse against 14 a minor in support of, in furtherance of, or consistent with the right of a victim 15 or alleged victim to disclose under Paragraph (A)(1) of this Section. 16 B. Notwithstanding any provision of law to the contrary, nothing in this 17 Section shall be construed to prohibit a person, including a victim or alleged 18 victim of sexual abuse against a minor, from entering into a contract or 19 agreement that restricts the disclosure of information, including the amount or 20 payment terms of a settlement, by another party to the contract or agreement, 21 including an alleged perpetrator, provided that such restriction does not 22 prevent disclosure protected pursuant to Subsection A of this Section. 23 C. No person may enforce or attempt to enforce a nondisclosure clause 24 provided for in this Section, regardless of the date that the contract or 25 agreement containing the nondisclosure clause was entered into. 26 Section 2. The provisions of this Act shall apply retroactively and prospectively to 27 any nondisclosure clause in a contract or agreement entered into before, on, or after the 28 effective date of this Act. 29 Section 3.
