Title: COURTS:  Provides for the administration of courts in Orleans Parish and the number of court judges. (gov sig) (EN SEE FISC NOTE GF EX See Note)
Summary: COURTS:  Provides for the administration of courts in Orleans Parish and the number of court judges. (gov sig) (EN SEE FISC NOTE GF EX See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 217 BY SENATOR MORRIS 1 2 AN ACT 3 To amend and reenact R.S. 13:1137(B), 1302, 1312(A), (B), (C), and (D)(1), 1335, 1337(A) 4 and (B), 1343(A), 1344(A) and (D), 1347(A)(3), (B), and (C), 1383, 1384, 1595, 5 2151(D), 2151.4(D), 2492(A)(1), (5), and (6), to enact R.S. 13:1312(E) and (F), and 6 2492(A)(8), and to repeal R.S. 13:1304, 1347(D), (F), and (G), 1595.1, and 7 2492(A)(7)(b), relative to courts in Orleans Parish; to provide for the number of 8 court judges and commissioners; to provide for payment of court expenses from the 9 Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund; to provide for an effective date; and to provide 10 for related matters. 11 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 12 Section 1. R.S. 13:1137(B), 1302, 1312(A), (B), (C), and (D)(1), 1335, 1337(A) and 13 (B), 1343(A), 1344(A) and (D), 1347(A)(3), (B), and (C), 1383, 1384, 1595, 2151(D), 14 2151.4(D), 2492(A)(1), (5), and (6) are hereby amended and reenacted and R.S. 13:1312(E) 15 and (F), and 2492(A)(8) are hereby enacted to read as follows: 16 §1137. Civil district court for the Parish of Orleans; jurisdiction 17 * * * 18 B. The court of appeal with jurisdiction over the parish of Orleans has 19 appellate jurisdiction over all cases tried in the city courts of New Orleans where the 20 amount in dispute, value of the movable property involved, or fund to be distributed 21 does not exceed one hundred dollars, exclusive of interest. These appeals shall be 22 tried de novo by a single judge, and without a jury; however, the Civil District Court 23 for the Parish of Orleans may provide by rule that no evidence shall be admitted on 24 the trial de novo which was not offered in the city court unless it is shown to the 25 satisfaction of the court that despite the exercise of reasonable diligence by the party 26 offering it such evidence could not have been produced at the trial in the city court. 27 * * * ACT No. 748 SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 §1302. Criers 2 The judge of each division of the civil district court shall appoint a crier for 3 his division who shall be commissioned by the civil sheriff as a deputy sheriff, and 4 who shall perform such duties as may be provided by law and the rules of the court. 5 When directed by the court, the crier shall administer the oath required by law of all 6 witnesses and jurors. Each crier shall receive a compensation payable by the sheriff 7 of Orleans Parish for service as a bailiff in the same manner as a deputy of 8 similar experience and any such other amount as the court en banc executive 9 committee of the judicial expense fund may fix which in any event shall not be less 10 than three hundred dollars per month, payable out of the judicial expense fund of the 11 parish of Orleans on the warrant of the judge of the division in which the crier 12 serves. In addition to the compensation payable to each crier from any source 13 whatsoever, each crier shall receive additional compensation of one hundred dollars 14 per month payable by the city of New Orleans, plus additional compensation of two 15 hundred dollars per month payable by the city of New Orleans when and if funds are 16 made available therefor. For Fiscal Year 2026-27, the judicial expense fund shall 17 reimburse the sheriff for the full amount of compensation of criers. Thereafter, 18 the reimbursement for the compensation of criers shall be subject to an 19 agreement between the executive committee of the Consolidated Judicial 20 Expense Fund and the sheriff. Nothing in this Section relieves a sheriff of any 21 obligation under applicable law, including R.S. 13:5539(B). 22 * * * 23 §1312. Judicial expense fund; sources, control, and administration 24 A. The clerks of the First and Second City Court shall place all sums 25 collected or received under this Section in a separate account to be designated as the 26 Judicial Expense Fund for the Civil District Court for the Parish of Orleans. The 27 judges, en banc, of the Civil District Court for the Parish of Orleans and the First and 28 Second City Courts of the city of New Orleans The executive committee of the 29 Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund for the parish of Orleans shall have control 30 over the fund and all disbursements made therefrom. They shall cause to be SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 conducted annually an audit of the fund and the books and accounts relating thereto, 2 and shall file the audit with the office of the legislative auditor where it shall be 3 available for public inspection. 4 B. The judges of the Civil District Court for the Parish of Orleans and the 5 First and Second City Courts of the city of New Orleans, sitting en banc, executive 6 committee of the Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund for the parish of Orleans 7 shall have control over the judicial expense fund for the parish of Orleans, accruing 8 and accrued, and to this end shall fix and regulate from time to time the number of 9 deputies and employees of the offices of the clerks of the City Courts and their 10 expenses, and also shall have the power to fix the tariff of costs and charges to be 11 paid for official services in the offices, which are paid into, and constitute the fund; 12 due publication of which tariff, when made, shall be given. They shall have power 13 to determine whether any amounts from the fund shall be devoted to the expense or 14 payment of taking testimony by shorthand and to regulate and provide for the same. 15 The judges are authorized to contribute out of the fund, to the embellishment, 16 maintenance, improvement or repair, or both, of each courtroom, the courthouse 17 itself or its furnishings, or both, and may contribute to any pension, retirement 18 system and group hospitalization plans to which officers and employees paid out of 19 the fund may belong. 20 C. The judges, en banc, executive committee of the Consolidated Judicial 21 Expense Fund for the parish of Orleans may further appoint such secretarial, 22 clerical, research, administrative, or other personnel as they deem necessary to 23 expedite the business and function of the court and fix and pay all or any part of the 24 salaries of such personnel out of the monies in the judicial expense fund. In like 25 manner, the judges, en banc, executive committee of the Consolidated Judicial 26 Expense Fund for the parish of Orleans may utilize the monies in the judicial 27 expense fund to pay all or any part of the cost of establishing or maintaining, or both, 28 a law library for the court, or for buying or maintaining, or both, any type of 29 equipment, supplies, or other items consistent with or germane to the efficient 30 operation of the court. In general, the judicial expense fund is established and may SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 be used for any purpose or purposes connected with, incidental to, or related to the 2 proper administration or function of the said court or the offices of the individual 3 judges and is in addition to any and all other funds, salaries, expenses, or other 4 monies that are now or hereafter provided, authorized, or established by law for any 5 of the aforesaid purposes. 6 D.(1) The judges, en banc, executive committee of the Consolidated 7 Judicial Expense Fund for the parish of Orleans may utilize the monies in the 8 judicial expense fund to pay all or any part of the cost of planning, designing, and 9 constructing a new courthouse for the parish of Orleans, which shall house the 10 following courts and all related support offices: 11 (a) Civil District Court. 12 (b) First and Second City Courts. 13 (c) Juvenile Courts. 14 * * * 15 E. All funds collected pursuant to R.S. 22:822(B)(3) or that would 16 otherwise be deposited in the Judicial Expense Fund for Criminal District Court 17 as provided for in R.S. 13:1381.4 shall instead be deposited into the 18 Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund for Orleans Parish as created pursuant to 19 this Section. 20 F. The executive committee of the Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund 21 for Orleans Parish shall be comprised of the following members: two judges 22 appointed by the civil district court judges; two judges appointed by the 23 criminal district court judges; one judge appointed by the city court judges; and 24 two sitting and two retired judges appointed by the supreme court. The 25 executive committee shall have the authority to govern the fund. 26 §1335. Criminal district court for the parish of Orleans 27 A. There shall be one criminal district court for the parish of Orleans, which 28 shall be composed of twelve nine judges. 29 B.(1) Divisions A, E, and J of the Criminal District Court for the parish 30 of Orleans shall be abolished in accordance with this Section. SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 (2) No qualifying shall be opened for any successor to a judgeship that 2 is abolished pursuant to this Subsection, and no election shall be held for any 3 judgeship abolished pursuant to this Subsection. 4 * * * 5 §1337. Appellate and supervisory jurisdiction; writs 6 A. The Criminal District Court Court of appeal with jurisdiction for the 7 Parish of Orleans shall have appellate jurisdiction of all cases tried before the 8 Municipal Court of New Orleans and the Traffic Court of New Orleans. Appeals 9 from the municipal and traffic courts shall be on the law and the facts and shall be 10 tried upon the records made and the evidence offered in said courts by the judge to 11 whom the appeal shall be allotted. In all cases tried before the judges of the criminal 12 district court in which an appeal does not lie to the supreme court, an appeal shall lie 13 on questions of law and fact to two or more of the judges of the criminal district 14 court, as prescribed by said court. The criminal district court shall adopt rules 15 regulating the manner of taking and hearing and deciding such appeals. 16 B. The Criminal District Court Court of appeal with jurisdiction for the 17 Parish of Orleans shall have general supervisory jurisdiction over the municipal and 18 traffic courts of New Orleans and shall have authority to issue writs of habeas corpus 19 in criminal cases, as well as such other writs and orders as are necessary in aid of the 20 jurisdiction of the court. 21 * * * 22 §1343. Allotment of cases among judges; holding of preliminary examination not 23 ground for recusation 24 A. All cases pending in the criminal district court shall be allotted equally 25 among Sections A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, and L the divisions of the court. 26 Except on Sundays, legal holidays, and legal half-holidays, the allotment of cases 27 shall be made public by classes daily at noon by the clerk or a deputy clerk selected 28 by him, in the presence of the district attorney. The fact the accused was committed 29 for trial at a preliminary examination shall not be grounds for the recusation of the 30 trial judge who held the preliminary examination. SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 * * * 2 §1344. Homicide section; Orleans Parish Criminal District Court; assignment of 3 judges; jurisdiction; transfer of cases; expedited handling of writs and 4 appeals; applicability of section to other district courts 5 A. The judges of the Criminal District Court for the parish of Orleans who 6 are in office on August 15, 2009, may create the homicide section of that court, by 7 rule of court adopted by the judges sitting en banc. The rule may designate not less 8 than one incumbent judge who may be assigned to a homicide section in accordance 9 with the rules of the Criminal District Court for the parish of Orleans. 10 * * * 11 D. Any homicide section, court, or other special division adopted pursuant 12 to R.S. 13:587.4 or other authority shall conform to the extent practicable to the 13 provisions of this Section rules of court provided that the rules and assignments 14 shall be subject to the general supervisory authority of the supreme court 15 pursuant to Article V of the Constitution of Louisiana. 16 * * * 17 §1347. Commissioners of magistrate section 18 A. * * * 19 (3) The judges of the criminal district court, sitting en banc, shall determine 20 the further powers, duties, functions, and policy affecting the offices of 21 commissioner, not inconsistent with the provisions of this Section provided that the 22 rules shall be subject to general supervision of the supreme court pursuant to 23 Article V of the Constitution of Louisiana. Unless otherwise provided by any 24 rule adopted by the court in accordance with this Paragraph, the commissioners 25 shall have the same authority for cases within the jurisdiction of the Orleans 26 Criminal Court as a commissioner pursuant to R.S. 13:713, including the 27 authority to make a report and recommendation to the judges concerning 28 claims for post-conviction relief by the incarcerated. 29 (4) The salaries of commissioners may be optionally supplemented by the 30 city of New Orleans or the Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund for the parish SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 of Orleans, as determined by its executive committee, provided that total 2 compensation in accordance with this Section shall not exceed that for a 3 commissioner pursuant to R.S. 13:713. 4 B. The initial appointments by the governor of the three commissioners 5 created in R.S. 13:1347, Act No. 548 of the 1974 Regular Session of the Louisiana 6 Legislature, are hereby approved, confirmed, ratified, continued, validated, and 7 affirmed and said appointment shall be for a term of six years from the date thereof 8 shall appoint a nominating committee of five members to vet and recommend 9 future commissioners. 10 C. Should there be a vacancy created by the removal, resignation or death of 11 any commissioner of the four commissioners, the judges of the criminal district 12 court sitting en banc shall fill the vacancy by appointment for the unexpired term 13 from a list provided by the nominating committee as provided for in Subsection 14 B of this Section. At the termination of the initial term and or any subsequent terms 15 of a commissioner, the judges of the criminal district court sitting en banc shall 16 appoint successors to the office for like terms from a list provided by the 17 nominating committee as provided for in Subsection B of this Section. All 18 commissioners are subject to removal for any reason for which a judge of the 19 criminal district court may be removed from office. Such removal shall be by order 20 of the judges of the criminal district court sitting en banc, upon recommendation 21 of the nominating committee after notice and hearing, and shall be subject to 22 review by the Supreme Court of Louisiana in the same manner as if the removal 23 was recommended by the Judiciary Commission. Nothing in this Section shall 24 divest the Judiciary Commission of concurrent jurisdiction to recommend 25 discipline of commissioners. No one shall serve in the office of commissioner 26 unless he has practiced law in the state of Louisiana for a period of not less than five 27 years. Commissioners shall be allowed to practice civil law but shall not engage in 28 the practice of criminal law. 29 * * * 30 §1383. Sections; court buildings SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 There shall be ten nine separate sections of Criminal District Court for the 2 Parish of Orleans, designated as Sections A through J L subject to the provisions 3 of R.S. 13:1335 or other applicable law. Each shall be presided over by one of the 4 judges. The judges shall hold court in one building to be provided by the city of New 5 Orleans. 6 §1384. Criminal district courts; office or positions and functions 7 Each criminal district court in the parish of Orleans shall have a position or 8 office of judicial administrator, deputy judicial administrator, and assistants, at least 9 thirteen law clerks, and at least four secretaries and law clerks and secretaries for 10 each section at amounts approved by the executive committee of the 11 Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund for the parish of Orleans, and shall provide 12 for the conduct of the jury commission and sanity hearings, the cost of all of which 13 shall be appropriated in the appropriation bill which provides for judicial expenses 14 and shall be allocated by the Judicial Budgetary Control Council to such court. The 15 amount necessary in excess of the amount appropriated may be charged on the 16 Consolidated Judicial Expense Fund or otherwise, when already required by 17 law, shall be paid by the parish of Orleans. 18 * * * 19 §1595. Judges; criers and stenographers 20 A. In addition to Sections "A", "B", and "C" of the Juvenile Court for the 21 Parish of Orleans, there is hereby created a new section of the court to be designated 22 as Section "D". The additional judge created by this Section shall be known as the 23 judge of Section "D" of the Juvenile Court for the Parish of Orleans. Each judge shall 24 have the right to appoint a crier and stenographer for his own section of the court, 25 who shall perform the same duties and receive the same compensation, payable in 26 the same manner and from the same sources as similar officials in other sections of 27 the court. The judge of Section "D" of the juvenile court shall be elected by the 28 voters of Orleans Parish concurrently with the next election for mayor of the city of 29 New Orleans. 30 B. The judgeship created for Section "D" of the Juvenile Court for the parish SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 of Orleans shall be abolished effective December 31, 2014, at midnight. 2 C.B. The first judgeship becoming vacant by death, resignation, retirement, 3 or removal during the term of office of any of the other Sections after January 1, 4 2027, shall be abolished at midnight of such day. If any incumbent judge is 5 prohibited from serving in office at the commencement of the next term due to 6 the provisions of Article V, Section 23 of the Constitution of Louisiana, that 7 section shall be considered as vacant by retirement solely for the purposes of 8 this Subsection as of the end of that term and no qualifying shall be opened for 9 a successor if that seat is the judgeship that is required to be abolished pursuant 10 to this Subsection at the end of that term of office. 11 D.C.(1) Upon the abolishment of a judgeship as provided for in Subsections 12 Subsection B and or C of this Section, the funding from the city of New Orleans for 13 such judgeship shall be allocated for improved juvenile services within the city of 14 New Orleans. 15 (2) The judges, en banc, may appoint a hearing officer in the same 16 manner as any district court or may appoint a commissioner to perform duties 17 in accordance with the rules of the court as prescribed by the elected judges that 18 are in accordance with the Louisiana Constitution and do not exceed authority 19 otherwise provided for in the law for a hearing officer or commissioner in a 20 district court in the state. Nothing in this Section shall require additional 21 appropriation from the city of New Orleans without its consent. 22 * * * 23 §2151. Sections of first city court; election of judges; presiding judge; jurisdiction 24 * * * 25 D. The civil jurisdiction is concurrent with the district court in cases where 26 the amount in dispute, as defined in Code of Civil Procedure Article 4841(B), or the 27 value of the property involved, does not exceed twenty fifty thousand dollars. 28 * * * 29 §2151.4. Second City Court; judge; clerk; constable; jurisdiction 30 * * * SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 D. The civil jurisdiction is concurrent with the district court in cases where 2 the amount in dispute, as defined in Code of Civil Procedure Article 4841(B), or the 3 value of the property involved, does not exceed twenty-five fifty thousand dollars. 4 * * * 5 §2492. Number of judges; qualifications; election; salary; vacation 6 A. (1)The Municipal and Traffic Court of New Orleans shall consist of eight 7 judgeships through December 31, 2020. Thereafter Until January 1, 2027, it shall 8 consist of seven judgeships. Thereafter it shall consist of five judgeships. 9 * * * 10 (5) Division "H" and the judgeship for Division "H" shall be abolished 11 December 31, 2020, effective at midnight. Divisions "B" and "F" and the 12 judgeships for Divisions "B" and "F" shall be abolished on December 31, 2026, 13 effective at midnight. The other divisions shall remain unchanged as previously 14 designated Divisions "A" through "G". 15 (6) A vacancy in Divisions "A", through "C", "D", "E", and "G" shall be 16 filled by election to that division as provided in this Section. 17 * * * 18 (8) Excluding the judgeships provided for in Paragraph (5) of this 19 Subsection and notwithstanding any other provision of this Subsection, the first 20 judgeship becoming vacant by death, resignation, retirement, or removal of any 21 division after December 31, 2026, shall be abolished at midnight of such day. If 22 any incumbent judge is prohibited from serving in office at the commencement 23 of the next term due to the provisions of Article V, Section 23 of the 24 Constitution of Louisiana, that division shall be considered as vacant by 25 retirement solely for the purposes of this Paragraph as of the end of that term 26 and no qualifying shall be opened for a successor if that seat is the judgeship 27 that is required to be abolished pursuant to this Paragraph. 28 * * * 29 Section 2. R.S. 13:1304, 1347(D), (F), and (G), 1595.1, and 2492(A)(7)(b) are hereby 30 repealed. SB NO. 217 ENROLLED 1 Section 3. Any judge and the related division otherwise abolished pursuant to this 2 Act shall remain in effect only to the end of the current term of office. No provision of this 3 Act shall deprive any judge in office of the ability to serve the entire remainder of his current 4 unexpired term of office. To effectuate the provisions of this Act, no further elections shall 5 be held for any judgeship abolished pursuant to the provisions of this Act, including for any 6 division repealed under either Section 1 or 2 of this Act. 7 Section 4. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 8 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 9 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 10 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 11 effective on the day following such approval. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: ELECTIONS DEPARTMENT:  Provides for certification programs for election officials. (8/1/26) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: ELECTIONS DEPARTMENT:  Provides for certification programs for election officials. (8/1/26) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 218 BY SENATOR TALBOT 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 18:59.4(D)(1), relative to certification programs; to provide for 3 training programs approved by the State Board of Election Supervisors; and to 4 provide for related matters. 5 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 6 Section 1. R.S. 18:59.4(D)(1) is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 7 §59.4. The Louisiana Voter Registration Administrators' Certification Program; 8 requirements; compensation 9 * * * 10 D.(1) The requisite education and training will be provided through courses 11 of the Certified Elections Registration Administrator program administered through 12 Auburn University and the Election Center, a national certification designation, or 13 a substantially equivalent program approved by the State Board of Election 14 Supervisors. Any course taken by a registrar of voters, chief deputy registrar, or 15 confidential assistant shall be a course that is required for the attainment of 16 certification or maintenance of such certification. 17 * * * PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED: ACT No. 254

Title: HEALTH DEPARTMENT:  Creates the office of health and nutrition within the Louisiana Department of Health. (gov sig) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: HEALTH DEPARTMENT:  Creates the office of health and nutrition within the Louisiana Department of Health. (gov sig) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 219 BY SENATOR MCMATH 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 36:251(C)(1), 254(B)(1)(a)(i), 257(A), and 258(A) and (B) and 3 R.S. 46:450.3(A) and to enact R.S. 36:253.1, 258(N), and 258.1, relative to the 4 Louisiana Department of Health; to create the office of health and nutrition within 5 the Louisiana Department of Health; to provide for purposes, duties, and functions 6 of the office; to provide for an executive director of the office; to provide for offices 7 under the direct supervision of the secretary; to provide for an effective date; and to 8 provide for related matters. 9 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 10 Section 1. R.S. 36:251(C)(1), 254(B)(1)(a)(i), 257(A), and 258(A) and (B) are hereby 11 amended and reenacted and R.S. 36:253.1, 258(N), and 258.1 are hereby enacted to read as 12 follows: 13 §251. Louisiana Department of Health; creation; domicile; composition; purpose 14 and functions 15 * * * 16 C.(1) The Louisiana Department of Health shall be composed of the 17 executive office of the secretary, the office of the surgeon general, the office of 18 management and finance, the office of public health, the office of behavioral health, 19 the office for citizens with developmental disabilities, the office of aging and adult 20 services, the office on women's health and community health, the office of health 21 and nutrition, and such other offices as shall be created by law. 22 * * * 23 §253.1. Executive office of the secretary; certain offices 24 A. The provisions of this Section shall apply to the following offices 25 within the Louisiana Department of Health: 26 (1) The executive office of the secretary. 27 (2) The office of health and nutrition. 28 B. The offices shall be under the direct supervision, direction, and ACT No. 368 SB NO. 219 ENROLLED 1 control of the secretary. 2 C. The duties and functions of these offices shall be determined by the 3 secretary and shall be exercised under the direct supervision and control of the 4 secretary. 5 §254. Powers and duties of the secretary of the Louisiana Department of Health 6 * * * 7 B. The secretary shall have authority to: 8 (1)(a) Except as otherwise specifically provided in R.S. 36:801 and 803: 9 (i) Employ, appoint, remove, assign, and promote such personnel as is 10 necessary for the efficient administration of the executive office of the secretary and 11 the office of health and nutrition and the performance of its the offices' powers, 12 duties, functions, and responsibilities and such other personnel, who are not assigned 13 to an office, as may be necessary for the efficient administration of the department 14 and for the performance of the responsibilities, powers, duties, and functions of 15 agencies transferred to it. 16 * * * 17 §257. Assistant secretaries 18 A. Each office within the Louisiana Department of Health, except the 19 executive office of the secretary, the office of management and finance, the office 20 of health and nutrition, and the office of the surgeon general, shall be under the 21 immediate supervision and direction of an assistant secretary, who shall be appointed 22 by the governor with consent of the Senate. Each shall serve at the pleasure of the 23 governor and shall be paid a salary which shall be fixed by the governor, which 24 salary shall not exceed the amount approved for such position by the legislature 25 while in session. 26 * * * 27 §258. Offices; purposes and functions 28 A. The purposes for which the offices of the Louisiana Department of Health, 29 other than the executive office of the secretary and the office of management 30 and finance, are created shall be set forth in this Section. SB NO. 219 ENROLLED 1 B. The office of public health shall perform the functions of the state which 2 relate to the general health of the people of the state, including but not limited to 3 responsibility for the preparation and supervision of the Sanitary Code, local health 4 units, sewage treatment and disposal within the state, physical fitness, supplemental 5 food programs for women, infants, and children, emergency medical services, and 6 related functions affecting the public health. It shall also perform those functions of 7 the state provided by law relating to environmental quality and pollution control 8 which are related to the public health and which are specifically assigned to the 9 department, including but not limited to functions relating to the treatment and 10 disposal of sewage within the state, with the exception of those functions assigned 11 by law to the Department of Environmental Quality including, but not limited to, the 12 licensing of sewage sludge transporters or haulers. 13 * * * 14 N. The office of health and nutrition shall be responsible for leading and 15 coordinating efforts within the Louisiana Department of Health that are 16 intended to promote health and nutrition, healthcare access, preventative 17 healthcare services, chronic disease prevention and management programs, 18 physical fitness, and health outcomes, including supplemental nutrition 19 assistance services and commodity programs for women, infants, and children. 20 §258.1. Office of health and nutrition 21 A. The department shall have an executive director for the office of 22 health and nutrition that reports directly to the secretary. 23 B. The office of health and nutrition shall be responsible for: 24 (1) Leading and coordinating the Governor's Council on Physical Fitness 25 and Sports, the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program for Women, 26 Infants, and Children (WIC), and the Commodity Supplemental Food Program 27 (CSFP). 28 (2) Developing and strengthening policy to increase access to and 29 consumption of healthy foods among recipients of the Supplemental Nutrition 30 Assistance Program (SNAP). SB NO. 219 ENROLLED 1 (3) Leading and coordinating department initiatives that include 2 addressing health and nutrition, healthcare access, preventative healthcare 3 services, and chronic disease prevention and management programs. 4 (4) Coordinating with the office of aging and adult services and the office 5 of public health to provide public education on risk reduction strategies for 6 Alzheimer's and related dementias, including the diseases' links to other chronic 7 diseases. 8 Section 2. R.S. 46:450.3(A) is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 9 §450.3. WIC participants; homeless 10 A. The office of public health health and nutrition within the Louisiana 11 Department of Health shall adopt rules and regulations for the WIC program in 12 accordance with the Administrative Procedure Act to provide nonperishable food 13 items to women and children. Such rules shall provide for the distribution of WIC 14 vouchers, in small quantities on a frequent basis, for food items that do not require 15 refrigeration or storage to eligible WIC participants that have been identified by 16 qualified organizations as being homeless. Such rules shall comply with 17 requirements of federal laws and regulations in order to ensure receipt of federal 18 funding for the program. 19 * * * 20 Section 3. The Louisiana Department of Health is authorized to transfer funding and 21 positions within the department to establish and implement the office of health and nutrition. 22 Section 4. This Act shall become effective on October 1, 2026. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: HEALTH SERVICES:  Provides relative to ambulance providers. (8/1/26) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: HEALTH SERVICES:  Provides relative to ambulance providers. (8/1/26) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 221 BY SENATOR BOUDREAUX AND REPRESENTATIVE CHASSION 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 40:1132, relative to ambulance providers; to provide for definitions; to 3 provide for Medicaid coverage of certain services; to provide for reimbursement 4 rates; to provide for rulemaking; and to provide for related matters. 5 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 6 Section 1. R.S. 40:1132 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 7 §1132. Ambulance provider treatment-in-place services; medical assistance 8 program 9 A. As used in this Section, the following definitions apply unless 10 otherwise indicated: 11 (1) "911 call" means a communication made on behalf of a beneficiary 12 indicating that the beneficiary may need emergency medical services. 13 (2) "Ambulance provider" means an ambulance provider as defined in 14 R.S. 40:1131. For purposes of this Section, "ambulance provider" does not 15 include an air ambulance provider. 16 (3) "Beneficiary" means a person who is entitled to receive covered 17 healthcare services under the terms of the medical assistance program. 18 (4) "Medical assistance program" means the medical assistance program 19 provided for in Title XIX of the Social Security Act as administered by the 20 Louisiana Department of Health. 21 (5) "Qualified medical practitioner" means a licensed physician, nurse 22 practitioner, or physician's assistant. 23 (6) "Treatment-in-place service" means an encounter between an 24 ambulance provider and beneficiary which takes place in response to a 911 call 25 where an assessment or treatment is provided by the ambulance provider to the 26 beneficiary but transport of the beneficiary is not performed. 27 B. Treatment-in-place services, as provided for by this Section, shall be ACT No. 370 SB NO. 221 ENROLLED 1 a covered service under the medical assistance program. 2 C. Initiation and facilitation of treatment-in-place services may be 3 performed by ambulance providers on site to beneficiaries, with no transport, 4 and may include the use of audio and video telecommunication systems that 5 permit real-time communication between the ambulance provider and a 6 qualified medical practitioner. However, audio and video telecommunications 7 between the ambulance provider and a qualified medical practitioner shall not 8 be required in order for the ambulance provider to be reimbursed for 9 treatment-in-place services by the medical assistance program. 10 D. Treatment-in-place services shall only be provided in accordance with 11 the ambulance provider's medical direction and established protocols. Any 12 medical assessment or services rendered by EMS practitioners shall be 13 consistent with the practitioner's scope of practice. 14 E. All treatment-in-place services provided by ambulance providers 15 during the initiation and facilitation of the treatment-in-place encounter shall 16 be covered at the associated basic life support, emergency base rate, or the 17 advanced life support, Level 1 emergency base rate provided for in the 18 Medicaid fee schedule, depending on level of response and services rendered. 19 F. The Louisiana Department of Health shall promulgate rules and 20 regulations as necessary for the implementation of the provisions of this Section. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: BEHAVIORAL HEALTH:  Provides for Medicaid behavioral health services. (gov sig) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: BEHAVIORAL HEALTH:  Provides for Medicaid behavioral health services. (gov sig) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 222 BY SENATOR PRESSLY 1 AN ACT 2 To enact Chapter 2 of Title 28 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, to be comprised 3 of R.S. 28:301 through 308, relative to Medicaid behavioral health services; to 4 provide for administrative requirements; to provide for certain behavioral health 5 telehealth services; to provide for an effective date; and to provide for related 6 matters. 7 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 8 Section 1. Chapter 2 of Title 28 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, comprised 9 of R.S. 28:301 through 308, is hereby enacted to read as follows: 10 CHAPTER 2. MEDICAID BEHAVIORAL HEALTH ADMINISTRATIVE 11 REQUIREMENTS 12 §301. Legislative findings and purpose 13 A. The Legislature finds that administrative duplication, workforce 14 shortages, and regulatory inconsistencies reduce access to behavioral health 15 services in Louisiana. 16 B. The purpose of this Chapter is to eliminate unnecessary 17 administrative barriers, align behavioral health program requirements with 18 federal standards and licensing board supervision requirements, and expand 19 workforce flexibility while maintaining patient safety and quality of care. 20 §302. Definitions 21 As used in this Chapter, the following terms have the following 22 meanings: 23 (1) "Cardiopulmonary resuscitation" or "CPR" means an emergency 24 procedure combining chest compressions and breaths to maintain blood flow 25 and oxygenation during cardiac arrest. 26 (2) "Community psychiatric support and treatment services" or "CPST" 27 services, means CMS-approved Medicaid mental health rehabilitation services ACT No. 476 SB NO. 222 ENROLLED 1 designed to reduce disability from mental illness, restore functional skills of 2 daily living, build natural supports, and achieve identified person-centered 3 goals or objectives through counseling, clinical psycho-education, and ongoing 4 monitoring needs as set forth in an individualized treatment plan. 5 (3) "CMS" means the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services. 6 (4) "Department" means the Louisiana Department of Health. 7 (5) "Psychosocial rehabilitation services" or "PSR" services, means 8 CMS-approved Medicaid mental health rehabilitation services designed to assist 9 the individual with compensating for or eliminating functional deficits and 10 interpersonal or environmental barriers associated with mental illness through 11 skill building and supportive interventions to restore and rehabilitate social and 12 interpersonal skills and daily living skills. 13 §303. Pre-employment reference letters; prohibition 14 A. Notwithstanding any provision of law or administrative rule to the 15 contrary, the Louisiana Department of Health and any managed care entity 16 administering Medicaid behavioral health services shall not require 17 pre-employment reference letters as a condition of provider enrollment, staff 18 credentialing, or the delivery of Medicaid behavioral health services. 19 B. Nothing in this Section shall prohibit verification of employment 20 history, licensure status, education, or required work experience through 21 primary source verification. 22 §304. CPR, first aid, and seizure assessment training; approved programs 23 A. The Louisiana Department of Health and any managed care entity 24 administering Medicaid behavioral health services shall not require CPR, first 25 aid certification, or seizure assessment training for behavioral health staff as a 26 condition of provider enrollment, staff credentialing, or reimbursement, except 27 as provided in Subsection B of this Section. 28 B. The department may require CPR, first aid certification, or seizure 29 assessment training only for staff who provide services in settings where such 30 training is required by federal law, state law, or applicable licensing SB NO. 222 ENROLLED 1 requirements for a residential facility, inpatient facility, or medication 2 administration program. 3 §305. Supervision of licensed staff; alignment 4 A. For purposes of Medicaid behavioral health programs administered 5 by the Louisiana Department of Health, supervision required for staff who are 6 provisionally licensed or otherwise under supervision pursuant to the 7 requirements of a Louisiana licensing board shall be deemed to satisfy clinical 8 supervision requirements when such supervision meets or exceeds applicable 9 program standards. 10 B. The department shall not require duplicative supervision for the same 11 staff member when supervision required by the relevant licensing board 12 overlaps with program requirements. 13 §306. Documentation timeliness; CMS alignment; exceptions 14 A. The Louisiana Department of Health shall revise Medicaid behavioral 15 health provider progress note documentation requirements for CPST and PSR 16 services to align with federal guidance stating that documentation shall be 17 completed during the service or as soon as practicable after the service is 18 provided. 19 B. The department may establish a general progress note documentation 20 timeliness standard. However, reasonable exceptions shall be permitted, 21 including but not limited to a state or federal declared emergency or any other 22 exception as defined by the department. 23 C. When documentation is completed outside the general standard, the 24 provider shall include a late-entry attestation stating the date of entry and the 25 reason for the delay. 26 §307. Nursing requirement; provider equivalency 27 A. When a Medicaid behavioral health provider of CPST or PSR 28 services is required by rule to maintain a nurse on staff or to maintain a written 29 agreement for nursing services solely for the purpose of medication 30 administration, such requirement shall be satisfied by the presence of a licensed SB NO. 222 ENROLLED 1 physician, psychiatrist, advanced practice registered nurse, or physician 2 assistant whose duties shall include medication administration. 3 B. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to authorize any individual 4 to perform acts outside the scope of professional licensure. 5 §308. Medical director; inclusion of physician assistants 6 A. A medical director for a Medical behavioral health provider of CPST 7 or PSR services may be any of the following: 8 (1) A physician. 9 (2) An advanced practice registered nurse. 10 (3) A medical psychologist. 11 (4) A physician assistant. 12 B. Any healthcare provider serving as medical director pursuant to this 13 Section shall satisfy all of the following criteria: 14 (1) Have a current, unrestricted license to practice in this state. 15 (2) Have a minimum of two years of qualifying experience in treating 16 psychiatric disorders. 17 (3) Be practicing pursuant to a collaborative practice agreement or 18 supervision arrangement as required by state law. 19 C. The Louisiana Department of Health shall revise all applicable rules 20 and provider manuals to reflect the eligibility provided for in this Section. 21 Section 2. The Louisiana Department of Health shall promulgate rules in accordance 22 with the Administrative Procedure Act as necessary to implement the provisions of this 23 Chapter. 24 Section 3.(A) The Louisiana Department of Health shall submit to the Centers for 25 Medicare and Medicaid Services, no later than October 1, 2026, any necessary state plan 26 amendment, waiver, or other request to authorize reimbursement for psychosocial 27 rehabilitation services delivered via telehealth. 28 (B) No later than thirty days after submission to the Centers for Medicare and 29 Medicaid Services, the department shall provide written notice to the House Committee on 30 Health and Welfare and the Senate Committee on Health and Welfare. SB NO. 222 ENROLLED 1 (C) If the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services requests additional 2 information or denies the submission, the department shall report such request or denial to 3 the committees within thirty days of the submission. 4 Section 4. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 5 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 6 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 7 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 8 effective on the day following such approval. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Proposed law is violated by a motor carrier when all of the following co
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 223 Original 2026 Regular Session Luneau Proposed law creates the crime of failure to test for alcohol and controlled substances resulting in serious bodily injury or death. Proposed law is violated by a motor carrier when all of the following conditions exist: (1) The motor carrier is required by federal law to test an operator of a commercial motor vehicle for alcohol and controlled substance use. (2) The motor carrier fails to conduct the testing required by federal law. (3) The operator of a commercial motor vehicle who is required to be tested by the motor carrier, but has not been tested, causes an accident while operating the commercial vehicle under the influence of alcohol or under the influence of a controlled substance. (4) The accident results in the serious bodily injury or death of another person. Proposed law defines "commercial motor vehicle", "motor carrier", "operator", and "under the influence of alcohol". Proposed law provides that an offender will be fined not more than $25,000, or imprisoned for not more than one year, or both. Effective August 1, 2026. (Adds R.S. 14:39.3)

Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 226 Original 2026 Regular Session Luneau Present law requires that insurance rates not be excessive inadequate or unfairly discriminatory. Authorizes classification of risks using any criteria but prohibits risk classifications on the basis of race, color, creed, or national origin. Proposed law retains present law and further prohibits risk classification made on the basis of gender. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends R.S. 22:1454(A))

Title: LAW ENFORCEMENT:  Creates a pre-Spectrum Alert action plan. (8/1/26) (OR NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: LAW ENFORCEMENT:  Creates a pre-Spectrum Alert action plan. (8/1/26) (OR NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 6 227 SLS 26RS HARRIS, JIMMY Author: Subject: Department of Public Safety Pre-Spectrum Alert Plan March 18, 2026 1:15 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Katherine Granier Proposed law establishes a “pre�Spectrum Alert action plan” to improve safety and response for individuals with autism spectrum disorder who may wander or go missing. Proposed law requires coordination among the Department of Public Safety and Corrections (DPS) and the Department of Children and Family Services (DCFS) to develop standardized procedures, training, and communication protocols to facilitate early intervention and rapid response. Proposed law provides an effective date of August 1, 2026. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental expenditures as a result of this measure. The Louisiana State Police (LSP) reports that costs associated with developing and implementing the pre�Spectrum Alert action plan, including administrative coordination, personnel training, and minor system or procedural updates, are expected to be minimal and absorbed within existing agency resources. LSP and the Office of Technology Services (OTS) have indicated that the Spectrum Alert will be added to the existing RAVE Alert system, which will not require additional costs under the current contract. Information regarding any additional fiscal impact from the DCFS has been requested but was not received prior to the publication of this fiscal note. This note will be updated once information is provided. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. Proposed law does not create, increase, or modify any fees, taxes, or other revenue sources. REVENUE EXPLANATION EXPENDITURE EXPLANATION Creates a pre-Spectrum Alert action plan. Summary: LOCAL FINANCE:  Constitutional amendment to allow for the use of public funds to replace drinking water utility service lines located on private property. (2/3 - CA13s1(A)) (EN SEE FISC NOTE LF EX)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 228 BY SENATOR DUPLESSIS AND REPRESENTATIVES BOYD, BRASS, BRAUD, BRYANT, CHASSION, FISHER, FREEMAN, GREEN, JACKSON, TRAVIS JOHNSON, JORDAN, KNOX, LAFLEUR, MANDIE LANDRY, TERRY LANDRY, LARVADAIN, LYONS, MARCELLE, MENA, MILLER, MOORE, NEWELL, THOMPSON, WALTERS AND YOUNG 1 A JOINT RESOLUTION 2 Proposing to amend Article VII, Section 14(B) of the Constitution of Louisiana, relative to 3 water utility service lines; to provide for the use of public funds to remove or replace 4 drinking water utility service lines located on property owned by utility customers; 5 to provide relative to identifying, inventorying water utility service lines made of or 6 affected by certain hazardous materials; to specify an election for submission of the 7 proposition to electors and provide a ballot proposition. 8 Section 1. Be it resolved by the Legislature of Louisiana, two-thirds of the members 9 elected to each house concurring, that there shall be submitted to the electors of the state, for 10 their approval or rejection in the manner provided by law, a proposal to amend Article VII, 11 Section 14(B) of the Constitution of Louisiana, to read as follows: 12 §14. Donation, Loan, or Pledge of Public Credit 13 Section 14.(A) * * * 14 (B) Authorized Uses. Nothing in this Section shall prevent (1) the use of 15 public funds for programs of social welfare for the aid and support of the needy; (2) 16 contributions of public funds to pension and insurance programs for the benefit of 17 public employees; (3) the pledge of public funds, credit, property, or things of value 18 for public purposes with respect to the issuance of bonds or other evidences of ACT No. 607 SB NO. 228 ENROLLED 1 indebtedness to meet public obligations as provided by law; (4) the return of 2 property, including mineral rights, to a former owner from whom the property had 3 previously been expropriated, or purchased under threat of expropriation, when the 4 legislature by law declares that the public and necessary purpose which originally 5 supported the expropriation has ceased to exist and orders the return of the property 6 to the former owner under such terms and conditions as specified by the legislature; 7 (5) acquisition of stock by any institution of higher education in exchange for any 8 intellectual property; (6) the donation of abandoned or blighted housing property by 9 the governing authority of a municipality or a parish to a nonprofit organization 10 which is recognized by the Internal Revenue Service as a 501(c)(3) or 501(c)(4) 11 nonprofit organization and which agrees to renovate and maintain such property until 12 conveyance of the property by such organization; (7) the deduction of any tax, 13 interest, penalty, or other charges forming the basis of tax liens on blighted property 14 so that they may be subordinated and waived in favor of any purchaser who is not 15 a member of the immediate family of the blighted property owner or which is not 16 any entity in which the owner has a substantial economic interest, but only in 17 connection with a property renovation plan approved by an administrative hearing 18 officer appointed by the parish or municipal government where the property is 19 located; (8) the deduction of past due taxes, interest, and penalties in favor of an 20 owner of a blighted property, but only when the owner sells the property at less than 21 the appraised value to facilitate the blighted property renovation plan approved by 22 the parish or municipal government and only after the renovation is completed such 23 deduction being canceled, null and void, and to no effect in the event ownership of 24 the property in the future reverts back to the owner or any member of his immediate 25 family; (9) the donation by the state of asphalt which has been removed from state 26 roads and highways to the governing authority of the parish or municipality where 27 the asphalt was removed, or if not needed by such governing authority, then to any 28 other parish or municipal governing authority, but only pursuant to a cooperative 29 endeavor agreement between the state and the governing authority receiving the 30 donated property; (13) the investment 9 of public funds to capitalize a state infrastructure bank and the loan, pledge, or 10 guarantee of public funds by a state infrastructure bank solely for transportation 11 projects; (14) pursuant to a written agreement, the donation of the use of public 12 equipment and personnel by a political subdivision upon request to another political 13 subdivision for an activity or function the requesting political subdivision is 14 authorized to exercise; or (15) a political subdivision from waiving charges for water 15 if the charges are the result of water lost due to damage to the water delivery 16 infrastructure and that damage is not the result of any act or failure to act by the 17 customer being charged for the water; or (16) the use of public funds by a political 18 subdivision for the purpose of identifying, inventorying, removing or replacing 19 drinking water utility service lines made of or affected by materials as specified 20 or prescribed by the Lead and Copper Rule Improvements of the United States 21 Environmental Protection Agency, promulgated October 30, 2024, or 22 subsequent promulgation, on property owned by utility customers. 23 * * * 24 Section 2. Be it further resolved that this proposed amendment shall be submitted to 25 the electors of the state of Louisiana at the statewide election to be held on November 3, 26 2026. 27 Section 3. Be it further resolved that on the official ballot to be used at said election 28 there shall be printed a proposition, upon which the electors of the state shall be permitted 29 to vote YES or NO, to amend the Constitution of Louisiana, which proposition shall read as 30 follows: SB NO. 228 ENROLLED 1 Do you support an amendment to allow for the use of public funds by a 2 political subdivision for the purpose of identifying, inventorying, removing 3 or replacing drinking water utility service lines made of or affected by 4 materials as specified or prescribed by the Lead and Copper Rule 5 Improvements of the United States Environmental Protection Agency, 6 promulgated October 30, 2024, or subsequent promulgation, on property 7 owned by utility customers? 8 (Amends Article VII, Section 14) PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES

Title: EMPLOYMENT:  Constitutional amendment to establish a state livable wage. (2/3-CA13s1(A)) (1/1/27) (OR INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Summary: EMPLOYMENT:  Constitutional amendment to establish a state livable wage. (2/3-CA13s1(A)) (1/1/27) (OR INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 530 230 SLS 26RS BARROW Author: Subject: Louisiana Works Establishes state livable wage rate March 20, 2026 1:44 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Darynn Hoppe Proposed constitutional amendment is anticipated to increase state governmental expenditures by $222,343 in FY 27 as a result of additional salary expenses for 382 employees due to the new minimum wage. The impact in subsequent years is indeterminable. The actual impact of the bill beyond FY 27 will depend on the relationship between the CPI for urban wage earners and clerical workers (commonly referred to as the CPI-W) and standard merit or other wage increases state employees may receive annually. According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics, the CPI-W has averaged 3.1% annual growth over the past 10 years. The bill creates a state minimum wage of $10.25 effective January 1, 2027. Each September, beginning in 2027, the state minimum wage shall be increased, effective the following January 1st, by the rate of inflation for the twelve-month period prior to that September according to the CPI-W or its successor index as calculated by the federal government, rounded to the nearest five cents. Wage Increase Impact According to the State Civil Service Department, as of 1/1/2027, there are 26 classified employees, 121 unclassified employees, and 235 When Actually Employed (WAE) employees anticipated to earn less than $10.25 per hour on 1/1/2027. In order to comply with the first minimum wage tier of $10.25, additional state expenditures of $292,847 in FY 27 (one-half fiscal year implementation), $603,264 in FY 28, $621,362 in FY 29, $640,625 in FY 30, and $660,484 in FY 31. These figures represent the sum of salaries and related benefits (assuming and incorporating an estimated 31.71% retirement rate contribution, corresponding to the FY 27 LASERS contribution rate, and a 3.1% annual growth in accordance with the average CPI-W over the past 10 years). Salaries and wages of classified employees and WAE employees are paid with all means of financing (MOF). EXPENDITURE EXPLANATION CONTINUED ON PAGE TWO Proposed constitutional amendment establishes a state minimum wage of $10.25 per hour beginning January 1, 2027. Each September 30th, beginning in 2027, the state minimum wage rate for the following calendar year shall be increased by the rate of inflation for the prior 12 month period as measured by the change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI). Proposed constitutional amendment does not apply to employees under the age of 16, employees of businesses with annual gross receipts of $300,000 or less for the preceding year (adjusted annually by the CPI), employees who are family members working in their family-owned businesses, and licensed employers of certain individuals with mental or physical disabilities. Tipped workers may be paid half the minimum wage if the employer can demonstrate that tips will cover or exceed the minimum wage gap. To be submitted to the electors at the statewide election to be held on November 3, 2026. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. Boxberger Legislative Fiscal Officer Dual Referral Rules Senate House 13.5.1 >= $100,000 Annual Fiscal Cost {S & H} 6.8(F)(1) >= $100,000 SGF Fiscal Cost {H & S} 13.5.2 >= $500,000 Annual Tax or Fee Change {S & H} 6.8(G) >= $500,000 Tax or Fee Increase or a Net Fee Decrease {S} x x Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 530 230 SLS 26RS BARROW Author: Subject: Louisiana Works Establishes state livable wage rate March 20, 2026 1:44 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Darynn Hoppe EXPENDITURE EXPLANATION CONTINUED FROM PAGE ONE Enforcement of the Minimum Wage The bill does not specify an enforcement mechanism by which the minimum wage rate will be enforced on employers. Therefore, the anticipated costs associated with regulation by a state agency or impact upon the judicial system, if necessary, are indeterminable. State Issued Permits for Employers with Individuals with Mental and Physical Disabilities The bill allows for the possibility of the state to issue permits for employers to pay a wage below the minimum to certain individuals with mental and physical disabilities. Currently, the U.S. Dept. of Labor Wage and Hour Division issues certificates to employers for these particular purposes. The state agency charged with issuing these particular permits is unspecified in the bill. Further, the number of certificates to be issued and whether these costs can be absorbed with existing resources is unknown. Local Wage Impact Costs The bill will increase local governmental expenditures by an indeterminable amount as a result of the newly created state minimum wage that is higher than the current federal minimum wage. To the extent any employees at the local level are paid below the new minimum wage, local governmental expenditures will increase by an amount that is equal to the difference between the employee’s current wage and the minimum wage in proposed law. Public Assistance Programs To the extent the minimum wage increase results in individuals’ earnings exceeding the means-tested threshold for TANF cash assistance programs (FITAP and KCSP) and SNAP, there may be an indeterminable decrease in public assistance enrollment and expenditures. The number of individuals that would be impacted by the increase is unknown. The Department of Children and Family Services (DCFS) administers SNAP. Approximately half of the cost of administering SNAP is funded with SGF. The rest of the administrative costs are funded through a federal match provided by Food and Nutrition Services (FNS). The possible decrease in SNAP enrollment and expenditures could generate a decrease in the costs associated with administering the program. DCFS reports the magnitude of this decrease is unknown, but is unlikely to be significant. Page 2 of 2 CONTINUED EXPLANATION from page one: Alan M. Title: BLIGHTED PROPERTY:  Provides relative to blighted property. (8/1/26)
Summary: , which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 231 Original 2026 Regular Session Barrow Present law (R.S. 40:600.35) provides that each local governmental subdivision maintaining a blighted housing property list shall submit the list to the state housing agency no later than December 31st of
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 231 Original 2026 Regular Session Barrow Present law (R.S. 40:600.35) provides that each local governmental subdivision maintaining a blighted housing property list shall submit the list to the state housing agency no later than December 31st of each year. Further provides that the information submitted to the state housing agency shall be updated quarterly. Proposed law retains present law. Present law provides that the state housing agency has the duty of maintaining the statewide blighted housing property list. The state housing agency shall publish, no later than March 15th of each year, a statewide blighted housing property list based on information received from local governmental subdivisions in accordance with the provisions of present law. Further provides that the list shall also be maintained on the state housing agency's website and shall be updated quarterly. Present law provides that the state housing agency shall utilize a statewide housing locator system for the purpose of maintaining the blighted housing property list. The system shall be updated as needed. Proposed law retains present law but requires the statewide housing locator system to be made available to the general public online through the state housing agency's website. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends R.S. 40:600.35(C))

Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 233 BY SENATOR MIZELL 1 AN ACT 2 To enact Subpart F of Part I of Chapter 1 of Subtitle 1 of Title 39 of the Louisiana Revised 3 Statutes of 1950, to be comprised of R.S. 39:18.1 through 18.3, relative to data 4 exchange; to establish the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact; to provide 5 for authorization and purpose; to provide for definitions; to provide for terms, 6 conditions, administration, and procedure; to provide for an effective date; and to 7 provide for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. Subpart F of Part I of Chapter 1 of Subtitle 1 of Title 39 of the Louisiana 10 Revised Statutes of 1950, comprised of R.S. 39:18.1 through 18.3, is hereby enacted to read 11 as follows: 12 SUBPART F. LOUISIANA STATEWIDE DATA EXCHANGE COMPACT 13 §18.1. Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact; authorization and 14 purpose 15 The office of technology services is authorized to create the Louisiana 16 Statewide Data Exchange Compact pursuant to the provisions of this Subpart. 17 The Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact shall establish a single legal 18 and technical framework for secure, privacy-compliant interagency data 19 sharing between the office of technology services and agencies as defined in this 20 Subpart. The Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact shall facilitate ACT No. 477 SB NO. 233 ENROLLED 1 disclosure of confidential information and describe each participating agency's 2 rights and obligations with respect to confidential information and the limited 3 purposes for which each agency may use or have access to confidential 4 information. 5 §18.2. Definitions 6 As used in this Subpart, the following terms shall have the following 7 meanings: 8 (1) "Agency" means either of the following: 9 (a) A department, board, bureau, commission, office, authority, council, 10 or institution of state government. 11 (b) A state university or college, a community or technical college, or any 12 service or part of a state institution of higher education. 13 (2) "Data exchange" means the secure and structured process of sharing 14 information between agencies, involving transforming data into compatible 15 formats to ensure seamless transfer, interoperability, and usability, allowing 16 agencies to gain insights, improve operations, and foster collaboration. 17 (3) "Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact" means a collective 18 of data sharing and data security agreements entered into between participating 19 agencies and the office of technology services to facilitate an efficient and 20 consistent method of compliance with state and federal laws regarding data 21 sharing and data security. 22 §18.3. Administration of the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact; 23 office of technology services 24 A. The office of technology services shall serve as the Louisiana 25 Statewide Data Exchange Compact administrator and shall develop a template 26 for the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact. 27 B. The office of technology services shall establish a committee of 28 participating agencies that shall advise the office on the Louisiana Statewide 29 Data Exchange Compact and provide input on the establishment of the template 30 of the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact. The committee shall SB NO. 233 ENROLLED 1 review the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact at least biannually to 2 correspond with any changes to state and federal laws and the evolving data 3 security landscape and shall report any recommended changes to the template 4 or any rules adopted pursuant to this Section to the office of technology services. 5 C. The use of the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact by the 6 agency is voluntary and each participating agency at any time may determine 7 that the use of the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact is no longer 8 appropriate for the specific data and may amend its agreement, terminate its 9 participation in the Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact, or pursue an 10 alternate approach to protecting data shared by the agency. 11 D. Each participating agency shall sign the Louisiana Statewide Data 12 Exchange Compact to serve as the primary governance document for the 13 safeguarding of data shared among agencies. 14 E. The Louisiana Statewide Data Exchange Compact shall specify that 15 the receiving agency shall establish, implement, and maintain administrative, 16 physical, and technical safeguards to preserve and maintain the confidentiality, 17 integrity, and availability of the confidential information disclosed to it by 18 another agency. 19 F. The office of technology services shall not acquire any ownership 20 rights or interest in data shared pursuant to the Louisiana Statewide Data 21 Exchange Compact. Each participating agency shall retain the ownership rights 22 of any data shared by the agency pursuant to the Louisiana Statewide Data 23 Exchange Compact. 24 G. The office of technology services shall promulgate rules, in 25 accordance with the Administrative Procedure Act, to implement the provisions 26 of this Subpart. Any proposed rules shall be submitted to the committee for 27 approval by a majority vote of the participating agencies prior to adoption and 28 implementation. 29 Section 2. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 30 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature SB NO. 233 ENROLLED 1 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 2 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 3 effective on the day following such approval. Title: HEALTH CARE:  Provides relative to kidney disease. (8/1/26) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: HEALTH CARE:  Provides relative to kidney disease. (8/1/26) (EN NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 236 BY SENATOR OWEN AND REPRESENTATIVE CHASSION 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 46:450.5.1, relative to kidney disease; to provide for legislative intent; to 3 provide for requirements for the Louisiana Department of Health; to provide for 4 annual reviews; to provide for reporting; and to provide for related matters. 5 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 6 Section 1. R.S. 46:450.5.1 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 7 §450.5.1. Chronic and rare kidney diseases; coverage; annual review 8 A. The legislature hereby finds all of the following: 9 (1) More than thirty-five million Americans currently suffer from kidney 10 disease and millions more may not be aware that they are at risk for developing 11 kidney disease in the future. 12 (2) There is a new era emerging of clinical innovation in kidney disease 13 treatment, with the first-ever treatments approved by the United States Food 14 and Drug Administration to slow the decline of kidney function in patients with 15 chronic and rare kidney diseases. 16 (3) Although the main causes of kidney disease are diabetes and high 17 blood pressure, kidney disease also has genetic and hereditary causes, including 18 rare diseases that cause glomerulonephritis. 19 (4) According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, as 20 many as nine out of ten Americans with chronic kidney disease are unaware 21 they have it. 22 (5) If kidney disease is detected early through blood, urine, or other tests, 23 it may be possible to slow its progression and delay the need for dialysis or 24 transplant through lifestyle changes and pharmacological treatments. 25 (6) Dialysis and later stages of kidney disease are costly to the state and 26 its healthcare system and often result in a patient leaving the workforce. ACT No. 478 SB NO. 236 ENROLLED 1 B. The legislature further finds that, considering the detrimental health 2 effects of kidney disease in this state and the United States, its enormous impact 3 on the lives of patients and their caregivers, and the avoidable costs to the state, 4 its workforce, and the healthcare system of undetected or undertreated kidney 5 disease, it is in the interest of the state to encourage the prevention, early 6 screening, diagnosis, treatment, disparity of impact, and overall awareness and 7 understanding of kidney disease in Louisiana. 8 C.(1) The Louisiana Department of Health shall conduct an annual 9 review of all medications and forms of treatment for chronic and rare kidney 10 diseases and services for enrollees with a diagnosis of chronic or rare kidney 11 disease that are eligible for coverage under Medicaid. The purpose of the annual 12 review is to determine if the available covered medications, treatments, and 13 services are adequate to meet the needs of enrollees with a diagnosis of chronic 14 or rare kidney disease, and whether the department should seek to add 15 additional medications, treatments, or services. 16 (2) When conducting the annual review required by this Subsection, the 17 department shall solicit and consider input from the general public, with 18 specific emphasis on attempting to receive input from persons or groups with 19 knowledge and experience in the area of chronic and rare kidney diseases. 20 D. The department shall examine and pursue strategic partnerships with 21 state-based entities who represent or provide services to individuals living with 22 chronic and rare kidney diseases for the purpose of increasing education, 23 awareness, and diagnostic screening for chronic and rare kidney diseases, 24 through new or existing initiatives including but not limited to Well-Ahead 25 Louisiana. 26 E. On or before July 1, 2027, and on or before July first each year 27 thereafter, the department shall submit a report to the president of the Senate, 28 the speaker of the House of Representatives, the Senate Committee on Health 29 and Welfare, the House Committee on Health and Welfare, and the David R. 30 Poynter Legislative Research Library as required by R.S. 24:771 and 772. The SB NO. 236 ENROLLED 1 report shall detail the department's findings from the annual review required 2 by this Section, as well as any recommendations to the legislature based upon 3 the department's findings. The department shall publish the annual report on 4 its website in a manner accessible by the general public. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: EARLY CHILDHOOD ED:  Provides relative to early learning centers. (gov sig) (OR INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Summary: EARLY CHILDHOOD ED:  Provides relative to early learning centers. (gov sig) (OR INCREASE GF EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 182 240 SLS 26RS BARROW Author: Subject: Education Licensing of early learning centers March 19, 2026 12:48 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Julie Silva Beginning in FY 27, proposed legislation will result in an estimated increase of $5.9 M and require an additional 53 authorized T.O. positions for the Louisiana Department of Education (LDOE) to process licenses, conduct inspections, perform monitoring activities, and verify background check compliance associated with licensing 1,848 public school prekindergarten sites as early learning centers. These positions include 37 Licensing Specialists to conduct initial, compliance, follow-up, and annual inspections; eight (8) Licensing Supervisors to train and supervise Licensing Specialists, review inspection reports, and conduct onsite quality monitoring; and eight (8) Licensing Consultants to review applications for initial, renewal, and change licensing and to provide consultation to schools regarding compliance with Bulletin 137 – Louisiana Early Learning Center Licensing Regulations. The department reports the staffing estimates are based on a caseload of approximately fifty (50) sites per Licensing Specialist, five (5) Licensing Specialists per Licensing Supervisor, and two hundred fifty (250) sites per Licensing Consultant, with additional staffing considerations related to the geographic distribution of early learning sites across the state. Positions Salaries + Related Benefits Per Position Total Licensing Specialists I/II (37) $90,692 $3,355,604 Licensing Supervisor (8) $108,877 $871,016 Licensing Consultant (8) $108,877 $871,016 Total Personal Services $5,097,636 Operating Expenses $ 14,791 $783,923 Total (FY 27) $5,881,559 Proposed legislation is expected to result in additional local expenditures for public prekindergarten programs attached to schools. Actual costs for any single LEA are indeterminable and will depend on administrative and facility adjustments needed to comply with Type IV early learning center licensing and inspection requirements. Continued on page two. Present law requires that all public and nonpublic prekindergarten programs comply with child welfare minimum standards and further requires all nonpublic prekindergarten programs be licensed as early learning centers. Proposed legislation retains present law requirements concerning complicity with child welfare minimum standards and requires that, in addition to all nonpublic prekindergarten programs, all public prekindergarten programs be licensed as early learning centers. Creates a new Type IV license specifically for programs attached to schools, with adjusted inspection and training requirements. The bill clarifies that employees of Type IV centers are subject to background checks and requires that Type IV centers be represented on the Early Childhood Care and Education Commission and the Advisory Council on Early Childhood Care and Education. Clarifies that early learning center background check requirements do not apply to any Type IV early learning center employee who has complied with the criminal history background check and state central registry check requirements needed to be employed by the school to which it is attached. Proposed legislation is anticipated to increase SGR to LDOE from annual licensing fees for public prekindergarten programs, with fees based on enrollment: $25 for 1–15 students, $100 for 16–50, $175 for 51–100, and $250 for 101 or more. LDOE estimates annual increase in revenue of $46,200 from the 1,848 public school prekindergarten sites. However, actual receipts will be dependent on enrollment.
