Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Julie Silva Note: Proposed legislation exempts employees of Type IV early learning centers from Child Care Criminal Background Check (CCCBC) requirements and from Shaken Baby Syndrome (SBS) training, allowing compliance with standard school employment background and state central registry checks instead. The federal Child Care and Development Block Grant Act (45 CFR §98.43(a)(1)) requires all employees of early learning centers licensed by the state to complete CCCBCs, which include fingerprint-based FBI checks, multi-state child abuse and neglect registry searches, and sex offender registry checks, as well as SBS training. Exempting Type IV employees from these requirements creates a risk of federal noncompliance, which could affect early childhood program funding and increase liability for the Louisiana Department of Education (LDOE) and local school districts. Schools serving only pre-K 3 and 4-year-olds may be eligible for licensing exemptions. However, campuses that also operate programs for children under age 3 could face conflicts with LAC 28:CLXI.709, which prohibits multiple licenses issued to a single address. LDOE reports that additional research would be needed to determine whether multiple licenses could be authorized or program adjustments would be required. Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 466 245 SLS 26RS MYERS Author: Subject: Louisiana Department of Health/Office of Behavioral Health Provides relative to treatments for veterans May 3, 2026 3:52 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Anthony Shamis Proposed law requires the Louisiana Department of Health (LDH), Office of Behavioral Health (OBH) to develop a comprehensive plan to prevent veteran suicides, including connecting veterans to ongoing, barrier-free care and utilizing non-traditional, high-impact interventions that can rapidly stabilize post-traumatic stress disorder symptoms. Proposed law requires LDH to submit the plan to the legislature no later than January 1, 2027. Effective August 1, 2026. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental expenditures as a result of this measure. LDH indicates that the costs and resources associated with the development of a comprehensive plan to prevent veteran suicides, including connecting veterans to ongoing barrier-free care and utilizing non-traditional, high-impact interventions that can rapidly stabilize post-traumatic stress disorder, can be absorbed within existing staff and resources. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. REVENUE EXPLANATION EXPENDITURE EXPLANATION Provides relative to treatments for veterans. Further provides for appeals from the utilization review decision. Proposed law retains present law. Present la
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 246 Original 2026 Regular Session Luneau Present law provides procedures for utilization review determinations by healthcare insurers. Further provides for appeals from the utilization review decision. Proposed law retains present law. Present law requires a healthcare insurer to provide written or electronic notice to an insured of an adverse utilization review determination and the reasons for the adverse determination. Proposed law retains present law. Proposed law requires a healthcare insurer to inform an insured if artificial intelligence was used in the determination process. Proposed law defines artificial intelligence. Proposed law provides requirements for the use of artificial intelligence in utilization review. Proposed law provides that artificial intelligence shall not replace the role of a healthcare provider in the utilization review determination. Proposed law requires independent judgment from human utilization review personnel before making an adverse determination for a claim based on medical necessity or a procedure requiring prior authorization. Proposed law requires compliance with applicable regulations and guidance from the United States Department of Health and Human Services. Proposed law requires compliance with the Health Insurance and Portability Act of 1996. Proposed law provides that an insured has a right to appeal an adverse determination made with a recommendation from artificial intelligence. Proposed law forbids the use of artificial intelligence in any subsequent review of an adverse determination that used artificial intelligence. Proposed law allows an insured as part of the appeals process to review and have copies of all documents relevant to any artificial intelligence used in the utilization review or determination process. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends R.S. 22:1260.44(E)(2); adds R.S. 22:1260.49 and 2401(4))

Title: WILDLIFE/FISHERIES DEPT:  Constitutional amendment to add two members to the Louisiana Wildlife and Fisheries Commission. (2/3-CA13s1(A)) (EG SEE FISC NOTE GF EX)
Summary: WILDLIFE/FISHERIES DEPT:  Constitutional amendment to add two members to the Louisiana Wildlife and Fisheries Commission. (2/3-CA13s1(A)) (EG SEE FISC NOTE GF EX)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 623 249 SLS 26RS KLEINPETER Author: Subject: Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries Louisiana Wildlife and Fisheries Commission March 4, 2026 6:44 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Richie Anderson Present constitution provides that the Louisiana Wildlife and Fisheries Commission consists of seven members to be appointed by the governor. Proposed constitutional amendment increases the membership from seven (7) to nine (9) members. Proposed law clarifies that these nine members shall be appointed by the governor, six shall be electors from the state at large, and three members shall be electors of the coastal parishes and representatives of the commercial fishing and fur industries. Proposed constitutional amendment provides that six members shall serve overlapping terms of six years and three members shall serve a term concurrent with that of the governor. Proposed constitutional amendment provides that no member that has served for six or more years shall be eligible for reappointment. To be submitted to the electors at the statewide election to be held on November 3, 2026. In certain circumstances, the Secretary of State may incur minimal ballot processing costs associated with this measure. As a regular practice, the department budgets for up to 10 constitutional amendments and statewide propositions for the fall statewide elections. To the extent the ballot includes more than 10 constitutional amendments and statewide propositions, the Secretary of State may require additional SGF resources for the 11/03/26 statewide election. Any expenditure impact would be realized in FY 27. Note: A companion bill (SB 296) is the enabling legislation. The impact of adding two additional commissioners is contemplated in the fiscal note for SB 296. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. Title: HEALTH SERVICES:  Provides relative to comprehensive weight management services. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SG EX See Note)
Summary: HEALTH SERVICES:  Provides relative to comprehensive weight management services. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SG EX See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 250 BY SENATOR MCMATH AND REPRESENTATIVES BERAULT, CARVER, CHASSION, EGAN, FISHER, JACKSON, MIKE JOHNSON, LYONS, MARTINEZ AND WYBLE 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 42:802(B)(9) and to enact R.S. 42:851(W), 855, and 855.1, 3 relative to comprehensive weight management services; to provide for authority of 4 the Office of Group Benefits; to provide for a comprehensive weight management 5 plan; to provide for covered services; to provide relative to premiums; to provide for 6 notification requirements; and to provide for related matters. 7 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 8 Section 1. R.S. 42:802(B)(9) is hereby amended and reenacted and R.S. 42:851(W), 9 855, and 855.1 are hereby enacted to read as follows: 10 §802. Powers and duties; Office of Group Benefits 11 * * * 12 B. In addition, the office shall have the following powers and duties: 13 * * * 14 (9) To administer or provide for the administration of an Internal Revenue 15 Service qualified cafeteria plan, including any weight management plan, if 16 applicable. The plan shall be developed, negotiated, and implemented by the office 17 subject to approval of the appropriate standing committees of the legislature having 18 jurisdiction over review of agency rules of the Office of Group Benefits as 19 designated by R.S. 49:966(B)(21)(c) or the subcommittee on oversight of such 20 standing committees. The provisions of Chapter 17 of Subtitle III of Title 39 of the 21 Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950 shall not apply. 22 * * * 23 §851. Authority for employee benefit programs; payroll deduction for payment of 24 premiums 25 * * * 26 W. Notwithstanding any provision of law to the contrary, any enrollee ACT No. 749 SB NO. 250 ENROLLED 1 or dependent enrolled in a comprehensive weight management plan offered by 2 the Office of Group Benefits shall pay one hundred percent of the premium for 3 the weight management plan. 4 * * * 5 §855. Office of Group Benefits; comprehensive weight management services 6 A. Beginning January 1, 2027, the Office of Group Benefits shall offer 7 enrollment in a comprehensive weight management plan to plan participants 8 for enrollees and dependents. Any such plan shall include the following 9 comprehensive weight management and metabolic health services: 10 (1) FDA-approved medications. 11 (2) Metabolic health therapies compounded in compliance with 21 U.S.C. 12 353a. 13 (3) Medical evaluations. 14 (4) Prescribing oversight. 15 (5) Follow-up care. 16 (6) Related support services. 17 (7) Longitudinal metabolic health outcome measurements. 18 B. Enrollment shall be voluntary and shall not be a condition of 19 enrollment in any health benefit plan. 20 C. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to require any health 21 benefit plan administered by the Office of Group Benefits to alter existing 22 medical necessity standards, utilization management protocols, or formulary 23 requirements. 24 §855.1. Weight loss medications; notification to enrollees 25 A. No later than October 1, 2026, the Office of Group Benefits shall 26 notify all enrollees of available options for direct purchasing of FDA-approved 27 medications through pharmaceutical manufacturers. 28 B. The Office of Group Benefits shall provide the notification by posting 29 information on its website and by electronic distribution. 30 C. Notwithstanding any provision of law to the contrary, any enrollee or SB NO. 250 ENROLLED 1 dependent in the Office of Group Benefits plans who purchases a weight loss 2 pharmaceutical product directly through a pharmaceutical manufacturer shall 3 pay one hundred percent of the cost of the medications. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: HOMELAND SECURITY:  Provides for critical infrastructure protection. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SG RV)
Summary: HOMELAND SECURITY:  Provides for critical infrastructure protection. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SG RV)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 251 BY SENATORS PRESSLY, ALLAIN, BARROW, BASS, BOUDREAUX, JACKSON- ANDREWS, SELDERS AND WHEAT 1 2 AN ACT 3 To enact Part B of Chapter 51 of Title 51 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, to be 4 comprised of R.S. 51:3081 through 3089, and to designate R.S. 51:3071 through 5 3080 as Part A of Chapter 51 of Title 51 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, 6 relative to critical infrastructure protection; to provide for critical infrastructure that 7 needs protection from foreign adversaries accessing state critical infrastructure; to 8 provide for assessing the state's vulnerability to sanctioned communications 9 equipment; to prohibit use of adversary cameras and laser sensor technologies in 10 Louisiana transportation systems; to provide enforcement of protected activities; and 11 to provide for related matters. 12 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 13 Section 1. Part B of Chapter 51 of Title 51 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, 14 comprised of R.S. 51:3081 through 3089, is hereby enacted to read as follows: 15 PART B. CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE PROTECTION 16 §3081. Title 17 This Part shall be known and may be cited as the "Louisiana Critical 18 Infrastructure Protection Act of 2026". 19 §3082. Purpose 20 The purpose of this Part is to protect this state's critical infrastructure 21 by prohibiting foreign adversaries from accessing state critical infrastructure, ACT No. 750 SB NO. 251 ENROLLED 1 by assessing this state's vulnerability to sanctioned communications equipment, 2 and by prohibiting the use of adversary cameras and laser sensor technologies 3 in this state's transportation systems. 4 §3083. Rule of construction 5 This Part shall be construed and applied in a manner consistent with 6 federal law and shall not be interpreted or enforced to prohibit, restrict, 7 condition, or delay any transaction, agreement, activity, technology, software, 8 service, or access that has been specifically authorized, approved, licensed, 9 exempted, or otherwise permitted by the United States government. 10 §3084. Definitions 11 The following definitions apply in this Part unless the context indicates 12 otherwise: 13 (1) "Company" means a for-profit sole proprietorship, organization, 14 association, corporation, partnership, joint venture, limited partnership, limited 15 liability partnership, or limited liability company, including a wholly owned 16 subsidiary or majority-owned subsidiary of those entities or business 17 associations that exist to make a profit; or a nonprofit organization. 18 (2) "Critical infrastructure" means systems and assets, whether physical 19 or virtual, so vital to this state or the United States of America that the 20 incapacity or destruction of those systems and assets would have a debilitating 21 impact on state or national security, state or national economic security, state 22 or national public health, or any combination of those matters. A critical 23 infrastructure may be publicly or privately owned, and includes but is not 24 limited to any of the following: 25 (a) Gas and oil production, storage, or delivery systems. 26 (b) Water supply, refinement, storage, or delivery systems. 27 (c) Telecommunications networks. 28 (d) Electrical power delivery systems. 29 (e) Emergency services. 30 (f) Transportation systems and services. SB NO. 251 ENROLLED 1 (g) Personal data or otherwise classified information storage systems, 2 including cybersecurity. 3 (3) "Cybersecurity" means the measures taken to protect a computer, 4 computer network, computer system, or other technology infrastructure against 5 unauthorized use or access. 6 (4) "Domicile" means either the country in which a company is 7 registered or where the company's affairs are primarily completed. 8 (5) "Foreign adversary" means those countries listed in 15 CFR 791.4. 9 (6) "Foreign principal" means any of the following entities: 10 (a) The government or any official of the government of a foreign 11 adversary. 12 (b) A political party or member of a political party or any subdivision of 13 a political party of a foreign adversary. 14 (c) A partnership, association, corporation, organization, or other 15 combination of persons organized under the laws of or having its principal place 16 of business in a foreign adversary, or a subsidiary of such entity, that is owned 17 or controlled, directly or indirectly, by persons or entities of a foreign adversary 18 through the ownership of a majority voting interest or the power to direct 19 management or policies of the entity. Ownership shall not include passive 20 ownership of publicly traded securities that does not confer the power to direct 21 or influence management or operations. 22 (d) Any person who is not an entity described in Subparagraph (c) of this 23 Paragraph, who is domiciled in a foreign adversary, and is not a citizen or 24 lawful permanent resident of the United States or otherwise permitted by law 25 to perform work on critical infrastructure in the United States. 26 (e) Any person, entity, or collection of persons or entities, described in 27 Subparagraphs (a) through (d) of this Paragraph having a controlling interest 28 in a partnership, association, corporation, organization, trust, or any other legal 29 entity or subsidiary formed for the purpose of owning real property. 30 (7) "Office" means the Governor's Office of Homeland Security and SB NO. 251 ENROLLED 1 Emergency Preparedness. 2 (8) "Operational command authority" means the authority to direct, 3 control, or make real-time operational decisions affecting the physical 4 functioning, safety, or emergency response of critical infrastructure. 5 (9) "Significant access" means access that enables the holder to alter 6 physical operations, safety systems, or emergency response functions of critical 7 infrastructure. 8 (10) "Software" means any program or routine, or any set of one or 9 more programs or routines, which are used or intended for use to cause one or 10 more computers or pieces of computer-related peripheral equipment, or any 11 combination thereof, to perform a task or set of tasks, as it relates to state 12 infrastructure, or any operational software. 13 §3085. Prohibited access to critical infrastructure 14 A. A company or other entity constructing, repairing, operating, or 15 otherwise having significant access to critical infrastructure may not knowingly 16 enter into an agreement relating to critical infrastructure in this state with a 17 foreign principal from a foreign adversary if the agreement would allow the 18 foreign principal from a foreign adversary to directly or remotely exercise 19 operational command authority over critical infrastructure in this state. 20 B. A governmental entity may not knowingly enter into a contract or 21 other agreement relating to critical infrastructure in this state with a company 22 that is a foreign principal from a foreign adversary if the agreement would 23 allow the foreign principal from a foreign adversary to directly or remotely 24 exercise operational command authority over critical infrastructure in this 25 state. 26 C. Notwithstanding the provisions in Subsections A and B of this Section, 27 an entity or governmental entity may enter into a contract or agreement 28 relating to critical infrastructure with a foreign principal from a foreign 29 adversary country if all of the following apply: 30 (1) There is no other reasonable option for addressing the need relevant SB NO. 251 ENROLLED 1 to state critical infrastructure. 2 (2) Not entering into the contract or agreement would pose a greater 3 threat to the state than the threat associated with entering into the contract. 4 D.(1) Nothing in this Part shall apply to any transaction, agreement, 5 activity, technology, software, service, or access that is conducted pursuant to 6 and in compliance with a specific license or authorization issued by the United 7 States government, including but not limited to licenses or authorizations issued 8 by the United States Department of Homeland Security, United States 9 Department of the Treasury, or United States Department of Commerce, or 10 where such activities were previously conducted pursuant to a license or 11 authorization that is no longer required due to the lifting, modification, 12 removals, or expiration of applicable United States sanctions, executive orders, 13 regulations, or statutes. 14 (2) Intra-company shared services that do not involve a foreign 15 adversary company do not constitute agreements with a foreign principal so 16 long as ownership and operational command authority remain with a parent 17 company domiciled in the United States. 18 §3086. Prohibitions on certain software in critical infrastructure 19 A. All software providing operational command authority over critical 20 infrastructure shall not include any software produced by a company 21 headquartered in and subject to the laws of a foreign adversary, or a company 22 under the direction or control of a foreign adversary. 23 B. All software used in state infrastructure in operation within or serving 24 this state, including any state infrastructure which is not permanently disabled, 25 shall have all software prohibited pursuant to the provisions of this Section 26 removed and replaced with software which is not prohibited pursuant to the 27 provisions of this Section unless that software was lawfully installed prior to 28 August 1, 2026, and remains compliant with applicable federal law. 29 C. Any state infrastructure provider that removes, discontinues, or 30 replaces any prohibited software shall not be required to obtain any additional SB NO. 251 ENROLLED 1 permits from any state agency or political subdivision for the removal, 2 discontinuance, or replacement of such software as long as the state agency or 3 political subdivision is properly notified of the necessary replacements and the 4 replacement software is similar to the existing software. 5 §3087. Telecommunications providers in compliance with federal law 6 Nothing in this Part applies to any provider of an interactive computer 7 service, as defined in 47 U.S.C. 230, or any provider of a telecommunications 8 service or an information service, as defined in 47 U.S.C. 153, nor to any 9 employee, agent, or contractor acting on behalf of such provider, to the extent 10 that the provider is in compliance with the Secure and Trusted Communications 11 Networks Act of 2019, 47 U.S.C. 1601, et seq. 12 §3088. Electric utilities in compliance with federal law 13 Nothing in this Part applies to any member-owned electric cooperatives 14 or investor-owned utilities subject to the North American Electric Reliability 15 Corporation, NERC, Reliability Standards, or to any member-owned electric 16 cooperatives or investor-owned utility assets subject to the Nuclear Regulatory 17 Commission, NRC. 18 §3089. Enforcement; civil proceedings 19 The attorney general may institute civil proceedings to enforce the 20 provisions of this Part, including but not limited to actions against a foreign 21 adversary company or any other vendor, provider, distributor, or relabeler or 22 white-labeler of a foreign adversary technology for violations of the Unfair 23 Trade Practices and Consumer Protection Law, R.S. 51:1401 et seq. 24 Section 2. The Louisiana State Law Institute is hereby directed to designate the 25 provisions of R.S. 51:3071 through 3080 as Part A of Chapter 51 of Title 51 of the Louisiana 26 Revised Statutes of 1950 to be entitled, "PART A. DATABASE SECURITY BREACH 27 NOTIFICATION". 28 Section 3. It is the intent of this legislature that Venezuela not be considered a foreign 29 adversary unless and until a new determination is made by the United States Department of 30 Commerce to designate the successor regime to the Venezuelan politician Nicolas Maduro SB NO. 251 ENROLLED 1 to be a foreign adversary to the United States. 2 Section 4. (A) The provisions of R.S. 51:3089 as enacted by Section 1 of this Act 3 shall become effective on November 29, 2026. 4 (B) Sections 2, 3, and this Section of this Act shall become effective on August 1, 5 2026. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: DONATIONS:  Provides for anatomical gift authorization on driver's license. (6/1/27) (EN +$200,000 SG EX See Note)
Summary: DONATIONS:  Provides for anatomical gift authorization on driver's license. (6/1/27) (EN +$200,000 SG EX See Note)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 252 BY SENATOR PRESSLY 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 32:410(B), relative to anatomical gifts; to provide for 3 clarification relative to anatomical gift authorization; to provide for refusal of 4 donation of anatomical gifts; to provide for procedures relating to anatomical gifts; 5 to provide for record keeping by the Department of Public Safety and Corrections, 6 relative to anatomical gifts; to provide for an effective date; and to provide for 7 related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. R.S. 32:410(B) is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 10 §410. Form of license; photograph, signature of licensee, anatomical gift statement, 11 declaration of life-sustaining procedures, interest in bone marrow 12 donation, and additional information on license 13 * * * 14 B.(1)(a) The Department of Public Safety and Corrections shall give the 15 widest possible dissemination to the anatomical donation program relating to eyes, 16 kidneys, entire bodies, etc., and shall make whatever provisions are feasible and 17 desirable, through its drivers licensing examining offices, to enable persons desiring 18 to make such anatomical donations to confirm their desires and intentions through 19 the use of suitable documents to be maintained and issued in all driver's license ACT No. 373 SB NO. 252 ENROLLED 1 examining offices. Additionally the license issued by the department shall contain 2 an indication thereon whether or not the named applicant has elected to make an 3 anatomical gift pursuant to the provisions of this Chapter or R.S. 17:2351 et seq., 4 said indication to be by placing an "X" in the appropriate box marked "Yes" or "No" 5 under or adjacent to the term "anatomical gift". 6 (b) The following information shall be made available free of charge to 7 applicants for a driver's license on a form to be promulgated by the department and 8 maintained on file with the department: 9 (i) An anatomical gift statement indicating the organs donated for the purpose 10 of transplantation or transplantation research; 11 (ii) A space for indicating any limitation or special wish of the donor; and 12 (iii) The signature of the donor and the witnesses to the signature of the 13 donor. 14 (c) If the applicant elects not to execute an anatomical gift statement at the 15 time of application, the statement shall remain blank when presented to the licensee 16 so it can be executed by the licensee at any time after the license is issued. No other 17 form or statement shall be required for the purpose of providing for the donation of 18 organs. 19 The Department of Public Safety and Corrections shall make provisions 20 through its driver's license examining offices that enable persons desiring to 21 make anatomical donations to confirm their desires and intentions through the 22 use of suitable documents to be maintained and issued in all driver's license 23 examining offices. The department shall provide to each applicant for a driver's 24 license or identification card information presented in equivalent form and 25 manner, without characterization, recommendation, or emphasis favoring 26 authorization or refusal, regarding the option to make or refuse an anatomical 27 gift, and shall provide the applicant the opportunity to indicate authorization 28 for an anatomical gift, refusal to make an anatomical gift, or no selection. Any 29 indication made by an applicant under this Subparagraph shall be recorded in 30 a manner prescribed by the department and shall have the effect provided by SB NO. 252 ENROLLED 1 law governing anatomical gifts, refusals, and donor registries. Additionally, the 2 license issued by the department shall contain a clear indication of whether the 3 named applicant has authorized an anatomical gift, refused to make an 4 anatomical gift, or made no selection, displayed by placement of an "X" or 5 other clearly visible marking in the appropriate box corresponding to the 6 applicant's selection under or adjacent to the term "anatomical gift". No 7 applicant shall be required to make a selection as a condition of issuance or 8 renewal of a driver's license or identification card. 9 (b) The following information shall be made available, without charge, 10 to each applicant on a form promulgated by the department and maintained on 11 file by the department: 12 (i) A statement describing the option to make an anatomical gift for 13 transplantation or transplantation research and the option to refuse to make an 14 anatomical gift. 15 (ii) A space for the applicant to indicate authorization for an anatomical 16 gift, refusal to make an anatomical gift, or no selection, including any limitation 17 or special instruction the applicant chooses to express. 18 (iii) A space for the signature of the applicant and, where required by 19 law, any required witness or attestation. 20 (c) If an applicant makes no selection under Subparagraph (a) of this 21 Paragraph at the time of application or renewal, no authorization or refusal 22 shall be inferred. The absence of a selection shall not constitute authorization 23 for an anatomical gift, a refusal to make an anatomical gift, or a revocation of 24 any prior authorization or refusal, and shall have no independent legal effect 25 under R.S. 17:2351 et seq. 26 * * * 27 Section 2. This Act shall be known and may be cited as the "Driver's License 28 Anatomical Gift Consent Clarity Act". 29 Section 3. If any provision or item of this Act, or the application thereof, is held 30 invalid, such invalidity shall not affect other provisions, items, or applications of the Act SB NO. 252 ENROLLED 1 which can be given effect without the invalid provision, item, or application and to this end 2 the provisions of this Act are hereby declared severable. 3 Section 4. This Act shall become effective on June 1, 2027. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: PUBLIC HEALTH:  Provides relative to peptides. (8/1/26)
Summary: PUBLIC HEALTH:  Provides relative to peptides. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 253 BY SENATOR MCMATH AND REPRESENTATIVES BAYHAM, BERAULT, CARVER AND WYBLE 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 37:23.5, relative to peptides; to provide for the regulation of peptides by 3 licensing boards; to provide requirements for providers and compounding 4 pharmacies; and to provide for related matters. 5 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 6 Section 1. R.S. 37:23.5 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 7 §23.5. Peptides; prohibited regulations 8 A. No professional or occupational licensing board shall prohibit a 9 healthcare provider with prescriptive authority from providing patients with 10 peptides shipped from either of the following: 11 (1) A FDA-registered 503B outsourcing facility that is in compliance with 12 21 U.S.C. 353b. 13 (2) A 503A compounding pharmacy that is in compliance with 21 U.S.C. 14 353a and applicable United States Pharmacopeia - National Formulary 15 chapters. 16 B. No professional or occupational licensing board shall prohibit a 17 Louisiana licensed pharmacist in a state permitted pharmacy from 18 compounding and dispensing peptides as long as the Louisiana licensed ACT No. 374 SB NO. 253 ENROLLED 1 pharmacist in the state permitted pharmacy is in compliance with 21 U.S.C. 2 353a and applicable United States Pharmacopeia - National Formulary 3 chapters. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: CREDIT:  Provides relative to surcharges for debit card use. (8/1/26)
Summary: CREDIT:  Provides relative to surcharges for debit card use. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 254 BY SENATOR MIZELL AND REPRESENTATIVE CHASSION 1 AN ACT 2 To enact Chapter 52 of Title 51 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, to be comprised 3 of R.S. 51:3081 and 3082, relative to debit card surcharges; to prohibit certain excess 4 surcharges; to provide for liability; to provide for a private right of action; to provide 5 for reporting requirements; to provide for enforcement; to provide for civil penalties; 6 and to provide for related matters. 7 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 8 Section 1. Chapter 52 of Title 51 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 1950, 9 comprised of R.S. 51:3081 and 3082, is hereby enacted to read as follows: 10 CHAPTER 52. DEBIT CARD SURCHARGE 11 §3081. Debit card surcharge; prohibiting certain excess fees; liability; 12 definitions 13 A. As used in this Chapter: 14 (1) "Cardholder" means the person named on the face of a debit card to 15 whom or for whose benefit the debit card is issued by an issuer and includes any 16 employee or other agent or authorized user of the card. 17 (2) "Debit card" means a card or other payment device that debits funds 18 directly from a cardholder's deposit account. 19 (3) "Retail business" means any person or entity engaged in the sale of 20 goods or services. 21 (4) "Surcharge" means any additional amount of money imposed at the 22 time of the transaction by a retail business that increases the charge to the 23 cardholder for the privilege of using a debit card. 24 B. In a sale of goods or services, a retail business shall not impose a 25 surcharge on a cardholder who uses a debit card instead of cash, check, credit 26 card, or any other similar means of payment. 27 C.(1) A retail business that violates the provisions of this Section shall be ACT No. 751 SB NO. 254 ENROLLED 1 liable to the cardholder for actual damages incurred as a result of the violation. 2 (2) Prior to filing a civil action, a cardholder shall provide written notice 3 of the alleged violation to the retail business. If the retail business cures the 4 violation and reimburses the cardholder for any surcharge assessed within 5 thirty days after receipt of written notice, no private right of action shall arise 6 from the alleged violation. 7 (3) A private right of action pursuant to this Section shall apply only to 8 willful violations, repeated violations, or violations that are not cured within the 9 time period provided in Paragraph (2) of this Subsection. 10 (4) Nothing in this Section shall limit the authority of the attorney 11 general to enforce the provisions of this Chapter pursuant to R.S. 51:3082. 12 §3082. Enforcement; penalties; reporting 13 A.(1) The attorney general may bring a civil action against a retail 14 business that violates the provisions of this Chapter. 15 (2) If a court of competent jurisdiction grants judgment or injunctive 16 relief to the attorney general, the court shall award the attorney general 17 reasonable attorney fees, court costs, and investigative costs. 18 (3) A person who violates an administrative order or court order issued 19 for a violation of this Section shall be subject to a civil penalty of not more than 20 five hundred dollars for each violation. A civil penalty may be imposed in any 21 civil action brought by the attorney general. 22 (4) All monies received from the payment of a fine or civil penalty 23 imposed and collected pursuant to the provisions of this Section shall be used 24 by the attorney general to promote consumer protection and education. 25 B. The attorney general shall establish and maintain a toll-free telephone 26 number and an electronic reporting system for cardholders to report an 27 unlawful surcharge on a debit card. 28 C. Any cardholder who is assessed a surcharge in violation of this 29 Chapter for the use of a debit card may submit a complaint to the attorney 30 general through the toll-free number or electronic reporting system established SB NO. 254 ENROLLED 1 pursuant to Subsection B of this Section. The complaint shall include all of the 2 following information: 3 (1) The name and address of the retail business. 4 (2) The date and amount of the transaction. 5 (3) The amount of the surcharge imposed. 6 (4) Any receipt, invoice, or other documentation evidencing the 7 surcharge. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 255 BY SENATOR SELDERS AND REPRESENTATIVES ADAMS, BOYD, BRYANT, ROBBY CARTER, WILFORD CARTER, CHASSION, FREEMAN, GREEN, TRAVIS JOHNSON, KNOX, LAFLEUR, TERRY LANDRY, LARVADAIN, LYONS, MARTINEZ, MURRAY, NEWELL AND TAYLOR 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 40:2162(D)(2)(a)(i), relative to psychosocial rehabilitation 3 services; to provide for educational requirements to provide psychosocial 4 rehabilitation services; and to provide for related matters. 5 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 6 Section 1. R.S. 40:2162(D)(2)(a)(i) is hereby amended and reenacted to read as 7 follows: 8 §2162. Specialized behavioral health rehabilitation services in the Louisiana medical 9 assistance program 10 * * * 11 D. In order to be eligible to receive Medicaid reimbursement, all behavioral 12 health services providers shall ensure that any individual rendering PSR or CPST 13 services for the licensed and accredited provider agency meets all of the following 14 requirements: 15 * * * 16 (2)(a) Any individual rendering PSR services for a licensed and accredited 17 provider agency shall hold a minimum of one of the following: 18 (i) A bachelor's degree from an accredited university or college in the field 19 of counseling, social work, psychology, sociology, rehabilitation services, special 20 education, early childhood education, secondary education, family and consumer ACT No. 375 SB NO. 255 ENROLLED 1 sciences, health sciences, therapeutic recreation, criminal justice, or human 2 growth and development. 3 * * * PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: COURTS:  Provides for a clerk of court in Orleans Parish. (gov sig) (EN SEE FISC NOTE LF EX)
Summary: COURTS:  Provides for a clerk of court in Orleans Parish. (gov sig) (EN SEE FISC NOTE LF EX)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 256 BY SENATOR MORRIS 1 AN ACT 2 To amend and reenact R.S. 13:761(C), 1211, 1211.1, 1212(A), (B)(1) through (5), 1212.1, 3 1213.1, 1213.2, 1216, 1222, 1338, 1371.2, 1373(A)(1), 1373.1, the introductory 4 paragraph of 1381, 1381(21), 1381.3, 1381.5(A) and (B)(2)(e), 1381.7(A), 2515, and 5 5971, R.S. 15:85.1(A)(2)(d) and R.S. 18:2(3), 444(E), 602(C)(1) and (E)(2)(c), 6 1300.7(B), 1354(A)(1), 1511.2(E), and 1511.3(A)(1) and to repeal R.S. 13:1031, 7 1033, 1371, 1371.1, and 1378, relative to the clerk of court in Orleans Parish; to 8 provide for one clerk of the civil district court and the criminal district court; to 9 provide for payments from the Clerk of Court Expense Fund; to provide for the 10 administration of the court by the clerk of court in Orleans Parish; to provide for an 11 effective date; and to provide for related matters. 12 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 13 Section 1. R.S. 13:761(C), 1211, 1211.1, 1212(A), (B)(1) through (5), 1212.1, 14 1213.1, 1213.2, 1216, 1222, 1338, 1371.2, 1373(A)(1), 1373.1, the introductory paragraph 15 of 1381, 1381(21), 1381.3, 1381.5(A) and (B)(2)(e), 1381.7(A), 2515, and 5971 are hereby 16 amended and reenacted to read as follows: 17 §761. Clerks' Supplemental Compensation Fund; creation; sources of funds 18 * * * 19 C. The clerks of all of the district courts and criminal district courts of this 20 state shall remit to the Clerks' Supplemental Compensation Fund from the funds 21 transferred from the advance deposit account to the salary fund account an amount 22 calculated at the rate of four dollars for every original civil suit filed in their offices. 23 The clerk of the Civil District Court court for the parish of Orleans shall remit the 24 fees required in this Subsection from the judicial expense fund of the Civil District ACT No. 15 SB NO. 256 ENROLLED 1 Court for the parish of Orleans. The clerk of the Criminal District Court for the 2 parish of Orleans shall remit the fees required in this Subsection from the clerk of the 3 Criminal District Court Expense Fund for the parish of Orleans. Any safety orientation and training program 19 established in compliance with this Subsection may include, as a condition of 20 satisfactory completion, a course in child cardiopulmonary resuscitation. 21 Participation in a safety orientation and training program by a coach, manager, 22 athletic trainer, team volunteer health care healthcare provider, or sports team 23 official may be waived by the league prior to the individual's or person's participation 24 in the sporting activities or in the practice thereof upon submission of appropriate 25 documented evidence as to that individual's or person's proficiency in first aid and ACT No. 377 SB NO. 260 ENROLLED 1 safety, which may include a current certification in child cardiopulmonary 2 resuscitation. 3 * * * 4 D. The Louisiana Department of Health shall establish and administer 19 a process for approving courses that meet the requirements of a youth injury 20 mitigation course. 21 B. Completion of an approved youth injury mitigation course may be 22 used to satisfy the requirements of a safety orientation and training program in 23 R.S. 9:2798. Each youth injury mitigation course shall include instruction on the 24 following: 25 (1) Emergency preparedness and planning and rehearsal for responding 26 to traumatic injuries sustained during youth athletic activities. 27 (2) Heat and extreme weather-related injuries. 28 (3) Musculoskeletal overuse, physical conditioning, recovery, and 29 training equipment usage. 30 (4) Heart defects and abnormalities leading to sudden cardiac death. SB NO. 260 ENROLLED 1 C. Not later than one hundred eighty days after the effective date of this 2 Section, the department shall publish and maintain on its official website a list 3 of approved courses. The department shall annually review and update the list 4 of approved injury mitigation courses. 5 D. The department may adopt rules, in accordance with the 6 Administrative Procedure Act, as necessary to carry out the provisions of this 7 Section. 8 E. This Section shall not require the department to spend its funds to pay 9 for the development of the training courses provided in this Section. The 10 department may accept donated online courses, provided that the donated 11 courses are approved by the department. 12 Section 3. This Act shall become effective upon signature by the governor or, if not 13 signed by the governor, upon expiration of the time for bills to become law without signature 14 by the governor, as provided by Article III, Section 18 of the Constitution of Louisiana. If 15 vetoed by the governor and subsequently approved by the legislature, this Act shall become 16 effective on the day following such approval. 17 Section 4. This Act shall be known and may be cited as the "Coach Safely Act". PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: SECURITIES:  Provides relative to administration of transfer on death accounts. (8/1/26)
Summary: , which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 262 Original 2026 Regular Session Abraham Proposed law provides that notwithstanding present law to the contrary, the following terms as used in proposed law are defined as follows: (1) "Transfer on death account" means any deposit or brokerage a
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 262 Original 2026 Regular Session Abraham Proposed law provides that notwithstanding present law to the contrary, the following terms as used in proposed law are defined as follows: (1) "Transfer on death account" means any deposit or brokerage account maintained by a financial institution on behalf of an owner where the owner designates by contract and, in accordance with proposed law, a named beneficiary to receive funds or assets held in the account upon the death of the owner. (2) "Financial institution" means any state or federally-chartered bank, credit union, mutual association, or registered and licensed investment management company. Provides that the commissioner of financial institutions is authorized to enact rules and regulations to determine whether an entity qualifies as a financial institution under proposed law. (3) "Owner" means the individual account holder of a transfer on death account. (4) "Beneficiary" means the named beneficiary of a transfer on death account designated by the owner in the transfer on death account agreement between the owner and the financial institution provided that such designation is made in accordance with proposed law. Proposed law provides that the lawful beneficiary designated in the owner's transfer on death account agreement to receive account funds or assets in the event of the owner's death, including the owner's estate, shall be entitled to the proceeds and avails of the transfer on death account against the representatives of the owner or the person effecting the transfer on death account, or the estate of either, and against the heirs and legatees of either person. Proposed law provides that the provisions of proposed law shall apply regardless of whether any of the following circumstances exist: (1) The right to change the beneficiary is reserved or permitted in the transfer on death account agreement between the owner and the financial institution. (2) The transfer on death account agreement between the owner and the financial institution is made payable to the owner, his estate, or to the estate of a beneficiary if the beneficiary predeceases the person. (3) The owner designates a beneficiary in a last will and testament. (4) The transfer on death account agreement between the financial institution and the owner provides terms and conditions for disposition of proceeds in the event the beneficiary predeceases the owner. Proposed law provides that the provisions of proposed law shall not apply unless the financial institution requires an owner to designate or change the beneficiary of a transfer on death account in the following manner: (1) The designation of beneficiary or change of beneficiary form shall be executed by authentic act by the owner at an office of the financial institution. Provides that a designation of beneficiary or change of beneficiary form submitted by the owner to the financial institution electronically shall be void and without effect. (2) The designation of beneficiary or change of beneficiary form shall not be executed on behalf of an owner by a representative of the owner under the authority of a power of attorney or other representative agreement. (3) For transfer on death accounts with more than one owner, the designation of beneficiary or change of beneficiary form shall be executed in the manner provided herein by all owners of the account. Proposed law provides that the procedures described in proposed law are considered minimum standards and shall not be interpreted to prohibit a financial institution from establishing additional terms and conditions under which it will handle transfer on death account designation of beneficiary. Provides that any additional terms and conditions imposed on transfer on death accounts shall be void and without effect to the extent the terms and conditions conflict with proposed law. Proposed law provides that the financial institution and the beneficiary, or the representatives and heirs of either, shall not be held liable and are discharged from all claims to the funds or assets held in the transfer on death account by the estate, surviving spouse, heirs, legatees or forced heirs of a deceased owner if it transfers the funds or assets of the account to the named beneficiary provided that the beneficiary is named by the owner in accordance with proposed law. Proposed law provides that the provisions of proposed law shall not be construed or interpreted to prohibit any claim by the owner's estate or his heirs and legatees to assert a vice of the owner's consent in executing a designation of beneficiary or change of beneficiary form. Provides that the financial institution shall be entitled to assert a vice of consent claim provided that the designation or change is made in accordance with proposed law. Proposed law provides that the provisions of proposed law shall not apply to a debt secured by the owner's pledge or assignment of the funds or assets held in the transfer on death account. Present law (R.S. 6:314) provides for certain procedures related to administration and payment by banks of trust deposits upon the death of a depositor. Proposed law repeals present law. Present law (R.S. 6:653.1) provides for certain procedures related to administration and payment by credit unions of trust deposits upon the death of a depositor. Proposed law repeals present law. Present law (R.S. 6:766.1) provides for certain procedures related to administration and payment by mutual associations of trust deposits upon the death of a depositor. Proposed law repeals present law. Present law (R.S. 9:1711-1711.9) provides for the Louisiana Uniform Transfer on Death Security Registration Act. Proposed law repeals present law. Effective August 1, 2026. (Adds R.S. 6:1368; repeals R.S. 6:314, 653.1, 766.1 and R.S. 9:1711.1-1711.9)

Title: MOTOR CARRIERS:  Provides relative to the Louisiana Towing and Storage Act. (8/1/26)
Summary: , which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 263 Original 2026 Regular Session Owen Present law provides for the owner of the towing, storage, or parking facility to send notice with a certificate of mailing to the owner of the vehicle at the owner's last known address and to the holder of
Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 263 Original 2026 Regular Session Owen Present law provides for the owner of the towing, storage, or parking facility to send notice with a certificate of mailing to the owner of the vehicle at the owner's last known address and to the holder of any lien on the vehicle within 10 business days from the date the department or its authorized agent sends the owner information of the stored vehicle to the owner of the towing, storage, or parking facility. Present law further provides when the department sends the information electronically, the owner of the towing, storage, or parking facility will send notice within five business days. Proposed law retains present law and changes from 10 business days to five business days. Proposed law retains present law and requires that within five business days of obtaining a vehicle, the owner of the towing, storage, or parking facility perform a good faith effort to establish the state of registration and title of a vehicle by examining the Louisiana Office of Motor Vehicle's database, the electronic National Motor Vehicle Title Information System or an equivalent to determine the state of registration; the vehicle for any type of tag, tag record, temporary tag, regular tag, an inspection sticker or other stickers and decals that may indicate a state of possible registration; the law enforcement report to determine whether an out-of-state address is indicated, a tag number or other information identifying the vehicle; the trip sheet or tow ticket; and when the vehicle is unlocked, the interior of the vehicle for any papers that may be in the glove box, trunk, or other areas for a state of registration. Proposed law provides for technical changes. Proposed law provides for prohibition on storage charges when it has been determined a good faith effort to comply with notice requirements was not adhered to. Proposed law provides for evidence of a persons interest when surrendering a stored vehicle to owner or lienholder including electronic title, paper title, a contract between a lender and the owner of the vehicle, a contract between a lessor and the lessee of the vehicle, credentials establishing the person as an employee or contract agent of an insurance company, a written agreement evidencing that the person is an agent of the vehicle owner or lienholder. Present law provides when a vehicle placed in a storage facility or a repair or body shop is encumbered by a lien, and the storage or repair or body shop operator mails the notification to the storage or repair or body shop operator shall surrender possession of the vehicle to the holder of any lien on the vehicle on behalf of the registered owner. Present law further provides the holder of any lien on the vehicle is the holder shown on the records of the office of motor vehicles and surrender does not occur until the secured party makes a complete payment of the towing and storage charges and executes a hold harmless agreement, agreeing to indemnify the storage or repair or body shop operator for surrender of the vehicle. Proposed law retains present law and adds that when surrendering the vehicle, a towing-storage operator must provide an itemized invoice containing: (1) The date and time the vehicle was towed. (2) The location to which the vehicle was towed. (3) The name, address, and telephone number of the towing-storage operator. (4) A description of the towed vehicle, including the color, make, model, model year, and vehicle identification number. (5) The license plate number and state of registration for the towed vehicle. (6) The cost of the initial towing service. (7) The cost of any storage fees, expressed as a per day rate and total cost. (8) Other fees, including administrative fees, vehicle search fees, fees for hazardous material and nonhazardous material cleanup, and fees for labor. (9) A list of the services that were performed under a warranty or that were otherwise performed at no cost to the owner of the vehicle. Proposed law further provides that a towing-storage operator will permit vehicle owners, lienholders, insurance company representatives, or their agents whose interest in the vehicle is evidenced, to inspect the towed vehicle and release to the owner, lienholder, or agent of the vehicle all personal property in the vehicle when the vehicle was taken by the storage operator. Proposed law further provides the inspection and release of the vehicle within one hour after presentation of any of the required documents during normal business hours at the site where the vehicle is stored. Proposed law further provides that a rental vehicle agreement is not evidence that the person who rented a vehicle is an agent of the rental vehicle owner for the purpose of releasing the vehicle. Proposed law provides, a towing-storage operator must release to the renter of a rental vehicle all personal property belonging to the renter within one hour after the renter's arrival. Effective August 1, 2026. (Amends R.S. 32:1720 and 1720.1)

Title: CHILDREN:  Abolishes the Department of Children and Family Services. (10/1/26) (OR DECREASE GF EX See Note)
Summary: CHILDREN:  Abolishes the Department of Children and Family Services. (10/1/26) (OR DECREASE GF EX See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 320 265 SLS 26RS BARROW Author: Subject: Children and Family Services Abolishes Dept of Children and Family Services (DCFS) March 17, 2026 10:27 AM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Patrice Thomas Proposed law may result in indeterminable savings from efficiencies and economies of scale in future fiscal years. In FY 27, proposed law is anticipated to result in decreased expenditures as certain duplicate services, functions, and positions are anticipated to be eliminated. Proposed law abolishes the Department of Children and Family Services (DCFS), and transfers the Division of Child Welfare to the LA Department of Health (LDH) and the Division of Child Support to LA Works. There is no anticipated material cost to the LDH or LA Works due to the transfer of programs. Potential Efficiencies from Transfers Division of Child Welfare and Division of Child Support. Proposed law does not eliminate any programs or services currently provided by the Division of Child Welfare or the Division of Child Support. However, in future fiscal years, potential savings could be realized by eliminating any duplication of effort, which would decrease the number of employees and/or the amount of physical space needed for future operations. To the extent that economies of scale are achieved and administrative savings result after the programs are transferred to their respective new departments, any administrative savings realized will be used to enhance program services and/or activities in future fiscal years. Such savings are speculative and depend on administrative decisions, and are therefore indeterminable. Potential Elimination of Duplication Division of Management and Finance. Proposed law is silent regarding the transfer of the Division of Management and Finance in DCFS. Since the division provides administrative services and support functions to both Child Welfare and Child Support, this note anticipates that services and functions, along with employees, that directly support Child Welfare and Child Support will be transferred proportionally to LDH and LA Works. To the extent that there is duplication, there may be savings from efficiencies. Also, certain executive leadership positions (i.e., Secretary, Deputy Secretary, Undersecretary, Press Secretary) are anticipated to be eliminated, resulting in annual savings of approximately $561,989. Name Change, Physical and IT Move. Proposed law requires the transfer of 2,180 positions that may result in increased one-time expenditures related to relocation of employees and IT equipment. Also, there will be increased expenditures to change office building signage, decals on state vehicles, printing new stationery and business cards, issuing new employee I.D. badges, and other incidental items. The fiscal note anticipates that name changes and moving costs will be accomplished over several fiscal years and can be absorbed within existing budgets. Expenditure Explanation Continues on See Page 2 Proposed law abolishes the Department of Children and Family Services (DCFS) and transfers its programs, activities, services, functions, contracts, and employees as follows: (1) Division of Child Support to LA Works; (2) Division of Child Welfare to LA Department of Health (LDH); and (3) TANF to LA Works. Proposed law removes all references to DCFS from the LA Administrative Code and replaces it with the appropriate agency - LDH or LA Works. Proposed law repeals current law that authorizes the reorganization of DCFS. Proposed law is effective 10/01/26. Under current law, upon the abolishment of one of the 20 executive branch departments, the Department of Elderly Affairs shall automatically be created. Proposed law repeals the automatic creation of the Department of Elderly Affairs. Since DCFS operates under a Public Assistance Cost Allocation Plan (PACAP), the department’s Divisions of Child Welfare, Child Support, and Management and Finance allocate administrative and overhead costs across all federal funding sources. Proposed law would require LDH to change its existing PACAP and LA Works to create a new PACAP. Therefore, LDH and LA Works must seek Federal approval of any new cost allocation, which may impact federal revenue if there is a delay in approval. Based on prior Federal approval of the transfers of Economic Stability programs to LDH and LWC, there is no anticipated direct material effect on federal revenues as a result of this measure. Title: FEES/LICENSES/PERMITS:  Provides relative to occupational licenses. (8/1/26) (OR SEE FISC NOTE LF EX)
Summary: FEES/LICENSES/PERMITS:  Provides relative to occupational licenses. (8/1/26) (OR SEE FISC NOTE LF EX)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 470 266 SLS 26RS PRESSLY Author: Subject: Boards and Commissions Relative to occupational licenses for judicial review April 12, 2026 11:25 AM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Katherine Granier Proposed law allows individuals to petition a court to review statutory requirements mandating occupational licenses. The court will determine whether such requirements are necessary and narrowly tailored to protect public health, safety, welfare, or fiduciary interests. Proposed law provides for an effective date of August 1, 2026. Proposed law is anticipated to result in an indeterminable, but minimal, increase in SGF expenditures in local courts due to potential judicial filings challenging occupational licensing statutory requirements. The number of cases is expected to be limited, so the overall impact is likely nominal. The LFO surveyed the following boards, which represent the largest licensing boards: Louisiana State Board of Medical Examiners, Louisiana State Board of Dentistry, Louisiana State Board of Massage Therapy, Louisiana State Board of Social Work Examiners, and Louisiana State Board of Nursing. Officials from these boards do not anticipate any additional expenses as a result of the proposed law. To the extent that any costs may occur, these costs are expected to be absorbed utilizing existing staff and current resources. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. REVENUE EXPLANATION EXPENDITURE EXPLANATION Provides relative to occupational licenses. Title: LOCAL FINANCE:  Provides for lead service line replacement. (8/1/26)
Summary: LOCAL FINANCE:  Provides for lead service line replacement. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 268 BY SENATOR DUPLESSIS AND REPRESENTATIVES ADAMS, BOYD, BRASS, BRYANT, CHASSION, FISHER, FREEMAN, FREIBERG, GREEN, HEBERT, JACKSON, JORDAN, KNOX, LAFLEUR, MANDIE LANDRY, TERRY LANDRY, LARVADAIN, LYONS, MARCELLE, MARTINEZ, MENA, MILLER, MOORE, MUSCARELLO, NEWELL, PHELPS, STAGNI, WALTERS AND YOUNG 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 33:4081.2, relative to water systems; to provide with respect to municipalities 3 and municipal water systems; to provide relative to lead service line replacement; to 4 provide for right-of-entry; to provide with respect to terms, conditions, and 5 procedures; to provide for notice and notice requirements; to provide for definitions; 6 to provide for funding; and to provide for related matters. 7 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 8 Section 1. R.S. 33:4081.2 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 9 §4081.2. Lead service line replacement; right-of-entry 10 A. A municipality may adopt an ordinance that allows the municipality 11 or municipal water system, or any agent thereof, to enter a property within the 12 municipality to perform a lead service line replacement, provided that the 13 municipality provides the owner and any residents of the property with notice 14 at least seven days before entering the property, unless in the case of an 15 emergency as determined by the municipality or municipal water system. A 16 municipality may not enter into a property that is not directly related to 17 performing a lead service line replacement. 18 B. For the purposes of this Section, notice to the owner and any residents 19 of the property shall include an attempt to inform the owner and any residents, 20 in person, of the date and time of the lead service line replacement, and if the 21 owner or a resident is unable to be reached in person, the municipality shall 22 send, by certified mail, a letter to the owner and any residents or post a written 23 notice in a prominent location on the property which shall include: ACT No. 957 SB NO. 268 ENROLLED 1 (1) The scheduled date and time of the lead service line replacement and 2 who will be performing the replacement. 3 (2) The likely extent of water service disruption. 4 (3) Nearby locations where the municipality or a public water system is 5 distributing supplementary drinking water, if any. 6 (4) Any remedies that the municipality shall take if the municipality or 7 municipal water system, or an agent thereof, is unable to access the property. 8 C. In addition to the notice requirements of Subsection B of this Section, 9 a municipality shall send, by certified mail, a letter to the owner after the 10 completion of the replacement stating the approximate time that the 11 replacement occurred, and providing a brief summary of the work performed. 12 D. As used in this Section: 13 (1) "Municipal water system" means a municipal utilities authority, 14 water district, waterworks, water commission, joint meeting or any other 15 political subdivision of the state authorized, pursuant to law, to operate or 16 maintain a public water system or to construct, rehabilitate, operate, or 17 maintain water supply facilities or otherwise provide water for human 18 consumption. 19 (2) "Service line" means the pipe, tubing, and fittings connecting a 20 municipal water main to a building or structure, and also includes the water 21 meter for the property. Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 190 269 SLS 26RS EDMONDS Author: Subject: Charlie Kirk Success Sequencing Act March 11, 2026 3:49 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Julie Silva Proposed legislation enacts the “Charlie Kirk Success Sequencing Act” and requires each public school and approved nonpublic school to provide instruction and informational materials on the “success sequence,” defined as obtaining a high school diploma or equivalency, entering the workforce, and marrying before having children. Proposed legislation requires such instruction to be incorporated into existing preventive education programs or high school programs of study, in accordance with rules adopted by the State Board of Elementary and Secondary Education (BESE). Proposed legislation authorizes the Louisiana Department of Education (LDOE) to develop, maintain, and distribute guidance and instructional materials and to collaborate with external stakeholders for such purposes. Proposed legislation may increase administrative workload for local school systems to incorporate success sequence instruction into preventive programs and high school programs of study. Additional impacts may be realized by the Louisiana Department of Education (LDOE) to the extent the department opts to engage research institutions, nonprofit organizations, or other stakeholders to assemble guidance, produce public service announcements, videos, handouts, and other supplemental materials to assist schools in implementing the provisions of the legislation. Note: Proposed legislation authorizes, but does not require, the Louisiana Department of Education (LDOE) to engage research institutions, nonprofit organizations, and other interested stakeholders in order to develop public service announcements, handouts, and posters to support success sequence education. If funding is available, LDOE would contract a communications firm to manage the project. Deliverables would include a 2–3 minute professionally produced PSA with captioning and Spanish subtitles, audience-specific digital handouts for students, parents, and educators, and roughly 3,900–4,000 printed 24x36 posters for about 1,300 schools. Estimated one-time costs total $140,000, including $105,000 for professional services, which covers strategy, creative design, video production, translation coordination, accessibility formatting, and project management, and $35,000 for printing and distribution. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure. Title: HEALTH CARE:  Provides for access to medical marijuana for terminally ill patients in a healthcare facility. (8/1/26)
Summary: HEALTH CARE:  Provides for access to medical marijuana for terminally ill patients in a healthcare facility. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 270 BY SENATOR JACKSON-ANDREWS 1 AN ACT 2 To enact R.S. 40:1046.5, relative to medical marijuana; to allow for the use of medical 3 marijuana by a terminally ill patient in a healthcare facility; to provide for 4 requirements; to provide for prohibitions; to provide for applicability; and to provide 5 for related matters. 6 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 7 Section 1. R.S. 40:1046.5 is hereby enacted to read as follows: 8 §1046.5. Access to medical marijuana in a healthcare facility 9 A. As used in this Section the following definitions shall apply: 10 (1) "Healthcare facility" means a healthcare facility licensed pursuant 11 to R.S. 40:2006(A)(2). Healthcare facility shall not mean any freestanding or 12 distinct part unit of a hospital providing behavioral health services or any 13 emergency or outpatient department of a hospital. 14 (2) "Medical marijuana" means marijuana for therapeutic use produced 15 and recommended to a patient in accordance with R.S. 40:1046. 16 (3) "Patient" means a person who has a terminal and irreversible 17 condition as defined by R.S. 40:1151.1 and maintains a current recommendation 18 to use medical marijuana. 19 B. A healthcare facility shall permit patient use of medical marijuana 20 and shall: 21 (1) Prohibit smoking or vaping as methods to use medical marijuana. 22 (2) Include the use of medical marijuana within the patient's medical 23 records. 24 (3) Require a patient to provide a copy of the patient's recommendation 25 for medical marijuana pursuant to R.S. 40:1046. ACT No. 602 SB NO. 270 ENROLLED 1 (4) Require a patient or a primary caregiver to be responsible for 2 acquiring, retrieving, administering, and removing medical marijuana. 3 (5) Subject to the policy adopted by the healthcare facility, require 4 medical marijuana to be stored securely at all times in a locked container 5 provided by the patient in the patient's room, other designated area, or with the 6 patient's primary caregiver. 7 (6) Prohibit health care professionals, healthcare facility staff, including 8 but not limited to physicians, nurses, and pharmacists, from administering, 9 storing, retrieving, or assisting the patient with the medical marijuana. 10 (7) Upon discharge, all remaining medical marijuana shall be removed 11 by the patient or patient's primary caregiver. If a patient cannot remove the 12 medical marijuana and does not have a primary caregiver that is available to 13 remove the medical marijuana, the medical marijuana shall be disposed of in 14 accordance with the health facility policy and procedure governing medical 15 marijuana. 16 (8) Develop, disseminate, and train health facility staff on the written 17 guidelines developed by the facility for patient use of medical marijuana within 18 the health care facility pursuant to this Section. 19 C. The provisions of this Section shall not require a healthcare facility 20 to provide a terminally ill patient with a recommendation to use medical 21 marijuana or include medical marijuana in a patient's discharge plan. 22 D. Compliance with this Section shall not be a condition for obtaining, 23 retaining, or renewing a license as a healthcare facility. 24 E. If a federal regulatory agency, the United States Department of 25 Justice, or the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services takes one of the 26 following actions against a healthcare facility, then all healthcare facilities may 27 suspend compliance with the provisions of this Section until the federal 28 regulatory agency, the United States Department of Justice, or the Centers for 29 Medicare and Medicaid Services notifies the healthcare facility that it may 30 resume allowing the use of medical marijuana within the facility: SB NO. 270 ENROLLED 1 (1) A federal regulatory agency or the United States Department of 2 Justice initiates enforcement action against a healthcare facility related to the 3 facility's compliance with a state-regulated medical marijuana program. 4 (2) A federal regulatory agency, the United States Department of Justice, 5 or the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services issues a rule or otherwise 6 provides notification to the healthcare facility that expressly prohibits the use 7 of medical marijuana in healthcare facilities or otherwise prohibits compliance 8 with a state-regulated medical marijuana program. 9 F. A healthcare facility shall not prohibit a terminally ill patient's use of 10 medical marijuana due solely to the fact that marijuana is a Schedule I drug 11 pursuant to the federal Uniform Controlled Substances Act or other federal 12 constraints on the use of medical marijuana that were in existence prior to the 13 enactment of this Section. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

Title: ROADS/HIGHWAYS:  Provides for permits for hauling grain and cotton on Interstate 20. (8/1/26) (OR SEE FISC NOTE SD RV)
Summary: ROADS/HIGHWAYS:  Provides for permits for hauling grain and cotton on Interstate 20. (8/1/26) (OR SEE FISC NOTE SD RV)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 575 271 SLS 26RS CATHEY Author: Subject: Department of Transportation and Development Special Permits for Hauling Grain and Cotton March 17, 2026 6:04 PM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Kimberly Fruge Proposed law provides for special permits to persons who own or operate vehicles which haul cotton and grain to travel upon a segment of Interstate 20 as an alternative route to US 80, provided there are no objections raised by the federal government; requires the Department of Transportation and Development shall promulgate rules that limits permits to harvest season operations and provides necessary weight limits and procedures to comply with federal requirements and laws. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental expenditures as a result of this measure. Proposed law establishes special permits for vehicles hauling grain and cotton to travel on Interstate 20 between US 165 in Monroe and LA 193 in Delta, provided there are no objections raised by the federal government. DOTD reports it may incur minimal administrative costs associated with developing and issuing special permits pursuant to proposed law. The LFO assumes any such costs can be absorbed in the department’s existing operating budget. Proposed law may result in an indeterminable increase in revenues deposited into the Statutorily Dedicated Transportation Trust Fund - Regular (TTF-R), to the extent the Department of Transportation and Development issues special permits for persons who own or operate vehicles that haul cotton and grain to travel along a segment of Interstate 20. The number of permits that may be issued on an annual basis is unknown, and the potential revenues are indeterminable. The department reports that issuing a special permit pursuant to proposed law may jeopardize 50% of federal transportation funding. Proposed law authorizes the department to issue the special permits provided no objections are raised by the federal government. The LFO assumes that if the federal government indicates that issuing such a permit will risk federal funding, based on vehicle weight limitations outlined in 23 USC 127, the department will not issue any special permits. Title: ENVIRONMENTAL CONTROL:  Provides relative to licensing reciprocity for nuclear power generation. (8/1/26) (OR NO IMPACT See Note)
Summary: ENVIRONMENTAL CONTROL:  Provides relative to licensing reciprocity for nuclear power generation. (8/1/26) (OR NO IMPACT See Note)
Content: Fiscal Note LEGISLATIVE FISCAL OFFICE Date: Dept./Agy.: Fiscal Note On: SB 403 272 SLS 26RS BASS Author: Subject: Department of Environmental Quality Nuclear Power Generation March 11, 2026 11:36 AM ORIGINAL Bill Text Version: Opp. Chamb. Action: Proposed Amd.: Sub. Bill For.: Analyst: Richie Anderson Present law authorizes the Secretary of the Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) to create a program to expedite processing of permits, modifications, licenses, registrations, and variances. Present law authorizes the Secretary to create a federal permitting parity program to expedite these processes for nuclear power generation. Proposed law clarifies that the Secretary shall adopt rules to provide notice that a permit issued under present law is being issued pursuant to the federal permitting parity program for nuclear power. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental expenditures as a result of this measure. Proposed law clarifies the Secretary of the Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) shall adopt rules to provide notice that a permit issued under the present law is being issued pursuant to the federal permitting parity program for nuclear power. The department reports that it can adopt rules to implement the proposed law using existing resources. There is no anticipated direct material effect on governmental revenues as a result of this measure.
