R.S. 37:3386.1, 3386.2(A), the introductory paragraph of 3386.2(B), and 15 3386.2(B)(5) and (6), (F), and (G), 3387.1(A)(2)(b) through (d), 3387.2(A)(2)(b) through 16 (d), 3387.3(B)(4), 3387.6(E)(1) and (3) and (I), 3388.3, the introductory paragraph of 17 3390.3(A), 3390.3(A)(11) and (B)(4), and 3390.6(A) and (B)(6) through (8) are hereby 18 amended and reenacted and R.S. 37:3386.2(B)(7) through (10) and (H), 3387.15, 3387.16, 19 3388.4, and 3390.6(B)(9) are hereby enacted to read as follows: 20 §3386.1. Definitions 21 As used in this Chapter, the following terms have the meaning ascribed to 22 them in this Section: 23 (1) "Addiction counselor" means any person who is licensed, certified, or 24 registered in accordance with the provisions of this Chapter and procedures 25 established by the board and who, by means of his special knowledge acquired 26 through formal education or practical experience, is qualified to provide addiction ACT No. 767 SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 counseling services to those individuals afflicted with or suffering from an addictive 2 disorder or certain co-occurring disorders. The counseling services provided shall be 3 those which utilize KSA or core functions, as determined by the board to be 4 appropriate for the addictive disorder or disorders presented. 5 (2) "Addictive disorder" means the repeated pathological use of substances 6 including but not limited to alcohol, drugs, or tobacco, or repeated pathological 7 compulsive behaviors including but not limited to gambling, which cause physical, 8 psychological, emotional, economic, legal, social, or other harms to the individual 9 afflicted with the addiction or to others affected by the individual's affliction. As 10 used in this Chapter, "addictive disorder" shall include not only those instances 11 where withdrawal from or tolerance to the substance or behaviors are present but 12 also those instances involving use and abuse of substances. 13 (3) "Behavioral health condition" means a condition that affects an 14 individual's emotional, psychological, cognitive, behavioral, or social 15 functioning and may impact their ability to pursue health, wellness, recovery, 16 and quality of life, and includes substance use-related conditions, mental health 17 conditions, co-occurring conditions, trauma-related impacts, and patterns of 18 behavior that create functional impairment, distress, or increased risk of harm 19 to the individual or others. 20 (3)(4) "Board" means the governing body of the Addictive Disorder 21 Regulatory Authority. 22 (4)(5) "Certified clinical supervisor" means any person holding the necessary 23 credential of licensed, certified, or registered addiction counselor or any person who 24 holds a specialty substance abuse credential in another professional discipline in a 25 human services field at the master's level or higher; and who has satisfied the 26 requirements established by the board to provide clinical supervision. 27 (5)(6) "Client" means the individual, couple, family, group, organization, or 28 community that seeks or receives addiction counseling services from the addiction 29 professional or an addiction facility until discharged. 30 (6)(7) "Clinical supervision" means the interpersonal tutorial relationship SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 between a certified clinical supervisor and other licensed, certified, or registered 2 addiction counseling professionals centered on the goals of skill development and 3 professional growth through learning and practicing. Through observation, 4 evaluation, and feedback, clinical supervision enables the supervisee to acquire the 5 competence needed to deliver effective patient care while fulfilling professional 6 responsibility. Clinical supervision is understood to emphasize improvement of the 7 counseling skills and effectiveness of the supervisee and is to be distinguished from 8 administrative supervision. 9 (7)(8) "Compulsive gambling counselor" means any person holding a 10 necessary credential as a licensed, certified, or registered addiction counselor, or a 11 necessary credential as a qualified mental health professional, who is certified by the 12 board, in accordance with the provisions of this Chapter, as possessing special 13 knowledge acquired through formal education and clinical experience and thus is 14 qualified to provide gambling addiction counseling to persons who have a gambling 15 addiction disorder or who exhibit gambling addictive behaviors. 16 (8)(9) "Co-occurring disorder" means a disorder in which an individual has 17 at least one psychiatric disorder as well as an addictive disorder. While these 18 disorders may interact differently in any one person, at least one disorder of each 19 type can be diagnosed independently of the other. 20 (9)(10) "Core functions" means the screening, intake, orientation, assessment, 21 treatment planning, counseling, case management, crisis intervention, client 22 education, referral, reports, and record-keeping activities associated with counseling 23 and consultation with other credentialed professionals. 24 (10)(11) "Counselor-in-training" means any person who has not yet met the 25 qualification to become a licensed, certified, or registered counselor, but who has 26 made application to the board in accordance with the provisions of this Chapter and 27 procedures established by the board. 28 (12) "Credentialing" means licensing, certification, registration, or 29 recognition of status of an individual to provide mental health services in 30 accordance with the provisions of this Chapter. SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 (11)(13) "Custodial environment" means a setting created by a court system 2 in which a person has been deprived of freedom of action in any significant way. 3 (12)(14) "KSA" means the knowledge, skills, and attitudes designated by the 4 board as being necessary for effective addiction counseling and required by the board 5 to be utilized by addictive disorders counselors in providing addiction counseling 6 services. 7 (15) "Licensed mental health professional" means an individual who is 8 currently licensed and in good standing in the state of Louisiana to practice 9 within the scope of all applicable state laws, practice acts, and the individual's 10 professional license, as one of the following: 11 (a) Medical psychologist. 12 (b) Licensed psychologist. 13 (c) Licensed clinical social worker. 14 (d) Licensed professional counselor. 15 (e) Licensed marriage and family therapist. 16 (f) Licensed addiction counselor. 17 (g) Advanced practice registered nurse. 18 (h) Licensed rehabilitation counselor. 19 (16) "Peer support" means nonclinical, strengths-based activities and 20 services provided by an individual in recovery from a behavioral health 21 condition to a person experiencing a behavioral health condition. Such services 22 are grounded in lived experience and include mentoring, guiding, and 23 supporting individuals in the identification and implementation of recovery 24 strategies through relationship-based support. Peer support utilizes shared 25 experience to provide empathy, practical guidance, encouragement, and hope, 26 and may include assistance with self-advocacy, goal setting, and engagement 27 with treatment and community-based recovery supports. Peer support services 28 complement but do not replace clinical treatment and shall not include 29 diagnosis, clinical assessment, psychotherapy, or any service requiring 30 professional licensure. SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 (17) "Peer support specialist" means any person who is credentialed in 2 accordance with the provisions of this Chapter and procedures established by 3 the board and who, by means of his special knowledge acquired through formal 4 education and lived experience, is qualified to provide behavioral health 5 services to those individuals afflicted with or suffering from a behavioral health 6 condition. The services provided shall be those which utilize support services or 7 core functions, as determined by the board. 8 (18) "Peer support specialist supervisor" means any person holding the 9 necessary credential of peer support specialist, licensed mental health 10 professional, certified, or registered addiction counselor and who has satisfied 11 the requirements established by the board to provide peer supervision. 12 (13)(19) "Prevention" means those activities and services that prevent, 13 reduce, or stabilize the incidence of addictive disorders and thereby prevent, reduce, 14 or stabilize the prevalence of addictive disorders. The activities contemplated by this 15 definition include services to those at risk of developing an addictive disorder as well 16 as those individuals who, though not necessarily at risk of developing an addictive 17 disorder, are nonetheless appropriate for services. In addition, prevention shall be 18 understood to include educational programs and activities that are designed to raise 19 the awareness of and encourage healthy behaviors. 20 (14)(20) "Prevention specialist-in-training" means any person who has not 21 yet met the qualifications to become a licensed, certified, or registered prevention 22 professional, but who has made application to the board in accordance with the 23 provisions of this Chapter and procedures established by the board. 24 (15) "Qualified mental health professional" means: 25 (a) A psychiatrist licensed pursuant to R.S. 37:1261 et seq. 26 (b) A psychologist licensed pursuant to R.S. 37:2351 et seq., or a medical 27 psychologist licensed pursuant to R.S. 37:1360.51 et seq. 28 (c) A licensed clinical social worker licensed pursuant to R.S. 37:2701 et seq. 29 (d) A mental health counselor licensed pursuant to R.S. 37:1101 et seq. 30 (16)(21) "Substance abuse use disorder" means the repeated pathological use SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 of drugs, including alcohol, which causes physical, psychological, economic, legal, 2 or social harm to the individual user or to others affected by the user's behavior. 3 §3386.2. Addictive Disorder Regulatory Authority 4 A. The legislature hereby finds it appropriate that the responsibility and 5 authority to regulate the practice of addictive disorder treatment, and prevention, and 6 peer support shall be exercised by a licensing board created for that purpose. It is 7 the intent of the legislature that a single authority issue and recognize 8 credentials for addictive disorder counseling, prevention, and peer support 9 professionals in Louisiana, and that such authority shall be the Addictive 10 Disorder Regulatory Authority. 11 B. The Addictive Disorder Regulatory Authority (ADRA) is hereby created 12 within the Louisiana Department of Health. The ADRA shall be governed by a board 13 of directors, referred to hereafter in this Chapter as the "board", consisting of eight 14 eleven voting members, composed of one member appointed by the secretary of 15 the Louisiana Department of Health to represent the office of behavioral health 16 and ten members all of whom shall be appointed by the governor, subject to Senate 17 confirmation, as follows: 18 * * * 19 (5) Three members from a list of nine names divided into three groups of 20 three names each submitted by the Louisiana Association of Substance Abuse 21 Counselors and Trainers, Inc. One member with a master's degree in addiction 22 counseling, counseling, psychology, social work, or another human services field 23 submitted by the president of the University of Louisiana at Lafayette. 24 (6) One member with a master's degree in addiction counseling, 25 counseling, psychology, social work, or another human services field submitted 26 by the president of Louisiana State University. 27 (7) One member with a master's degree in addiction counseling, 28 counseling, psychology, social work, or another human services field submitted 29 by the president of Southern University New Orleans. 30 (8) One member with a master's degree in addiction counseling, SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 counseling, psychology, social work, or another human services field submitted 2 by the president of Northwestern State University. 3 (9) One member who is a trained and recognized peer support specialist 4 with a minimum of three years of experience as a peer support specialist 5 providing peer support services to persons with addictive disorders. 6 (10) One member who is a consumer selected from the state at large. 7 * * * 8 F. Except for the consumer member, each member of the board shall at 9 the time of appointment satisfy all of the following criteria: 10 (1) Be currently credentialed by the board. 11 (2) Have no disciplinary history with the board. 12 (3) Be eligible to provide supervised clinical services, mentor trainees, or 13 participate in board-approved supervision structures. 14 (4) Demonstrate competency in all of the following: 15 (a) Addiction counseling and recovery-oriented care. 16 (b) Co-occurring disorder treatment. 17 (c) Evidence-based behavioral health practices. 18 (5) Have familiarity with Louisiana's behavioral health infrastructure, 19 including state agencies, parish level services, and community based recovery 20 networks. 21 F.G.(1) The consumer member of the board shall possess all of the following 22 qualifications: 23 (a) Is a citizen of the United States and has been a resident of Louisiana for 24 at least one year immediately prior to appointment. 25 (b) Has attained the age of majority. 26 (c) Has never been licensed by any of the licensing boards identified in R.S. 27 36:259(A), nor shall he have a spouse who has ever been licensed by a board 28 identified in R.S. 36:259(A). 29 (d) Has never been convicted of a felony. 30 (e) Does not have and has never had a material financial interest in the SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 healthcare profession. 2 (2) The consumer member shall be a full voting member of the board with 3 all rights and privileges conferred on board members, except that the consumer 4 member shall not participate in the grading of individual examinations. 5 G.H. It is the intent of the legislature that the board exercise all regulatory 6 jurisdiction over the credentialing and practice of addiction counselors, peer support 7 specialists, prevention professionals, persons holding specialty certifications issued 8 by the board, paraprofessionals authorized by this Chapter, and those in training to 9 become addiction counselors and prevention professionals. 10 * * * 11 §3387.1. Peer support specialist; scope of practice 2 A.(1) The practice of peer support within the meaning and intent of this 3 Chapter shall consist of the supervised rendering of mentorship, guidance, and 4 support services to individuals suffering from a behavioral health condition to 5 assist them in gaining an understanding of the nature of their disorder and 6 developing and maintaining a responsible lifestyle. 7 (2) A peer support specialist shall not practice autonomously. 8 Supervision shall be rendered by a licensed mental health professional or peer 9 support specialist supervisor. A peer support specialist shall not render clinical 10 services. 11 (3) The scope of practice, in addition to any other applicable provision 12 of this Chapter, shall include implementing strategic support, providing 13 mentorship to family members and, as appropriate, to others affected by the 14 individual's behavioral health condition. 15 B. The board shall adopt and promulgate rules which govern peer 16 support specialists in accordance with the provisions of this Chapter. 17 C. Any person seeking to be certified by the board as a peer support 18 specialist shall submit an application on a form and in a manner prescribed by 19 the board. The initial application form shall be accompanied by an initial 20 application fee as provided for in this Chapter. 21 D. Upon investigation of the application, the board shall, within forty- 22 five days, notify each candidate that the application is satisfactory and accepted 23 or unsatisfactory and rejected. If an application is rejected, the notice shall state 24 the reason for such rejection. 25 E. The board shall recognize, as a peer support specialist, each candidate 26 who satisfies all of the following criteria: 27 (1) Is a legal resident of the United States and at least eighteen years of 28 age on the date the application is received. 29 (2) Is not in violation of any ethical standards prescribed by the board. 30 (3) If the applicant has lived experience with a behavioral health SB NO. 426 ENROLLED 1 condition, has attested to at least twelve months of stability and is not currently 2 engaged in acute treatment at the time the application is received. 3 (4) Has not been convicted of, or entered a plea of guilty or a plea of nolo 4 contendere to, a felony. However, the board may, in its discretion, waive this 5 requirement upon review of the individual's circumstances. Each candidate 6 shall sign a form prescribed by the board authorizing the board to obtain a 7 criminal history or to conduct a criminal background check to be performed by 8 the Bureau of Criminal Identification and Information. Title: DONATIONS:  Provides for procedures and methods to execute gifts under the Anatomical Gift Act. (1/1/27)
Summary: DONATIONS:  Provides for procedures and methods to execute gifts under the Anatomical Gift Act. (1/1/27)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 427 (Substitute of Senate Bill No. 311 by Senator Pressly) BY SENATOR PRESSLY 1 2 3 AN ACT 4 To amend and reenact R.S. 17:2351, 2352, 2354, 2354.1, 2354.2, 2354.7, 2354.8, and 5 2357(A) and (F) and to enact R.S. 17:2351.1 and 2354.10 through 2354.14, relative 6 to anatomical gifts; to provide requirements and methods for executing an 7 anatomical gift; to provide for definitions; to provide for authority to execute or 8 revoke an anatomical gift; to provide for an effective date; and to provide for related 9 matters. 10 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 11 Section 1. R.S. 17:2351, 2352, 2354, 2354.1, 2354.2, 2354.7, 2354.8, and 2357(A) 12 and (F) are hereby amended and reenacted and R.S. 17:2351.1 and 2354.10 through 2354.14 13 are hereby enacted to read as follows: 14 §2351. Definitions 15 As used in this Part, the following terms have the following meanings: 16 (1) "Adult" means a person who has attained eighteen years of age. 17 (2) "Agent" means a person who is any of the following: 18 (a) Authorized to make health care decisions on behalf of the principal by a 19 power of attorney for health care; or . 20 (b) Expressly authorized to make an anatomical gift on behalf of the principal 21 by any other record signed by the principal. 22 (3) "Anatomical gift" or "gift" means a donation of all or part of a human 23 body to take effect after the death of the donor for the purpose of transplantation, 24 therapy, research, or education. 25 (4) "Authorization" means a legally effective expression of intent 26 governing acts to be undertaken after the death of the individual, including the 27 recovery of organs or tissue pursuant to an anatomical gift. ACT No. 511 SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 (5) "Consent" means a legally effective permission governing acts to be 2 undertaken prior to the death of the individual, including premortem 3 interventions, tests, procedures, or medications. 4 (4)(6) "Decedent" means a deceased person whose body or part is or may be 5 the source of an anatomical gift. The term does not include a stillborn infant and, 6 subject to restrictions imposed by law, a fetus. 7 (5)(7) "Disinterested witness" means a witness other than the spouse, child, 8 parent, sibling, grandchild, grandparent, or guardian of the person who makes, 9 amends, revokes, or refuses to make an anatomical gift, or an adult who exhibited 10 special care and concern for the person. The term does not include a recipient of an 11 anatomical gift in accordance with the provisions of R.S. 17:2353. 12 (6)(8) "Document of gift" means a donor card or other record used to make 13 an anatomical gift. The term includes a statement or symbol on a driver's license, 14 identification card, fishing license, hunting license, or donor registry. 15 (9) "Donation after brain death" or "DBD" means the recovery of 16 organs or tissue for transplantation following the determination of death based 17 on irreversible total cessation of brain function in accordance with R.S. 9:111. 18 (10) "Donation after circulatory death" or "DCD" means the recovery 19 of organs or tissue for transplantation following the determination of death 20 based on irreversible cessation of spontaneous circulatory and respiratory 21 functions in accordance with R.S. 9:111. 22 (7)(11) "Donor" means a person whose body or part is the subject of an 23 anatomical gift. 24 (8) "Donor registry" means a database that contains records of anatomical 25 gifts and amendments to or revocations thereof. 26 (9)(12) "Driver's license" means a license or permit issued by the Louisiana 27 Department of Public Safety and Corrections, office of motor vehicles, to operate a 28 vehicle, whether or not conditions are attached to the license or permit. 29 (10)(13) "Eye bank" means a person that is licensed, accredited, or regulated 30 under federal or state law to engage in the recovery, screening, testing, processing, SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 storage, or distribution of human eyes or portions of human eyes. 2 (11)(14) "Fishing license" means a license or permit issued by the Louisiana 3 Department of Wildlife and Fisheries to fish as defined in R.S. 56:8, for a 4 recreational purpose as defined in R.S. 56:8. 5 (12)(15) "Guardian" means a person appointed by a court to make decisions 6 regarding the support, care, education, health, or welfare of a person. The term does 7 not include a guardian ad litem. 8 (13)(16) "Hospital" means a facility licensed as a hospital under the laws of 9 any state or a facility operated as a hospital by the United States, a state, or a 10 subdivision of a state. 11 (14)(17) "Hunting license" means a license or permit issued by the Louisiana 12 Department of Wildlife and Fisheries to hunt as defined in R.S. 56:8 for a 13 recreational purpose as defined in R.S. 56:8. 14 (15)(18) "Identification card" means a card issued by the Louisiana 15 Department of Public Safety and Corrections, office of motor vehicles. 16 (16)(19) "Know" means to have actual knowledge. 17 (20) "Legally effective" means having binding force under this Part and 18 not subject to override except as expressly provided by law. 19 (17)(21) "Minor" means a person who has not yet attained eighteen years of 20 age. 21 (18)(22) "Organ procurement organization" means a person designated by the 22 secretary of the United States Department of Health and Human Services as an eye 23 bank, organ procurement organization, or tissue bank. 24 (19)(23) "Parent" means a person whose parental rights have not been 25 terminated. 26 (20)(24) "Part" means an organ, an eye, or tissue of a human being. The term 27 does not include the whole body. 28 (21)(25) "Person" means an individual, corporation, business trust, estate, 29 trust, partnership, limited liability company, association, joint venture, public 30 corporation, government or governmental subdivision, agency, or instrumentality, SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 or any other legal or commercial entity. 2 (22)(26) "Physician" means a person authorized and licensed to practice 3 medicine or osteopathy under the laws of any state. 4 (23)(27) "Prospective donor" means a person who is dead or near death and 5 has been determined by a procurement organization to have a part that may be 6 medically suitable for transplantation, therapy, research, or education. The term does 7 not include a person who has made a refusal. 8 (24)(28) "Reasonably available" means able to be contacted by a an organ 9 procurement organization without undue effort and willing and able to act in a timely 10 manner consistent with existing medical criteria necessary for the making of an 11 anatomical gift. 12 (25)(29) "Recipient" means a person into whose body a part of a decedent has 13 been or is intended to be transplanted. 14 (26)(30) "Record" means information that is inscribed on a tangible medium 15 or that is stored in an electronic or other medium and is retrievable in perceivable 16 form. 17 (27)(31) "Refusal" means a record created pursuant to the provisions of R.S. 18 17:2354.1 legally effective expression of intent not to make an anatomical gift 19 under this Part, that expressly states an intent to bar or has the legal effect of 20 barring other persons from making an anatomical gift of a body or part of a person. 21 (32) "Registry" means a record or database maintained or recognized 22 by the state that contains records of legally effective anatomical gift decisions, 23 including authorizations, refusals, amendments, revocations, affirmations of 24 prior decisions, and indications that no anatomical gift decision should be 25 recorded. 26 (28)(33) "Sign" means, with the present intent to authenticate or adopt a 27 record, either of the following: 28 (a) To execute or adopt a tangible symbol. 29 (b) To attach to or logically associate with the record an electronic symbol, 30 sound, or process. SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 (29)(34) "State" means a state of the United States, the District of Columbia, 2 Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, or any territory or insular possession 3 subject to the jurisdiction of the United States. 4 (30)(35) "Technician" means any individual determined to be qualified to 5 remove or process parts by an appropriate organization that is licensed, accredited, 6 or regulated under federal or state law. The term includes an enucleator. 7 (31)(36) "Tissue" means a portion of the human body other than an organ or 8 an eye. The term does not include blood unless the blood is donated for the purpose 9 of research or education. 10 (32)(37) "Tissue bank" means a person that is licensed, accredited, or 11 regulated under federal or state law to engage in the recovery, screening, testing, 12 processing, storage, or distribution of tissue. 13 (33)(38) "Transplant hospital" means a hospital that furnishes organ 14 transplants and other medical and surgical specialty services required for the care of 15 transplant patients. 16 §2351.1. Rule of construction 17 A. Nothing in this Part shall be construed to permit the recovery of an 18 unpaired vital organ in a manner that causes or hastens the death of the donor, 19 or prior to death being determined in accordance with R.S. 9:111. 20 B. Nothing in this Part shall be construed to prohibit living organ or 21 tissue donation that does not result in the death of the donor and is otherwise 22 authorized by law. 23 C. Unless expressly provided by law, authorization to make an 24 anatomical gift after death shall not be construed as consent to any act 25 undertaken prior to death. 26 §2352. Applicability; persons who may make an anatomical gift or refusal prior to 27 the death of the donor 28 A. The provisions of this Part shall apply to an anatomical gift or amendment, 29 revocation, or refusal to make an anatomical gift. 30 B. Subject to the provisions of R.S. 17:2354.2, an anatomical gift or refusal SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 of a body or part of the donor may be made during his life for the purpose of 2 transplantation, therapy, research, or education as provided in R.S. 17:2354 by any 3 of the following: 4 (1) An adult donor. 5 (2) A minor donor, if he who has been emancipated or authorized under state 6 law to apply for a driver's license who is emancipated pursuant to law or who is 7 legally married. 8 (3) An agent of the donor, as defined in R.S. 17:2351, unless the power of 9 attorney for health care or other record prohibits it. 10 (4) A parent of the donor, if he is an unemancipated minor. 11 (5) The guardian of the donor. 12 * * * 13 §2354. Manner of executing anatomical gift or refusal prior to death of donor 14 A. A donor may make an anatomical gift An anatomical gift may be made 15 by persons as provided by and in accordance with R.S. 17:2352 by any of the 16 following methods: 17 (1) Authorizing By a document of gift signed by the donor, including 18 authorization of a statement or symbol indicating that the donor has made an 19 anatomical gift to be imprinted on his on a driver's license, identification card, 20 fishing license, or hunting license, or registry, as provided by law. 21 (2) Executing a will. 22 (3) Any form of communication addressed to at least two adults, at least one 23 of whom is a disinterested witness, during a terminal illness or injury. By a 24 document of gift signed by another individual acting at the direction of the 25 donor if the donor is physically unable to sign, provided the document is 26 witnessed by two adults, at least one of whom is a disinterested witness, and 27 states that it was signed and witnessed as provided in this Paragraph. 28 (4) Any form of communication made during a terminal illness or injury 29 and addressed to a physician or made in the presence of at least one 30 disinterested adult witness, provided that either of the following occurs: SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 (a) If communicated to a physician, the communication is promptly 2 documented in the medical record, including the date, time, and identity of the 3 person receiving the communication. 4 (b) If made in the presence of a disinterested adult witness, the witness 5 attests to the communication in writing. 6 B. A donor or other person authorized to make an anatomical gift pursuant 7 to R.S. 17:2352 may make a gift by a donor card or other record signed by the person 8 making the gift or by authorizing that a statement or symbol indicating that a gift has 9 been made be included on a donor registry . If the donor or other person is physically 10 unable to sign a record, the record may be signed by another person at the direction 11 of the person making the gift and the record shall: 12 (1) Be witnessed by two adults, one of whom is a disinterested witness, who 13 have signed at the request of the person making the gift; and 14 (2) State that it has been signed and witnessed as provided in Paragraph (1) 15 of this Subsection. 16 A refusal to make an anatomical gift may be executed in the same 17 manner and with the same legal effect as an anatomical gift under Subsection 18 A of this Section. 19 C. A gift or refusal shall not be revoked, 22 amended, or overridden except as expressly provided by law. A person who has made a refusal to make an anatomical gift, may amend 19 or revoke the refusal in any of the following ways: 20 (1) In the manner provided in Subsection A of this Section for making a 21 refusal. 22 (2) By subsequently making an anatomical gift in accordance with R.S. 23 17:2354 that is inconsistent with the refusal. 24 (3) By destroying or canceling the record or a portion thereof evidencing the 25 refusal, with the intent of revocation. 26 C. Except as otherwise provided in R.S. 17:2354.2, in the absence of an 30 express contrary indication set forth in the refusal, an unrevoked refusal bars all SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 other persons from making an anatomical gift of the body or part of the person 2 individual. 3 E. A refusal may include limitations or special instructions, which shall 6 be honored to the extent permitted by law. 7 G. A revocation of a refusal is a neutral act that withdraws the prior 8 refusal, does not constitute an anatomical gift, and results in no anatomical gift 9 decision being in effect unless and until a new authorization or refusal is made 10 in accordance with this Part. 11 §2354.2. Except as otherwise provided in Subsection G of this Section and subject 13 Subject to Subsection F of this Section, in the absence of an express, contrary 14 indication by the donor, a person other than the donor is barred from making, 15 amending, or revoking an anatomical gift of the body or part of the donor if the gift 16 complies with R.S. 17:2354 or 2356 was executed in accordance with R.S. 17:2354 17 and has not been amended or revoked in accordance with R.S. 17:2356. 18 B. If a document of gift an anatomical gift decision is valid in accordance 24 with this Part, the law of this state governs the interpretation of the document of gift 25 and effect of the anatomical gift decision. 26 C. A person may presume that a document of gift or amendment thereto an 27 anatomical gift decision or amendment thereto is valid unless that person knows 28 that it is not validly executed made or it was revoked. 29 D. If multiple anatomical gift decisions exist, including decisions made 30 in another state or country, the most recent legally effective authorization or SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 refusal shall control. 2 E. If the laws of another state or country conflict with the provisions of 3 this Part, the provisions of this Part shall govern the determination of the effect 4 of the anatomical gift decision. 5 F. An anatomical gift decision made by a minor, except as provided by 6 R.S. 17:2352(B)(2) or R.S. 17:2354(A), shall not constitute a legally effective 7 authorization or refusal in this state. 8 §2354.8. Donor registry Registry 9 A. The secretary of the Louisiana Department of Health may establish or 10 contract for the establishment of a donor registry. 11 B. The Louisiana Department of Public Safety and Corrections, office of 12 motor vehicles, and the Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries shall 13 cooperate with any person administering any donor registry that this state 14 establishes, contracts for, or recognizes for the purpose of transferring to the donor 15 registry all relevant information regarding the making, amendment to, or revocation 16 of an anatomical gift an individual's anatomical gift decision, including 17 authorization, refusal, amendment, revocation of a prior authorization or 18 refusal, affirmation of a previously recorded decision, indication that no 19 anatomical gift decision should be recorded, or informational indication in the 20 case of a minor. 21 C. A donor registry shall be accessible seven days a week, twenty-four hours 22 a day and allow each of the following: 23 (1) A donor An individual or other person authorized in accordance with 24 R.S. 17:2352 to include on the donor registry a statement or symbol that the donor 25 individual has made, amended, or revoked an anatomical gift or amended an 26 anatomical gift decision, revoked a prior authorization or refusal, affirmed a 27 previously recorded authorization or refusal, or has indicated that no 28 anatomical gift decision should be recorded. 29 (2) A procurement organization to obtain relevant information from the 30 registry to determine, at or near death of the donor or a prospective donor, whether SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 the donor or prospective donor has made, amended, or revoked an anatomical gift 2 individual, whether the individual has made or amended an anatomical gift 3 decision, revoked a prior authorization or refusal, affirmed a previously 4 recorded authorization or refusal, or whether no anatomical gift decision is 5 recorded. 6 D. Personally identifiable information on a donor registry may not be used 7 or disclosed without the express consent of the donor individual, prospective donor, 8 or person that made the anatomical gift, for any purpose except to determine, at or 9 near the death of the donor individual, whether he the individual has made, 10 amended, or revoked an anatomical gift or amended an anatomical gift decision, 11 revoked a prior authorization or refusal, affirmed a previously recorded 12 authorization or refusal, or whether no anatomical gift decision is recorded. 13 E. This Section does not prohibit any person from creating or maintaining a 14 donor registry that is not established by or contracted with the state. Any such 15 registry shall comply with Subsections C and D of this Section and shall not 16 supersede the registry recognized by the state under this Section unless the 17 record is in accordance with R.S. 17:2354.7. A revocation recorded in the 18 registry withdraws a prior authorization or refusal and results in no anatomical 19 gift decision being recorded unless and until a new authorization or refusal is 20 made. 21 * * * 22 §2354.10. Anatomical gifts by minors 23 A. No state agency, political subdivision, department, office, or 24 contractor thereof, including the office responsible for the issuance of driver's 25 licenses or identification cards, shall solicit, query, or request from a minor any 26 authorization for or refusal to make an anatomical gift in the absence of a 27 parent or legal guardian. 28 B. No representation by a minor concerning anatomical gift 29 authorization or refusal shall be recorded, relied upon, or given legal effect 30 unless made in the presence of a parent or legal guardian who affirmatively and SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 expressly concurs in writing in the decision of the minor. 2 C. Any representation made by a minor concerning an anatomical gift 3 authorization or refusal, when made in the presence of and with the affirmative 4 and express written concurrence of a parent or legal guardian, may be recorded 5 solely for informational purposes and shall not be legally operative prior to the 6 minor attaining the age of majority. 7 D. Upon attainment of the age of majority, any informational record of 8 anatomical gift authorization or refusal made by the individual as a minor in 9 accordance with this Section shall become legally operative unless the individual 10 affirmatively amends or revokes the record in accordance with law. 11 E. Notwithstanding any provision of this Part, this Section shall not 12 apply to a minor who is emancipated pursuant to law or who is legally married, 13 and the individual shall be treated as an adult for purposes of this Part. 14 F. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to limit the authority of a 15 parent or legal guardian to make or refuse an anatomical gift on behalf of a 16 minor as otherwise provided by law. 17 §2354.11. Premortem interventions; definitions; prohibitions 18 A. For purposes of this Section, "premortem intervention" means any 19 medical procedure, test, medication, cannulation, or other intervention 20 undertaken prior to the determination of death for the purpose of facilitating 21 organ or tissue recovery. 22 B. No premortem intervention shall be performed unless it is medically 23 indicated for the direct benefit of the patient or is performed pursuant to a 24 legally effective consent applicable to premortem interventions, as provided in 25 this Section. 26 C. Regardless of consent, no person shall perform or direct any 27 premortem intervention that has a material likelihood of any of the following: 28 (1) Hastening death. 29 (2) Manufacturing, accelerating, or securing irreversibility of 30 circulatory, respiratory, or neurologic cessation. SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 (3) Preventing or materially interfering with the restoration or 2 resumption of circulatory, respiratory, or neurologic function. 3 D. Any physician or individual who performs or directs a premortem 4 intervention shall be independent of, and shall not participate in, the recovery 5 or transplantation of any organ or tissue from the individual, and shall not be 6 a member of the transplant team. 7 E. Authorization to make an anatomical gift after death does not 8 constitute consent to any premortem intervention, test, procedure, or 9 medication. Consent for any premortem intervention shall be separately and 10 expressly obtained and shall be legally effective only if the following occur: 11 (1) The consent is given by the patient, or by another person legally 12 authorized under Louisiana law to consent to medical treatment on behalf of the 13 patient. 14 (2) The consent is voluntary and informed. 15 (3) The nature and purpose of the premortem intervention are 16 specifically described. 17 (4) The patient or other person authorized to consent is informed that 18 the intervention is performed for the purpose of facilitating organ or tissue 19 recovery and is not medically indicated for the care or treatment of the patient. 20 (5) The consent does not authorize any premortem intervention that 21 poses a material risk of causing or hastening the death of the patient, or a 22 material risk of interfering with the determination of death in accordance with 23 R.S. 9:111. 24 F. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to alter the standards for 25 determination of death under R.S. 9:111 or to authorize any practice 26 inconsistent with that Section. 27 G. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to prohibit the provision of 28 comfort care, palliative treatment, or the withdrawal or withholding of 29 life-sustaining treatment in accordance with applicable law and accepted 30 medical standards. SB NO. 427 ENROLLED 1 §2354.12. Post-determination handling and transport of the decedent 2 A. This Section applies following the determination and pronouncement 3 of death under R.S. 9:111 and the recovery of any organ or tissue pursuant to 4 an anatomical gift. 5 B. Prior to the recovery of organs or tissue, the individual authorized to 6 make an anatomical gift, or the individual's authorized representative where 7 applicable, shall be informed in writing of any plan to do any of the following: 8 (1) Transport the decedent from the hospital or facility where death was 9 determined to another facility for further organ or tissue recovery, processing, 10 or handling. 11 (2) Continue mechanical ventilation, perfusion, or other artificial 12 support of bodily functions following the determination of death for purposes 13 related to organ or tissue recovery. 14 C. No transport or continuation of mechanical support described in 15 Subsection B of this Section shall occur unless expressly authorized as part of 16 a legally effective authorization applicable to post-determination handling and 17 transport. 18 D. Authorization to make an anatomical gift after death shall not, by 19 itself, constitute authorization for transport of the decedent to a nonhospital 20 facility or for continuation of mechanical support following death 21 determination. 22 E. Any handling or transport of the decedent following death 23 determination and organ or tissue recovery shall be conducted in a manner 24 consistent with the dignity of the decedent and with the reasonable expectations 25 of the family, as informed by the disclosures made under this Section. 26 F. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to limit the recovery of 27 organs or tissue otherwise authorized by law, to alter the standards for 28 determination of death under R.S. 9:111, or, except to the extent necessary to 29 carry out a valid anatomical gift, to alter or transfer the custodial authority of 30 the person or persons authorized by law to control disposition of the body. An authorization recorded in the registry constitutes a legally 3 effective expression of intent and supersedes any prior refusal. 4 B. A refusal recorded in the registry constitutes a legally effective 5 expression of intent and supersedes any prior authorization. 6 C. The most recent authorization or refusal recorded in the registry shall 7 control. 8 D. The absence of a recorded authorization or refusal indicates that no 9 anatomical gift decision has been recorded in the registry and shall not 10 constitute authorization or refusal. 11 E. An informational indication made by a minor shall not constitute a 15 legally effective authorization or refusal. 16 §2354.14. Out-of-state anatomical gift decisions; effect 17 A. An anatomical gift decision, including an authorization or refusal, 18 made in another state that is valid under the laws of that state or of this state 19 shall be recognized as a legally effective anatomical gift decision in this state. 20 B. If multiple anatomical gift decisions exist, including decisions made 23 in another state, the most recent legally effective authorization or refusal shall 24 control. 25 D. When a hospital refers a person at or near death to a procurement 4 organization, the procurement organization shall make a reasonable search of the 5 records of the office of motor vehicles and any known donor the registry recognized 6 under R.S. 17:2354.8 to ascertain whether the person has made an anatomical gift 7 or amended an anatomical gift decision, revoked a prior authorization or 8 refusal, affirmed a previously recorded authorization or refusal, or whether no 9 anatomical gift decision is recorded. 10 * * * 11 F. Content: The original instrument and the following digest, which constitutes no part of the legislative instrument, were prepared by Senate Legislative Services. The keyword, summary, and digest do not constitute part of the law or proof or indicia of legislative intent. [R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 428 Original 2026 Regular Session Hensgens Present law provides for rights of use, which are regulated by the rules of usufruct and predial servitudes. Further provides that a predial servitude is a charge on a servient estate for the benefit of a dominant estate. Present law provides that a landowner may establish a pipeline servitude by contract. Present law provides that a contract has the effect of law for the parties thereto and that the obligations enumerated therein must be performed in good faith. Proposed law retains present law and provides that laws governing usufruct and predial servitudes cannot create additional obligations not stated in the contract. Proposed law provides that proposed law is prospective and shall apply only to claims and causes of action arising on or after the effective date. Effective August 1, 2026. (Adds R.S. 30:554.1)

[R.S. 1:13(B) and 24:177(E)] DIGEST SB 434 Original 2026 Regular Session Fesi Present law provides for definitions in the State Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Law. Title: CHILDREN:  Establishes the Commission on Fatherhood Engagement. (8/1/26)
Summary: CHILDREN:  Establishes the Commission on Fatherhood Engagement. (8/1/26)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 435 BY SENATORS MYERS, BARROW, BOUDREAUX, CATHEY, CONNICK, DUPLESSIS, HENRY, MIZELL, MORRIS, PRICE, SELDERS AND TALBOT AND REPRESENTATIVES BEAULLIEU, BILLINGS, BOYD, CHASSION, FARNUM, KNOX, LARVADAIN, LYONS, MARCELLE, THOMAS, WALTERS AND WYBLE 1 AN ACT 2 To enact Subpart C of Part II of Chapter 3 of Title 46 of the Louisiana Revised Statutes of 3 1950, to be comprised of R.S. 46:261, relative to the Louisiana Commission on 4 Fatherhood Engagement; to establish the commission within the office of the 5 governor; to provide for the membership, powers, and duties of the commission; to 6 provide for interagency cooperation; to provide for a termination date; and to provide 7 for related matters. 8 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 9 Section 1. Subpart C of Part II of Chapter 3 of Title 46 of the Louisiana Revised 10 Statutes of 1950, comprised of R.S. 46:261, is hereby enacted to read as follows: 11 SUBPART C. FATHERHOOD ENGAGEMENT 12 §261. Louisiana Commission on Fatherhood Engagement 13 A. There is hereby established within the office of the governor, the 14 Louisiana Commission on Fatherhood Engagement, hereafter referred to as the 15 "commission". ACT No. 512 SB NO. 435 ENROLLED 1 B. The commission shall be comprised of the following members: 2 (1) The director of early childhood systems in the office of the governor. 3 (2) The executive director of the Louisiana Children's Cabinet, or his 4 designee. 5 (3) The secretary of the Louisiana Department of Children and Family 6 Services, or his designee. 7 (4) The superintendent of the Louisiana Department of Education, or his 8 designee. 9 (5) The secretary of the Louisiana Department of Public Safety and 10 Corrections, or his designee. 11 (6) The director of the bureau of family health within the Louisiana 12 Department of Health, or his designee. 13 (7) The secretary of Louisiana Works, or his designee. 14 (8) A member of the Senate appointed by the president of the Senate. 15 (9) A member of the House of Representatives appointed by the speaker of 16 the House of Representatives. 17 (10) A representative from the Louisiana Supreme Court. 18 (11) The director of the Louisiana Head Start Collaboration. 19 (12) The chief executive officer of the Louisiana Policy Institute for Children, 20 or his designee. 21 (13) A family court judge appointed by the governor. 22 (14) A representative of a faith community that promotes fatherhood 23 engagement appointed by the governor. 24 (15) A representative from a parenting education organization that works in 25 multiple parishes appointed by the governor. 26 (16) One representative each from an organization that provides fatherhood 27 programs in each of the nine public health regions of the state. If there is no 28 organization that provides fatherhood programs in any region, this position shall be 29 filled by a father who is a community leader or an additional faith-based 30 representative appointed by the governor. SB NO. 435 ENROLLED 1 C. A majority of the members of the commission shall constitute a quorum 2 for the transaction of business, and all official action of the commission shall require 3 the favorable vote of a majority of those members present and voting. 4 D. Members appointed to the commission shall serve two-year terms. The 5 directors, secretaries, and superintendents, or their designees shall serve on the 6 commission until they cease to be a director, secretary, or superintendent. Each 7 member shall serve on the commission from the date of appointment until the end 8 of the term for which the member was appointed. Members may be reappointed. 9 E. Vacancies shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointments. 10 Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring prior to the expiration date of the 11 term for which the member's predecessor was appointed shall serve on the 12 commission for the remainder of that term. A member shall continue to serve on the 13 commission subsequent to the expiration date of the member's term until the 14 member's successor is appointed. 15 F. Members shall serve without compensation but shall be eligible for 16 reimbursement for necessary expenses. 17 G.(1) The director of early childhood systems in the office of the 18 governor shall call the first meeting of the commission. 19 (2) The commission shall elect a chairperson from among its members 20 in every even-numbered year. 21 H. The duties of the commission shall include but not be limited to all of 22 the following: 23 (1) Implementing the recommendations made by the Task Force on 24 Fatherhood Engagement and all ensuing recommendations developed and 25 approved by the commission pursuant to Senate Concurrent Resolution No. 9 26 of the 2025 Regular Session of the Legislature. 27 (2) Raising awareness of the importance of fathers' engagement in the 28 lives of their children, resources and support available to Louisiana fathers, and 29 the challenges and barriers facing Louisiana fathers. 30 (3) Identifying resources available to fund fatherhood-related programs SB NO. 435 ENROLLED 1 and assist in the creation or expansion of initiatives that do the following: 2 (a) Promote responsible fatherhood. 3 (b) Build the parenting and co-parenting skills of fathers. 4 (c) Provide employment-related services for low-income fathers. 5 (d) Provide services to fathers who are incarcerated or have been 6 recently released from incarceration to allow them to maintain or reestablish 7 their relationships with their families. 8 (e) Reconcile fathers with their families. 9 (f) Provide support for effective fatherhood programming across state 10 agencies. 11 (g) Improve data collection and usage on fathers and fatherhood 12 engagement across agencies. 13 (h) Improve support for fathers during the perinatal period. 14 (i) Assist fathers with minor children in addressing barriers to accessing 15 and maintaining stable housing. 16 (j) Improve policies and practices to ensure fair and equal opportunities 17 for fathers in child custody, visitation, and family court proceedings. 18 (k) Increase public awareness of the critical role fathers play. 19 (4) Organizing a state summit on fatherhood at least once every four 20 years. 21 (5) Reporting annually on the commission's expectations for the 22 outcomes of fatherhood-related programs and initiatives and the methods the 23 commission uses for conducting annual measures of those outcomes. 24 (6) Submitting a comprehensive report to the legislature annually with 25 a funding request for fatherhood programs that are overseen by the commission 26 and allocated to community organizations to support programs and services 27 aligned with the commission's intent and purpose. 28 I. The provisions of this Section shall terminate on January 1, 2030. 29 Section 2. Subpart C of Part II of Chapter 3 of Title 46 of the Louisiana Revised 30 Statutes of 1950, comprised of R.S. 46:261, is hereby repealed in its entirety. SB NO. 435 ENROLLED 1 Section 3.(A) This Section and Section 1 of this Act shall become effective August 1, 2 2026. 3 (B) Section 2 of this Act shall become effective January 1, 2030. Title: MULTIMODAL COMMERCE:  Provides relative to annual aviation fuel estimates. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SD EX)
Summary: MULTIMODAL COMMERCE:  Provides relative to annual aviation fuel estimates. (8/1/26) (EN SEE FISC NOTE SD EX)
Content: 2026 Regular Session ENROLLED SENATE BILL NO. 436 BY SENATOR CLOUD 1 2 AN ACT 3 To amend and reenact R.S. 47:306.6, relative to aviation fuel taxes; to provide for annual 4 estimates of aviation fuel taxes; to provide for the use of designated ramp space by 5 certain aircraft; to provide for rules and regulations relative to aviation fuel tax; to 6 provide for reporting of aviation fuel taxes; and to provide for related matters. 7 Be it enacted by the Legislature of Louisiana: 8 Section 1. R.S. 47:306.6 is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 9 §306.6. Annual aviation fuel estimates; calculations; agreements with the 10 Department of Transportation and Development; reporting 11 requirements; designated ramp space for public use 12 A.(1) Annual estimates. The secretary of the Department of Revenue shall 13 annually estimate the revenue to be derived from state taxes collected from 14 purchases of aviation fuel. Monies collected from the avails of the taxes levied on 15 aviation fuel shall be appropriated to the Department of Transportation and 16 Development to be used solely for airport-related purposes; however, no portion of 17 such monies shall be disbursed to any airport that does not clearly indicate on its 18 property the designated ramp space for public use within its published airport ACT No. 513 SB NO. 436 ENROLLED 1 directory or diagram. The designated ramp space for public use at an airport 2 shall be made available for transient aircraft without charge for no more than 3 two hours. Nothing in this Subsection shall be construed to require a fixed base 4 operator, airport operator, or tenant to provide fueling, ground handling, 5 towing, parking assistance, concierge services, or any other services to aircraft 6 utilizing the designated ramp space for public use. 7 (2)B. The calculation of "annual estimated revenue" shall be determined by 8 using all of the following: 9 (a)(1) Average price per gallon. The average price per gallon of aviation fuel 10 shall be determined through a two-year agreement between the secretary of the 11 Department of Revenue and the secretary of the Department of Transportation and 12 Development rules and procedures promulgated by the Department of 13 Transportation and Development, office of multimodal commerce. The average 14 price per gallon shall be based on historical data, current market fuel prices, and 15 trends provided by relevant reports, including reputable sources such as government 16 agencies, industry associations, or market analysis firms. 17 (b)(2) Gallons sold data source. The volume of aviation fuel sold in Louisiana 18 shall be determined based on data provided by the Energy Information 19 Administration or the Bureau of Transportation Statistics. In the event data from 20 either of these two organizations is unavailable, the secretary of the Department of 21 Revenue may utilize other reputable data sources, subject to approval by the Joint 22 Legislative Committee on the Budget through rules and procedures promulgated 23 by the Department of Transportation and Development, office of multimodal 24 commerce. When determining the volume of aviation fuel sold in Louisiana, the 25 office of multimodal commerce may utilize data published by federal 26 government agencies, including but not limited to the United States Energy 27 Information Administration and the Bureau of Transportation Statistics, as well 28 as other verifiable and publicly available data sources. 29 (3) State taxes collected from aviation fuel sales. The Department of 30 Revenue shall provide to the Department of Transportation and Development SB NO. 436 ENROLLED 1 an annual report of actual state tax collections derived from sales of aviation 2 fuel. The Department of Transportation and Development, office of multimodal 3 commerce, shall consider the collection amounts reported in accordance with 4 this Paragraph when developing rules and procedures for calculating estimated 5 aviation fuel tax revenue. 6 (c)(4) Sales tax rate. The sales tax rate applied in calculating the annual 7 estimated revenue shall be based on the current state sales tax rate in effect at the 8 time of the estimate. If the state sales tax rate changes during the fiscal year, the 9 annual estimated revenue shall be recalculated using the new sales tax rate. 10 (3) The secretary of the Department of Revenue shall submit the annual 11 estimated revenue to be derived from state taxes collected from purchases of aviation 12 fuel to the Revenue Estimating Conference within five calendar days of the annual 13 estimate being completed by the secretary. 14 C. The Revenue Estimating Conference shall utilize this estimation 15 formula and the rules and policies promulgated by the Department of 16 Transportation and Development, office of multimodal commerce, to develop 17 its annual estimate of aviation fuel taxes collected. 18 B. Reporting requirements. The secretary of the Department of Revenue shall 19 submit an annual report to the Joint Legislative Committee on the Budget no later 20 than March thirty-first of each year which contains the following information: 21 (1) The average price per gallon used in the calculation. 22 (2) The total gallons of aviation fuel sold in the state used in the calculation, 23 as reported by the Energy Information Administration or the Bureau of 24 Transportation Statistics. 25 (3) The sales tax rate applied in the calculation. 26 C. Legislative oversight. All agreements between the secretary of the 27 Department of Revenue and the secretary of the Department of Transportation and 28 Development required pursuant to the provisions of this Section related to the 29 calculation of the annual estimated revenue derived from sales of aviation fuel shall 30 be reviewed and approved by the Joint Legislative Committee on the Budget prior SB NO. 436 ENROLLED 1 to the agreement becoming binding between the parties. 2 D. The provisions of this Section shall be terminated and have no effect on 3 January 1, 2027. PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE SPEAKER OF THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF LOUISIANA APPROVED:

R.S. 33:2581.4 is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows: 11 §2581.4. Cancer screenings; certain firefighters and fire service employees 12 A. A classified firefighter employed as a full-time paid employee of a 13 municipal or parish fire department or fire protection district and fire service 14 employees employed by a nonprofit corporation under contract with a fire protection 15 district shall be provided a cancer screening examination and precancer screening 16 conducted by a physician or a qualified healthcare provider. The initial cancer 17 screening examination shall be conducted not less than three years after the start of 18 employment, and subsequent examinations shall be conducted during the course of 19 employment in accordance with the following schedule: 20 (1) Every five years for firefighters and fire service employees who are not 21 less than twenty years of age and not more than thirty-nine years of age. 22 (2) Every four years for firefighters and fire service employees who are not 23 less than forty years of age and not more than forty-nine years of age. 24 (3) Every three years for firefighters and fire service employees who are fifty 25 years of age or older. 26 (4) Every ten years for every firefighter and fire service employee 27 beginning with the first ten years of employment and in ten-year intervals ACT No. 389 SB NO. 439 ENROLLED 1 thereafter for precancer screening or testing. 2 B. The examination shall include but not be limited to screening for the 3 following cancers: 4 (1) Colon. 5 (2) Lung. 6 (3) Bladder. 7 (4) Oral. 8 (5) Thyroid. 9 (6) Skin. 10 (7) Blood. 11 (8) Breast. 12 (9) Cervical. 13 (10) Testicular. 14 (11) Prostate. 15 C. Effective January 1, 2027, precancer screenings for esophageal cancer 16 shall be made available to every firefighter and fire service employee in 17 accordance with the schedule provided in Subsection A of this Section, but 18 terminable in all cases at the age of sixty. 19 D. Any firefighter or fire service employee who retires from active service, 20 including a firefighter or fire service employee who retires due to a disability, shall 21 be provided cancer and precancer screening examinations by his former employer 22 pursuant to the schedule provided in Subsection A of this Section. 23 D.E. All costs associated with cancer screening and precancer screening 24 examinations provided for in this Section shall be paid by the firefighter's or fire 25 service employee's employer or former employer. No copayment, deductible, 26 coinsurance, or any out-of-pocket expense shall be required for such examinations. 27 E.F. No firefighter or fire service employee shall be required to participate 28 in cancer or precancer screening examinations. 29 F.G. The provisions of the Section shall not be applicable to any firefighter 30 or fire service employee who resigned or was terminated by his employer. SB NO. 439 ENROLLED 1 G.H. Nothing in this Section shall be construed to require a personal health 2 insurance company to pay for cancer and precancer screening examinations for 3 firefighters or fire service employees that are conducted more frequently than such 4 examinations are recommended in the most recently published recommendations 5 established by the American Cancer Society or the National Comprehensive Cancer 6 Network.
